source.subject: Newsletter surge — emergency partner promo credit + trial extension request
messages:
From: Elena Park <elena@brightgrid.co>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Urgent: Newsletter surge — need extra promo codes and 30d trial extension

Hi Hannah,

Quick one — we just deployed a partner spotlight in our weekday newsletter and the open rate is spiking (live CTR ~12%). We underestimated demand and our allocation of single-use promo codes for the BrightGrid community exhausted in <2 hours.

Can Redwood temporarily top up our promo pool by 300 trial credits (one-use trial seats) and extend the expiration window on the 1,200 existing codes by 30 days while we push a follow-up email to stagger redemptions? We need the change in place before the follow-up goes out tomorrow morning PT.

Attached is the subscriber segment CSV and the landing flow we will use. We can sign a quick amendment if needed — low friction on our side. If there are budget implications, flag them and we’ll route approval from procurement.

Appreciate any same-day help,
Elena

-- 
Elena Park
Head of Growth, BrightGrid
elena@brightgrid.co | +1 415-555-0123
BrightGrid — community tools for creators
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Urgent: Newsletter surge — need extra promo codes and 30d trial extension

Team — forwarding BrightGrid's urgent request. TL;DR: partner needs +300 one-use trials and a 30-day extension on current codes; they want it before tomorrow morning.

Ask: can product/ops approve a one-time credit top-up from the partner promo pool and can RevOps apply the 30-day extension at the account-level? If approval needed for dollars, please advise quick numbers so I can loop Elena.

Notes: their CSV looks clean but we should gate codes to authenticated sign-ups to avoid abuse. Marissa, would you own the gating and promo code name? Karthik, please confirm partner contract allows this amendment without exec sign-off. Logan, can you scope billing impact?

Thanks — Hannah

-------- Forwarded Message --------
From: Elena Park <elena@brightgrid.co>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Urgent: Newsletter surge — need extra promo codes and 30d trial extension
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>

(Original message attached above)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 13:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Urgent: Newsletter surge — need extra promo codes and 30d trial extension

Quick response — operationally ok to approve a temporary top-up under marketing's promo budget with the following guardrails:

- Approve: +300 one-use trial codes (partner-specific) and 30-day extension for the current issued pool (1,200 codes).
- Promo code string: BRIGHT24SURGE (partner will need to implement rate limit of 1 per account).
- Gating: require email verification + email domain match to the CSV segment — we should reject bulk signups.
- TTL: top-up valid for 7 days (after which unused codes expire).
- Reporting: partner must share redemption CSV within 7 days so we can reconcile fraud.

Marissa (marketing ops) — please draft the amendment email and add the small DocuSign amendment (I attached a quick template). Logan, can you confirm the dollar-equivalent and issue a one-time invoice or credit memo? If we need legal sign-off, keep it light; the amendment covers both top-up and extension.

I'll loop Elena once we have billing confirmation.

Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Marketing Ops, Redwood Inference
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena@brightgrid.co>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Urgent: Newsletter surge — need extra promo codes and 30d trial extension

Hi all — billing follow-up. We approved the one-time credit equal to 300 single-use trial grants (estimated unit cost / internal metric applied) — internal chargeback: $1,200 (one-time). I created a credit memo and attached an invoice draft for partner records.

Actions taken:
- Applied +300 partner-specific promo codes (namespace: BRIGHTGRID-BRK-20260719) to BrightGrid's partner account; codes expire in 7 days if unused.
- Applied 30-day extension to the outstanding 1,200 issued codes on the account.
- DocuSign amendment created: https://www.docusign.net/Member/StartInSigning.aspx?envelopeId=ABC123456
- HubSpot deal updated: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123/record/4512 (deal BG-2026-Q3-1289)

Elena — please confirm receipt and that the gating described by Marissa is acceptable (email verification + CSV domain match). Once signed we'll mark the invoice as agreed and close this as an approved one-off.

Thanks — Logan

--
Logan Wright
Revenue Operations, Redwood Inference
Attachments: invoice-draft-12345.pdf

source.subject: Post-Summit attendee engagement plan + SDR handoff
messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 18:02:00 -0700
Subject: Summit — attendee segmentation + recommended routing

Team,

Great job yesterday — turnout was strong and the Q&A drove some solid inbound interest. I exported the attendee list and tagged behavior (attended, watched-replay, asked-question, requested-demo). Primary asks below:

- MQL routing: high intent (requested-demo OR asked-question + company size > 50) -> SDRs A queue (Logan-led)
- Nurture: watched-replay + no-demo-request -> Marketing nurture (drip 4w)
- Enterprise: any CISO/Head of Platform -> AE follow-up (Tessa + Diego)

Please pick owners for the 47 high-intent attendees by EOD Friday. I dropped the attendee CSV and the slide deck into the shared Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_example_attendees

Attachments: [attachment: attendee_list_2026-07-15.csv (CSV, 1.8MB)], [attachment: summit-slide-deck.pdf (PDF, 6.4MB)]

Proposed next steps (rough playbook):
1) Email within 48h with replay + 1:1 booking link
2) SDRs call top 10 accounts in the high-intent list within 72h
3) Mark HubSpot contact with "2026-SUMMIT-HighIntent" and record source

Can each of you confirm who you'll own and if you want me to seed the first outreach templates?

Thanks,
Camila
Camila Reyes | Field Marketing Manager, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0149

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jul 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Summit — attendee segmentation + recommended routing

On Thu, 15 Jul 2026 at 18:02, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
>
> Great job yesterday ...

Thanks Camila — I'll own the SDR A queue for the high-intent 47. I'll split them between our team as follows:

- Logan (Top 10 by account ARR / strategic fit)
- Diego (Next 15 — mid-market)
- BDR pool (remaining 22 with follow-up cadences)

I'll ask ops to tag HubSpot contacts with the "2026-SUMMIT-HighIntent" property and add owner assignments. Can you confirm the replay + booking link for the outbound email template?

I'll also push the top 10 into my personal task list and assign calls for Monday morning.

—Logan
Logan Wright | Sales Development Manager, Redwood Inference
logan@redwood.com

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jul 2026 10:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Summit — attendee segmentation + recommended routing

On Fri, 16 Jul 2026 at 09:10, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Camila — I'll own the SDR A queue ...

Added routing rules to the shared doc and exported the HubSpot IDs for the 47 high-intent leads. Key notes:

- 8 are existing contacts with recent product usage; mark as "expand-opportunity"
- 5 are VPs / execs — flag for AE outreach (Tessa)
- 12 are in APAC timezone (we should rotate calls to match local hours)

I uploaded the routing CSV and a short MQL mapping spec: [attachment: mql-routing-rules.md (MD, 3KB)]. HubSpot reference: 87654321.

Template for outbound email (first touch) — use Camila's replay link and Calendly: https://calendly.com/redwood/30min-demo

Ava
Ava Chen | Demand Gen, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com

From: Maria Lopez <maria@insightai.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jul 2026 14:05:00 -0500
Subject: Thanks + quick ask about the replay

Hi Camila,

Thanks again for the session — really helpful framing on cost-aware inference. Can you send the replay link and the slide deck? Also curious if anyone from your team is available for a 30-min intro next week (we're evaluating inference options).

Best,
Maria Lopez
Head of ML Platform | InsightAI
maria@insightai.com
[mobile redacted]

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Maria Lopez <maria@insightai.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Thanks + quick ask about the replay

Maria — thanks for the note and glad it was useful. Replay + deck are here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_example_attendees (slide deck is "summit-slide-deck.pdf").

I've looped in Logan and Tessa from Sales. Logan will reach out to schedule a 30-min intro (we'll use the Calendly link below). For transparency, we flagged InsightAI as high-intent based on the live Q and request for follow-up.

SDR / AE handoff summary (final):
- Owner: Logan Wright (SDR outreach)
- AE primary: Tessa Morgan
- HubSpot tag: 2026-SUMMIT-HighIntent (Deal: DL-4982)
- Playbook snippet: initial email w/ replay + 1-click calendar -> if no reply, two touch calls over 5 business days -> escalate to AE for tailored tech dig if interest confirmed

Attachments included for the handoff: [attachment: sdr-playbook-snippet.md (MD, 2.1KB)], [attachment: attendee_list_2026-07-15.csv (CSV, 1.8MB)]

Logan / Tessa — confirm you have the attendee CSV and I'll send the templated outreach this morning.

Cheers,
Camila
Camila Reyes | Field Marketing Manager, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0149

source.subject: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Team —

Quick summary: DatumAI (enterprise prospect) is pushing to close in Q3. They want dedicated pools in the US and APAC with a single routing policy that prefers US for low-latency users and APAC for APAC traffic. They gave preliminary traffic estimates (attached) and asked for a recommended pool split, expected provisioning lead time, and a short MOU to attach to their procurement.

Asks:
- Marissa: please own the external comms and confirm their expected ramp cadence with them.
- Karthik: can you draft a sizing recommendation (GPU counts by region, burst vs reserved layout) and provide the latency/cost tradeoffs we should show?
- Mina: flag any compliance/region-residency constraints for APAC that would impact Private vs Dedicated.

I attached the traffic spreadsheet DatumAI shared. I want a short internal proposal by EOD Thu so we can send a recommendation + MOU to the customer and unblock procurement.

Thanks,
Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: datumai_traffic_estimates.xlsx (excel, 34KB)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 11:05:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Thanks Logan — looping David so DatumAI sees our timeline. David: quick ask on your side: can you confirm whether the APAC user sessions must remain in-region (Singapore) or if routing to a proximate public region is acceptable? That affects whether we recommend Redwood Private in APAC or a Dedicated pool in a cloud region.

Internal note: I've reviewed the traffic sheet — the initial commit looks like ~2M tokens/day user-facing by month 3 with 3x peak delta during promos. I'm attaching a brief proposal draft (PDF) with two options: Reserved-heavy (lower unit cost, higher commit) and Burst-optimized (smaller reserved pool + burst credits). Karthik, please verify the GPU counts — I made an initial pass in the PDF.

Next steps:
- Karthik: update counts and include expected latency range for 512-token LLM calls (p50/p95).
- Mina: write a 2-line compliance note for the MOU about data residency.
- Logan: can you confirm target SLA we should offer for p95 < 400ms?

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood_region_pool_proposal_v1.pdf (pdf, 220KB)

On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 08:12, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick summary: DatumAI (enterprise prospect) is pushing to close in Q3. They want dedicated pools in the US and APAC with a single routing policy that prefers US for low-latency users and APAC for APAC traffic. They gave preliminary traffic estimates (attached) and asked for a recommended pool split, expected provisioning lead time, and a short MOU to attach to their procurement.
> 
> Asks:
> - Marissa: please own the external comms and confirm their expected ramp cadence with them.
> - Karthik: can you draft a sizing recommendation (GPU counts by region, burst vs reserved layout) and provide the latency/cost tradeoffs we should show?
> - Mina: flag any compliance/region-residency constraints for APAC that would impact Private vs Dedicated.
> 
> I attached the traffic spreadsheet DatumAI shared. I want a short internal proposal by EOD Thu so we can send a recommendation + MOU to the customer and unblock procurement.
> 
> Thanks,
> Logan
From: David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 02:19:00 +08:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Marissa, Logan — thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our CTO:
- APAC must keep PII and user conversation data within Singapore for legal/regulatory reasons. So in-region hosting (Singapore) required for that portion of the traffic.
- Expected ramp: Month 1 ~ 400k tokens/day, Month 2 ~ 1.1M, Month 3 ~ 2.0M. Promo spikes up to ~6M tokens/day (short windows).
- Latency requirement: customer-facing APIs should aim for p95 < 350ms for US users and p95 < 450ms for APAC users.

We're open to a two-pool approach (US Dedicated + APAC Private) if that simplifies compliance. Also open to an initial smaller reserved commitment + burst credits to handle promos. We'll need a simple MOU showing expected costs and provisioning lead time for procurement.

Cheers,
David Jensen
Head of Platform Partnerships
DatumAI

On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 11:05 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Logan — looping David so DatumAI sees our timeline. David: quick ask on your side: can you confirm whether the APAC user sessions must remain in-region (Singapore) or if routing to a proximate public region is acceptable? That affects whether we recommend Redwood Private in APAC or a Dedicated pool in a cloud region.
> 
> Internal note: I've reviewed the traffic sheet — the initial commit looks like ~2M tokens/day user-facing by month 3 with 3x peak delta during promos. I'm attaching a brief proposal draft (PDF) with two options: Reserved-heavy (lower unit cost, higher commit) and Burst-optimized (smaller reserved pool + burst credits). Karthik, please verify the GPU counts — I made an initial pass in the PDF.
> 
> Next steps: Karthik: update counts and include expected latency range for 512-token LLM calls (p50/p95). Mina: write a 2-line compliance note for the MOU about data residency. Logan: can you confirm target SLA we should offer for p95 < 400ms?
> 
> Marissa
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 09:48:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Updated sizing notes after looking at the sheet and running the quick queuing calc:
- US Dedicated pool (primary): baseline reserved 8 x A100-80GB equiv (steady-state handles ~1.6M tokens/day at target latency p95 ~320ms with continuous batching), burst capacity + 4 GPUs for promos (~6M).
- APAC (Singapore): because of in-region residency requirement, recommend Redwood Private deploy (customer-managed VPC) with 4 x A100-80GB equiv reserved + burst pool 2 GPUs. This keeps data residency and reduces cross-border risk.
- Network/latency: with current model and token sizes, expect p50 ~85ms (US), p95 ~320-360ms (US) depending on batch aggressiveness. APAC p95 will be ~420-480ms if we use Private with smaller reservoir — can tune with larger reserved pool.
- Lead time: Dedicated pool spin-up in US ~3-4 weeks (capacity permitting); Private in APAC (onboarding + infra) ~6-8 weeks.
- Cost ballpark (USD/month): US reserved 8-GPU pool ~$62k; burst costs incremental. APAC Private reserved 4-GPU baseline ~$34k + onboarding one-time ~$12k.

I'll convert these into an appendix table for the PDF. If the customer wants lower p95 in APAC we should consider 6 GPUs reserved instead of 4; that'll push monthly cost +50%.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference

On Wed, Jul 15, 2026 at 02:19 AM David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com> wrote:
> Marissa, Logan — thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our CTO:
> - APAC must keep PII and user conversation data within Singapore for legal/regulatory reasons. So in-region hosting (Singapore) required for that portion of the traffic.
> - Expected ramp: Month 1 ~ 400k tokens/day, Month 2 ~ 1.1M, Month 3 ~ 2.0M. Promo spikes up to ~6M tokens/day (short windows).
> - Latency requirement: customer-facing APIs should aim for p95 < 350ms for US users and p95 < 450ms for APAC users.
> 
> We're open to a two-pool approach (US Dedicated + APAC Private) if that simplifies compliance. Also open to an initial smaller reserved commitment + burst credits to handle promos. We'll need a simple MOU showing expected costs and provisioning lead time for procurement.
> 
> Cheers,
> David
From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 07:32:00 +00:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Short compliance note for the MOU (to be included as a paragraph):
- DatumAI APAC traffic containing personal data will be hosted in a customer-controlled Private deployment in Singapore. Redwood will provide audit logs, KMS integration, and SOC2 evidence as part of the onboarding package. Cross-border backups will not be enabled without explicit customer consent.

Also: if the customer expects heavy query analytics on conversation data, advise they segregate analytics workloads (can run in US) from PII-hosted inference nodes in SG. That reduces Private infra footprint while preserving compliance.

Mina Haddad
Director, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 15:41:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

David — thanks for confirming. Per Karthik and Mina's notes, here's the plan we will share with procurement tomorrow: 

Proposal (summary):
1) US Dedicated pool: 8 x A100-80GB reserved, burst capacity +4 GPUs for promo windows. Target p95 < 350ms for US. Provisioning lead time ~3-4 weeks.
2) APAC Private in Singapore: 4 x A100-80GB reserved (option to increase to 6 for tighter p95), onboarding lead time ~6-8 weeks, includes KMS & audit logs.
3) Commercial: initial 6-month reserved commitment with burst credits to handle promos; monthly pricing indicated in the attached MOU draft.
4) Next steps: we will send an MOU (attached) and an appendix with the sizing table and provisioning timelines. If this looks good, David — can you confirm internal approvers and a preferred signature workflow (DocuSign ok)?

I'll attach the MOU and the sizing appendix. Legal will do a quick pass but this is intentionally concise for procurement.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

Attachment: datumai_mou_initial.docx (word, 46KB)

On Thu, Jul 16, 2026 at 07:32 AM Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com> wrote:
> Short compliance note for the MOU (to be included as a paragraph):
> - DatumAI APAC traffic containing personal data will be hosted in a customer-controlled Private deployment in Singapore. Redwood will provide audit logs, KMS integration, and SOC2 evidence as part of the onboarding package. Cross-border backups will not be enabled without explicit customer consent.
> 
> Also: if the customer expects heavy query analytics on conversation data, advise they segregate analytics workloads (can run in US) from PII-hosted inference nodes in SG. That reduces Private infra footprint while preserving compliance.
> 
> Mina Haddad

text:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Team —

Quick summary: DatumAI (enterprise prospect) is pushing to close in Q3. They want dedicated pools in the US and APAC with a single routing policy that prefers US for low-latency users and APAC for APAC traffic. They gave preliminary traffic estimates (attached) and asked for a recommended pool split, expected provisioning lead time, and a short MOU to attach to their procurement.

Asks:
- Marissa: please own the external comms and confirm their expected ramp cadence with them.
- Karthik: can you draft a sizing recommendation (GPU counts by region, burst vs reserved layout) and provide the latency/cost tradeoffs we should show?
- Mina: flag any compliance/region-residency constraints for APAC that would impact Private vs Dedicated.

I attached the traffic spreadsheet DatumAI shared. I want a short internal proposal by EOD Thu so we can send a recommendation + MOU to the customer and unblock procurement.

Thanks,
Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: datumai_traffic_estimates.xlsx (excel, 34KB)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 11:05:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Thanks Logan — looping David so DatumAI sees our timeline. David: quick ask on your side: can you confirm whether the APAC user sessions must remain in-region (Singapore) or if routing to a proximate public region is acceptable? That affects whether we recommend Redwood Private in APAC or a Dedicated pool in a cloud region.

Internal note: I've reviewed the traffic sheet — the initial commit looks like ~2M tokens/day user-facing by month 3 with 3x peak delta during promos. I'm attaching a brief proposal draft (PDF) with two options: Reserved-heavy (lower unit cost, higher commit) and Burst-optimized (smaller reserved pool + burst credits). Karthik, please verify the GPU counts — I made an initial pass in the PDF.

Next steps:
- Karthik: update counts and include expected latency range for 512-token LLM calls (p50/p95).
- Mina: write a 2-line compliance note for the MOU about data residency.
- Logan: can you confirm target SLA we should offer for p95 < 400ms?

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood_region_pool_proposal_v1.pdf (pdf, 220KB)

On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 08:12, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick summary: DatumAI (enterprise prospect) is pushing to close in Q3. They want dedicated pools in the US and APAC with a single routing policy that prefers US for low-latency users and APAC for APAC traffic. They gave preliminary traffic estimates (attached) and asked for a recommended pool split, expected provisioning lead time, and a short MOU to attach to their procurement.
> 
> Asks:
> - Marissa: please own the external comms and confirm their expected ramp cadence with them.
> - Karthik: can you draft a sizing recommendation (GPU counts by region, burst vs reserved layout) and provide the latency/cost tradeoffs we should show?
> - Mina: flag any compliance/region-residency constraints for APAC that would impact Private vs Dedicated.
> 
> I attached the traffic spreadsheet DatumAI shared. I want a short internal proposal by EOD Thu so we can send a recommendation + MOU to the customer and unblock procurement.
> 
> Thanks,
> Logan


From: David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 02:19:00 +08:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Marissa, Logan — thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our CTO:
- APAC must keep PII and user conversation data within Singapore for legal/regulatory reasons. So in-region hosting (Singapore) required for that portion of the traffic.
- Expected ramp: Month 1 ~ 400k tokens/day, Month 2 ~ 1.1M, Month 3 ~ 2.0M. Promo spikes up to ~6M tokens/day (short windows).
- Latency requirement: customer-facing APIs should aim for p95 < 350ms for US users and p95 < 450ms for APAC users.

We're open to a two-pool approach (US Dedicated + APAC Private) if that simplifies compliance. Also open to an initial smaller reserved commitment + burst credits to handle promos. We'll need a simple MOU showing expected costs and provisioning lead time for procurement.

Cheers,
David Jensen
Head of Platform Partnerships
DatumAI

On Tue, Jul 14, 2026 at 11:05 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Logan — looping David so DatumAI sees our timeline. David: quick ask on your side: can you confirm whether the APAC user sessions must remain in-region (Singapore) or if routing to a proximate public region is acceptable? That affects whether we recommend Redwood Private in APAC or a Dedicated pool in a cloud region.
> 
> Internal note: I've reviewed the traffic sheet — the initial commit looks like ~2M tokens/day user-facing by month 3 with 3x peak delta during promos. I'm attaching a brief proposal draft (PDF) with two options: Reserved-heavy (lower unit cost, higher commit) and Burst-optimized (smaller reserved pool + burst credits). Karthik, please verify the GPU counts — I made an initial pass in the PDF.
> 
> Next steps: Karthik: update counts and include expected latency range for 512-token LLM calls (p50/p95). Mina: write a 2-line compliance note for the MOU about data residency. Logan: can you confirm target SLA we should offer for p95 < 400ms?
> 
> Marissa


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 09:48:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Updated sizing notes after looking at the sheet and running the quick queuing calc:
- US Dedicated pool (primary): baseline reserved 8 x A100-80GB equiv (steady-state handles ~1.6M tokens/day at target latency p95 ~320ms with continuous batching), burst capacity + 4 GPUs for promos (~6M).
- APAC (Singapore): because of in-region residency requirement, recommend Redwood Private deploy (customer-managed VPC) with 4 x A100-80GB equiv reserved + burst pool 2 GPUs. This keeps data residency and reduces cross-border risk.
- Network/latency: with current model and token sizes, expect p50 ~85ms (US), p95 ~320-360ms (US) depending on batch aggressiveness. APAC p95 will be ~420-480ms if we use Private with smaller reservoir — can tune with larger reserved pool.
- Lead time: Dedicated pool spin-up in US ~3-4 weeks (capacity permitting); Private in APAC (onboarding + infra) ~6-8 weeks.
- Cost ballpark (USD/month): US reserved 8-GPU pool ~$62k; burst costs incremental. APAC Private reserved 4-GPU baseline ~$34k + onboarding one-time ~$12k.

I'll convert these into an appendix table for the PDF. If the customer wants lower p95 in APAC we should consider 6 GPUs reserved instead of 4; that'll push monthly cost +50%.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference

On Wed, Jul 15, 2026 at 02:19 AM David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com> wrote:
> Marissa, Logan — thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our CTO:
> - APAC must keep PII and user conversation data within Singapore for legal/regulatory reasons. So in-region hosting (Singapore) required for that portion of the traffic.
> - Expected ramp: Month 1 ~ 400k tokens/day, Month 2 ~ 1.1M, Month 3 ~ 2.0M. Promo spikes up to ~6M tokens/day (short windows).
> - Latency requirement: customer-facing APIs should aim for p95 < 350ms for US users and p95 < 450ms for APAC users.
> 
> We're open to a two-pool approach (US Dedicated + APAC Private) if that simplifies compliance. Also open to an initial smaller reserved commitment + burst credits to handle promos. We'll need a simple MOU showing expected costs and provisioning lead time for procurement.
> 
> Cheers,
> David


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 07:32:00 +00:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

Short compliance note for the MOU (to be included as a paragraph):
- DatumAI APAC traffic containing personal data will be hosted in a customer-controlled Private deployment in Singapore. Redwood will provide audit logs, KMS integration, and SOC2 evidence as part of the onboarding package. Cross-border backups will not be enabled without explicit customer consent.

Also: if the customer expects heavy query analytics on conversation data, advise they segregate analytics workloads (can run in US) from PII-hosted inference nodes in SG. That reduces Private infra footprint while preserving compliance.

Mina Haddad
Director, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: David Jensen <david.jensen@datumai.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 15:41:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: DatumAI region capacity ask: sizing + proposal

David — thanks for confirming. Per Karthik and Mina's notes, here's the plan we will share with procurement tomorrow: 

Proposal (summary):
1) US Dedicated pool: 8 x A100-80GB reserved, burst capacity +4 GPUs for promo windows. Target p95 < 350ms for US. Provisioning lead time ~3-4 weeks.
2) APAC Private in Singapore: 4 x A100-80GB reserved (option to increase to 6 for tighter p95), onboarding lead time ~6-8 weeks, includes KMS & audit logs.
3) Commercial: initial 6-month reserved commitment with burst credits to handle promos; monthly pricing indicated in the attached MOU draft.
4) Next steps: we will send an MOU (attached) and an appendix with the sizing table and provisioning timelines. If this looks good, David — can you confirm internal approvers and a preferred signature workflow (DocuSign ok)?

I'll attach the MOU and the sizing appendix. Legal will do a quick pass but this is intentionally concise for procurement.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference

Attachment: datumai_mou_initial.docx (word, 46KB)

On Thu, Jul 16, 2026 at 07:32 AM Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com> wrote:
> Short compliance note for the MOU (to be included as a paragraph):
> - DatumAI APAC traffic containing personal data will be hosted in a customer-controlled Private deployment in Singapore. Redwood will provide audit logs, KMS integration, and SOC2 evidence as part of the onboarding package. Cross-border backups will not be enabled without explicit customer consent.
> 
> Also: if the customer expects heavy query analytics on conversation data, advise they segregate analytics workloads (can run in US) from PII-hosted inference nodes in SG. That reduces Private infra footprint while preserving compliance.
> 
> Mina Haddad

source.subject: Demo request — pricing page (budget intent)
messages:
From: Emily Pierce <emily@greenfieldretail.com>
To: pricing@redwood.com
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Demo request — pricing page (budget intent)

Hi Redwood team,

I completed the pricing CTA and indicated budget intent; our procurement and product teams are evaluating inference platforms for a pilot focused on customer support summarization and search. We're a mid-market retail org (Greenfield Retail) with volume expectations of ~50k queries/day in Q4 — I'd like to see pricing guidance and a short demo to understand latency/cost tradeoffs.

Preferred windows (PDT): Wed Jul 14 11:00–13:00 or Thu Jul 15 09:00–11:00.

I'll include our engineering lead, Tom Alvarez (cc'd on follow-up). I attached a short procurement checklist for context.

Thanks,
Emily Pierce
Head of Product
Greenfield Retail
Attachment: procurement_prep.pdf
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Emily Pierce <emily@greenfieldretail.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — pricing page (budget intent)

Hi Emily — great to hear from you and thanks for filling out the pricing CTA. I'm Ben (SDR) on the account; Logan (AE) and Stephanie (solutions) are looped in. We can absolutely run a focused 30–45 minute walkthrough that covers pricing, deployment options (hosted vs dedicated), and a quick latency/cost sketch based on your volume.

I have the following slots (PDT):
- Wed Jul 14 11:30–12:00
- Thu Jul 15 09:30–10:15
- Thu Jul 15 14:00–14:45 (if you prefer afternoon)

Or feel free to pick on our Calendly: https://calendly.com/redwood-demo/30min?signup=gf

Two quick questions to help us prepare:
1) Any PII/PI requirements we should know about?
2) Who will be on the call (product, infra, procurement)?

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Sales Development Representative
Redwood Inference
From: Emily Pierce <emily@greenfieldretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@greenfieldretail.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — pricing page (budget intent)

Thanks Ben — Thu Jul 15 09:30–10:15 PDT works best for us. Attendees: me (product), Tom Alvarez (eng lead), and one procurement contact who may join late. For PII: none of our data in the pilot will contain customer PII; we do require audit logging and region controls (US-only) for the pilot.

I've attached a network/security checklist Tom compiled — will be handy for the meeting.

See you Thu; please send a calendar invite.

Best,
Emily
Attachment: network_requirements.xlsx
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Emily Pierce <emily@greenfieldretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — pricing page (budget intent)

Emily — great, thanks for confirming. I've reserved Thu Jul 15 09:30–10:15 PDT on our side and will send a calendar invite with a short agenda. Agenda items: 1) quick product fit + architecture (15m), 2) pricing scenarios for 50k q/day and deployment options (15m), 3) next steps and procurement timeline (15m).

I'll also attach an initial cost sketch we can refine in the call. If you have any specific SLAs or audit log retention targets, drop them in reply and we'll have Stephanie bring the details.

Sending invite now — please accept if the time looks good.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Account Executive
Redwood Inference
Attachment: redwood_demo_invite.ics
From: Emily Pierce <emily@greenfieldretail.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@greenfieldretail.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — pricing page (budget intent)

Hi Logan — confirmed, the invite for Thu Jul 15 09:30 PDT came through and we've accepted. Tom asked if you can include a one-page security checklist or SOC2 pointers in the invite so procurement has a heads-up before the call. If you can attach that (or a short link to the SOC2 report) that would be great.

Looking forward to the conversation.

Thanks,
Emily Pierce
Head of Product
Greenfield Retail

source.subject: Fixed+Usage hybrid pricing request — amortization and migration credit details
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Amy Chen <amy.chen@therahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Jul 2026 09:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot commercial — hybrid fee question

Hi Amy —

Thanks again for the time last week. Quick follow-up on the pricing construct we discussed: TheraHealth is asking whether we can structure a hybrid fixed+usage model where the platform fee is amortized over 12 months instead of charged up-front, and whether we can apply a migration credit against the first two invoices.

Attached is the pricing matrix I promised (thera_pricing_matrix_v3.xlsx). High-level ask from your side: confirm target run-rate for pilot (approx tokens/month) and whether procurement prefers an invoice-ready breakdown (line items: platform fee amortization, usage, support tier).

Proposed next steps:
- I send a draft SOW and invoice template once you confirm the pilot run-rate.
- If procurement likes the amortization approach we’ll show the 12-month amortized schedule plus a one-time migration credit (we can propose 15–25% of the platform fee applied to invoice 1 and 2).

Looping Marissa and Irene to advise on acceptable discount windows and legal items.

Thanks,
Logan
Logan Wright
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
(Attachment: thera_pricing_matrix_v3.xlsx)

From: Amy Chen <amy.chen@therahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: TheraHealth Procurement <procurement@therahealth.com>, Carlos Moreno <carlos@cloudops.co>
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 09:36:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot commercial — hybrid fee question

Logan —

Appreciate the quick follow-up. For the pilot run-rate: our initial estimate is ~120M inference tokens/month (chat + embeddings workload). Procurement would prefer a clear invoice with amortization schedule broken out (we need line-by-line for internal approvals).

Two questions from my side:
1) Can the migration credit be capped (we only need help covering engineering uplift for the first 2 months)?
2) For support, can you confirm the response SLAs for Standard vs Premium and whether the support tier can be swapped after month 3?

If you can send a draft SOW and invoice that lays this out, I’ll start routing to procurement legal. We’d like DocuSign-ready materials so we can move quickly if numbers align.

—Amy
Amy Chen
Head of Product, TheraHealth
(Please cc procurement for billing items)

> On Sun, Jul 12, 2026 at 9:07 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > [original message and attachment omitted]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Amy Chen <amy.chen@therahealth.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 13:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot commercial — hybrid fee question

Thanks Amy — copying in finance and legal. Quick proposal from the commercial side (high level):

source.subject: Brightpath production degradation — urgent mitigation
messages:
From: Dana Liu <dana@brightpath.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-14T22:12:07-07:00
Subject: High error rate + latency on prod

Logan —

We're seeing a large spike in 500s and p99 latency across our.calls endpoint beginning ~21:45 PDT. Traffic looks normal but error rate jumped to ~8% and p99 is ~2.6s (baseline ~0.6s). Throughput dropped by ~30% as clients are timing out and retrying.

Attached: a short trace dump and a screenshot of our Grafana panel.

Can you confirm if anything changed on your side? This is impacting SLAs — we need an ETA.

Thanks,
Dana Liu
Sr. SRE, Brightpath
Dana Liu | Brightpath Inc.

Attachments:
- brightpath-trace-2026-07-14.json (trace dump, 120KB)
- screenshot-500s.png (grafana capture)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc:
Date: 2026-07-14T22:18:33-07:00
Subject: Fwd: High error rate + latency on prod

Folks — forwarding Brightpath’s alert. Their traces point to a bunch of 502/500s originating from our model-serving tier, and the traffic pattern maps to a surge of small prefix-heavy requests. We rolled an optimization to prefix eviction/prefetch yesterday evening (config id: prefetch-v3) which could be misbehaving with their workload.

Immediate asks:
- Karthik/Jonas: can you validate whether prefetch-v3 was active on Brightpath’s Dedicated pool? If so, prioritize a rollback or mitigation.
- Marissa: please loop Brightpath with a short acknowledgement and let them know we’re on it — transparency matters here.

If this is not contained within 1 hour I want exec visibility.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Acct Exec, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

On Thu, Jul 14, 2026 at 22:12, Dana Liu <dana@brightpath.ai> wrote:
> Logan —
>
> We're seeing a large spike in 500s and p99 latency across our.calls endpoint beginning ~21:45 PDT....
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-14T22:27:50-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: High error rate + latency on prod

Quick analysis from the first-pass metrics and traces (attached runbook snippet):
- KEYS: high miss rate on the prefix store for Brightpath hot prefixes ~ 78% miss; eviction churn spiked at 21:42 concomitant with p99 jump.
- Symptoms: HOL blocking on hot partitions because background prefetch is aggressively evicting active prefixes (prefetch-v3). This causes long tail stalls as hot requests wait for KV fetch and then cascade to batching lanes.

Immediate mitigations I recommend (fast, reversible):
1) Disable prefetch-v3 for Brightpath namespace (hotfix toggle) — expected restoration in ~6–8 min after cache warms.
2) Temporarily enlarge hot-lane slot count by +30% for their Dedicated pool to reduce enqueue blocking — risk: higher GPU memory pressure but short window ok.
3) Bypass continuous batching for priority lane for Brightpath (i.e., force immediate dispatch of small requests) — cost increase but reduces queueing and p99.

I can run 1) now if we have a quick thumbs-up. If disabling prefetch doesn’t drop error rate to <1% within 20m we should roll back the prefetch change across the canary cohort and escalate to execs for broader action.

Attachments: mitigation-playbook-v1.pdf (playbook excerpt)

Karthik Iyer
SRE Lead, Serving Runtime
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-14T22:32:12-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: High error rate + latency on prod

Do it. Karthik: proceed with 1) disable prefetch-v3 for Brightpath namespace immediately.
Runbook steps I’ll take in parallel:
- Orchestrate a 10-minute canary on one Dedicated host first (validate traces/metrics).
- If canary stabilizes, expand to remaining hosts in 2 waves (5 min each).
- Monitor hot-lane queue lengths, p99, and 500s. Watch GPU mem; if we exceed safe thresholds rollback and notify.

Marissa — pls send Brightpath a short note (see template below) and add execs to the loop if we don’t see recovery by 23:10 PDT.

ETA: canary start in 3 minutes.

Jonas Weber
Head of Operations
jonas.weber@redwood.ai
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Liu <dana@brightpath.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-15T00:02:10-07:00
Subject: RE: High error rate + latency on prod

Dana —

Thanks for the alert and for the trace dump. We apologize for the disruption. Our SRE team identified an interaction between a new prefetch/eviction policy (prefetch-v3) and Brightpath’s prefix-heavy traffic that caused hot-partition thrash and head-of-line blocking.

What we did: 1) disabled prefetch-v3 for Brightpath’s Dedicated pool, 2) enlarged hot-lane slots by 30% for their hosts, and 3) temporarily bypassed continuous batching for Brightpath priority lane. Early telemetry shows p99 falling toward baseline and error rate dropping — we’ll continue to monitor for the next 30–60 minutes.

Next steps: we will run a staged canary rollback on the broader canary cohort tomorrow morning after further validation, and we’ll provide a post-incident summary with root cause, timeline, and permanant mitigations (config hardening + additional safety checks on prefetch rollouts).

We understand the SLA impact and will credit Brightpath for the affected window; Logan will follow up on the commercial side. If you need a live bridge, we can join now — otherwise I’ll update you in 20 minutes with fresh metrics.

Sincerely,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

On Thu, Jul 14, 2026 at 22:12, Dana Liu <dana@brightpath.ai> wrote:
> Logan —
>
> We're seeing a large spike in 500s and p99 latency across our.calls endpoint beginning ~21:45 PDT....

Subject: RE: High error rate + latency on prod

Dana —

Thanks for the alert and for the trace dump. We apologize for the disruption. Our SRE team identified an interaction between a new prefetch/eviction policy (prefetch-v3) and Brightpath’s prefix-heavy traffic that caused hot-partition thrash and head-of-line blocking.

What we did: 1) disabled prefetch-v3 for Brightpath’s Dedicated pool, 2) enlarged hot-lane slots by 30% for their hosts, and 3) temporarily bypassed continuous batching for Brightpath priority lane. Early telemetry shows p99 falling toward baseline and error rate dropping — we’ll continue to monitor for the next 30–60 minutes.

Next steps: we will run a staged canary rollback on the broader canary cohort tomorrow morning after further validation, and we’ll provide a post-incident summary with root cause, timeline, and permanant mitigations (config hardening + additional safety checks on prefetch rollouts).

We understand the SLA impact and will credit Brightpath for the affected window; Logan will follow up on the commercial side. If you need a live bridge, we can join now — otherwise I’ll update you in 20 minutes with fresh metrics.

Sincerely,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

> Logan —

Date: 2026-07-14T22:12:07-07:00
Subject: High error rate + latency on prod

Logan —

We're seeing a large spike in 500s and p99 latency across our.calls endpoint beginning ~21:45 PDT. Traffic looks normal but error rate jumped to ~8% and p99 is ~2.6s (baseline ~0.6s). Throughput dropped by ~30% as clients are timing out and retrying.

Attached: a short trace dump and a screenshot of our Grafana panel.

Can you confirm if anything changed on your side? This is impacting SLAs — we need an ETA.

Thanks,
Dana Liu
Sr. SRE, Brightpath
Dana Liu | Brightpath Inc.

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>

Cc:
Date: 2026-07-14T22:18:33-07:00
Subject: Fwd: High error rate + latency on prod

Folks — forwarding Brightpath’s alert. Their traces point to a bunch of 502/500s originating from our model-serving tier, and the traffic pattern maps to a surge of small prefix-heavy requests. We rolled an optimization to prefix eviction/prefetch yesterday evening (config id: prefetch-v3) which could be misbehaving with their workload.

Immediate asks:
- Karthik/Jonas: can you validate whether prefetch-v3 was active on Brightpath’s Dedicated pool? If so, prioritize a rollback or mitigation.
- Marissa: please loop Brightpath with a short acknowledgement and let them know we’re on it — transparency matters here.

If this is not contained within 1 hour I want exec visibility.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Acct Exec, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-14T22:27:50-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: High error rate + latency on prod

Quick analysis from the first-pass metrics and traces (attached runbook snippet):
- KEYS: high miss rate on the prefix store for Brightpath hot prefixes ~ 78% miss; eviction churn spiked at 21:42 concomitant with p99 jump.
- Symptoms: HOL blocking on hot partitions because background prefetch is aggressively evicting active prefixes (prefetch-v3). This causes long tail stalls as hot requests wait for KV fetch and then cascade to batching lanes.

Immediate mitigations I recommend (fast, reversible):
1) Disable prefetch-v3 for Brightpath namespace (hotfix toggle) — expected restoration in ~6–8 min after cache warms.
2) Temporarily enlarge hot-lane slot count by +30% for their Dedicated pool to reduce enqueue blocking — risk: higher GPU memory pressure but short window ok.
3) Bypass continuous batching for priority lane for Brightpath (i.e., force immediate dispatch of small requests) — cost increase but reduces queueing and p99.

I can run 1) now if we have a quick thumbs-up. If disabling prefetch doesn’t drop error rate to <1% within 20m we should roll back the prefetch change across the canary cohort and escalate to execs for broader action.

Attachments: mitigation-playbook-v1.pdf (playbook excerpt)

Karthik Iyer
SRE Lead, Serving Runtime
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-14T22:32:12-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: High error rate + latency on prod

Do it. Karthik: proceed with 1) disable prefetch-v3 for Brightpath namespace immediately.
Runbook steps I’ll take in parallel:
- Orchestrate a 10-minute canary on one Dedicated host first (validate traces/metrics).
- If canary stabilizes, expand to remaining hosts in 2 waves (5 min each).
- Monitor hot-lane queue lengths, p99, and 500s. Watch GPU mem; if we exceed safe thresholds rollback and notify.

Marissa — pls send Brightpath a short note (see template below) and add execs to the loop if we don’t see recovery by 23:10 PDT.

ETA: canary start in 3 minutes.

Jonas Weber
Head of Operations
jonas.weber@redwood.ai

messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Amira Khan <amira.khan@acme-corp.com>, infra-team@acme-corp.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 09 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private deployment: environment smoke validation and quick handoff

Hi Amira / Acme infra team,

We're scheduled to run a quick smoke validation for the Redwood Private deployment next week. To make the runbook efficient, I've attached a short checklist (redwood-private-checklist.xlsx) and the current network topology we sketched (network_topology_v2.drawio). Can you confirm the following before we book the 60-minute window?

- Edge gateway (VPN or Direct Connect) is configured and tested for bi-directional traffic to the 10.48.0.0/16 CIDR (see attached acme-vpc-cidr.txt).
- Firewall rules allow outbound TLS 443 to *.redwood.ai and inbound-only on the management port 2200 from our IP range (listed in the checklist).
- KMS access is provisioned for the service principal redwood-private@acme-corp; we need decrypt permissions for the onboarding key.
- An ops contact (pager/email) who will be on the call and can approve quick rollbacks.

If you can confirm the items above, propose two 60-minute slots next week and we'll lock one. If anything looks risky, flag it and we'll split the validation into a minimal set first (connectivity + API auth), then a scale step later.

Thanks —
Logan
Logan Wright
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: redwood-private-checklist.xlsx (xlsx), network_topology_v2.drawio (diagram), acme-vpc-cidr.txt (text)

From: Amira Khan <amira.khan@acme-corp.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: infra-team@acme-corp.com, Samir Rao <samir@cloudops.io>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment: environment smoke validation and quick handoff

Thanks for the checklist, Logan — this is helpful. Quick answers from the infra side:

- CIDR: Our declared VPC range is 10.48.0.0/16 (file attached). We have a transit gateway peered to our on-prem edge.
- Edge gateway: VPN is up and we can do a test tunnel; however the ops team wants to validate sustained sessions for ~30 minutes before the full run.
- Firewall: We allow outbound TLS 443 to external APIs, but we'll need the exact FQDNs/IP ranges for any management/control plane ports. 
- KMS: Pending — security needs the service principal ARN and expected key rotation policy to approve.

Samir (cc'd) is our on-prem lead and will join the call if we lock a slot. Two asks from our side:
1) Can you provide the management host FQDNs (or the IP ranges) we should permit?
2) Are there any non-standard ports used by the runtime that we should be aware of?

Appreciate it —
Amira
Amira Khan
Head of Platform Engineering, Acme Health
amira.khan@acme-corp.com

On Thu, Jul 9, 2026 at 9:12 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> We're scheduled to run a quick smoke validation for the Redwood Private deployment next week. To make the runbook efficient, I've attached a short checklist...

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Private deployment: environment smoke validation and quick handoff

Folks — forwarding Amira's note. Below are the quick items I can confirm and what I'll action Monday morning.

Answers to their asks:
- Management FQDNs/IP ranges: redwood-control-1.redwood.ai (34.210.11.22), redwood-control-2.redwood.ai (34.210.11.23). We'll also provide a CIDR for our agent egress: 192.88.32.0/24. I'll add both to the drive doc and send an ACL snippet.
- Runtime ports: Primarily 443 (TLS) and 2200 (SSH-like management port). For health and metrics we use 9100/tcp (Prometheus exporter) but that is optional and only required if Acme wants metrics scraped.
- Service principal/KMS: Dev will share the service principal ARN and a minimal IAM policy. We use a single key with yearly rotation by default; if they require a custom rotation window we can sign an exception for onboarding and then adjust.

Planned actions (Mon):
1) Publish the ACL snippet and the friendly FQDN list to the shared Drive link.
2) Dev will create the service principal and paste the ARN in HS-46281.
3) Ethan will schedule a 60-minute slot and include a rollback checklist in the calendar invite.

Also: Samir mentioned a sustained-session check — simple: we'll run a 30-minute continuous metadata exchange between their edge and our control plane prior to the main test. That should satisfy their gating request.

—Monica
Monica Patel
Solutions Manager, Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai

> On Fri, Jul 10, 2026 at 8:45 AM Amira Khan <amira.khan@acme-corp.com> wrote:
> > Thanks for the checklist, Logan — this is helpful. Quick answers from the infra side:...

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Amira Khan <amira.khan@acme-corp.com>, infra-team@acme-corp.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Jul 2026 16:44:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private deployment: environment smoke validation and quick handoff

Great — thanks everyone for the quick turnaround. Confirming the plan and next steps: 

1) Smoke test window: Wed, Jul 15, 2026 10:00-11:00 PDT. I will send the calendar invite with a Zoom link and the rollback checklist.
2) Pre-check (Tue, Jul 14) 09:00-09:30 PDT: sustained-session test between Acme edge and our control plane (Monica to run). This will validate the VPN tunnel under continuous session load.
3) Firewall ACLs: Acme to permit 34.210.11.22/32, 34.210.11.23/32 and 192.88.32.0/24 outbound to their VPC on ports 443 and 2200.
4) KMS: Dev will post the service principal ARN and the minimal policy in the HubSpot ticket HS-46281 before Tue 18:00 PDT.
5) Rollback: If we see authentication failures or TLS negotiation errors, we'll stop and rollback (step-by-step rollback in the attached checklist).

Attachments included in the invite: redwood-private-checklist.xlsx, network_topology_v2.drawio, acme-vpc-cidr.txt

If the Acme infra-team confirms the two network CIDRs and the KMS timeline, we'll proceed. Amira / Samir — can you ACK here so I can finalize the invites?

Best,
Logan
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

4) KMS: Dev will post the service principal ARN and the minimal policy in the HubSpot ticket HS-46281 before Tue 18:00 PDT.
5) Rollback: If we see authentication failures or TLS negotiation errors, we'll stop and rollback (step-by-step rollback in the attached checklist).

Attachments included in the invite: redwood-private-checklist.xlsx, network_topology_v2.drawio, acme-vpc-cidr.txt

If the Acme infra-team confirms the two network CIDRs and the KMS timeline, we'll proceed. Amira / Samir — can you ACK here so I can finalize the invites?

Best,
Logan
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

> > Folks — forwarding Amira's note. Below are the quick items I can confirm...

source.subject: Action required: throughput & error threshold — remediation and compensation plan
messages:
From: Elena Morales <elena@cascade.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Price <jordan.price@cascade.ai>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 08:23:00 -0700
Subject: Unacceptable throughput degradation + elevated 5xx — urgent

Logan —

We hit a persistent degradation this morning on our production calls to the rwinf-medium endpoint. Over the 06:00-08:00 PT window we saw effective throughput drop ~45% and sustained 5xx error rates averaging 8% (peaked ~14%) for our standard inference pipeline. This materially impacted our customer-facing recommendation feature.

We attached a short snapshot (error_rate_snapshot_2026-07-10.csv) and a brief latency report. We need:
- Immediate mitigation: can you confirm whether a rollback / model variant fallback was triggered on your side?
- A root cause ETA and timeline to full recovery
- Commercial remediation — given the SLA targets (99.95% availability / <1% 5xx for this tier), Cascade will expect a make-good proposal (credits or refund) and a contract amendment if this persists.

Elena
Director, Platform Engineering
Cascade AI

Attachments: error_rate_snapshot_2026-07-10.csv (csv), latency_report_2026-07-10.pdf (pdf)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@cascade.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Unacceptable throughput degradation + elevated 5xx — urgent

Elena — thanks for the signal and the attachments. I'm escalating to our SRE and CS leads immediately. Preliminary notes: we observed an increased queuing depth on the affected fleet in us-west-2 which correlates with a downstream storage flakiness during the window you cited. We did not auto-fallback to the smaller model variant because the routing policy kept your traffic pinned to the region for data locality.

Immediate next steps from our side (I will coordinate):
1) Ben (SRE) to validate whether autoscaling/burst policies were inhibited and start mitigation.
2) Naomi (Customer Success) to open a customer incident channel and coordinate running reports from both sides.
3) I'll pull our request traces and KV cache hit metrics and share a timeline within 3 hours.

Logan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: preliminary incident triage for Cascade AI

Team — quick triage:
- Observed: CPU and GPU utility on a subset of the us-west-2 pool spiking with concurrent host-level storage I/O timeouts between 05:40-08:10 PT.
- Impact: increased request latencies leading to client timeouts and upstream retries, manifesting as elevated 5xx.
- Temporary mitigation applied at 10:55 PT: expanded burst capacity and rebalanced traffic to secondary availability zones; we also increased max in-flight concurrency per-worker for the affected nodes to reduce queuing.

Attached: latency_report_2026-07-10.pdf (summary charts). Next actions:
- Collect per-request trace (Karthik to help correlate kernel fallback events).
- Prepare a remediation/commercial proposal for the account — suggest we propose a tiered credit (immediate service credit + conditional amendment if recurrence).

Ben
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference

Attachments: latency_report_2026-07-10.pdf

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@cascade.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Jordan Price <jordan.price@cascade.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Jul 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Proposed remediation & commercial make-good for Cascade AI incident

Elena / Jordan —

Thanks for the patience; appreciate the data you sent. Summary of our proposal:
1) Technical: we implemented the AZ rebalancing and burst expansion; metrics indicate throughput recovered to within SLA by 13:30 PT. We will add a targeted routing rule to allow automatic model-variant fallback for your org if regional contention repeats (opt-in). Implementation ETA: 48-72 hours for rule + testing.

2) Commercial: immediate service credit equivalent to 12 hours of prorated service (applied to July invoice) to account for the outage window and downstream customer impact. In addition, if a comparable breach recurs within the next 90 days we will provide an additive credit (up to 7 days prorated service) and prioritize a dedicated capacity review for Cascade.

3) Contract: attached redline (redline_contract_amendment_v1.docx) documents the optional fallback policy and the escalation SLA addendum. If acceptable, we'll convert to a DocuSign amendment and apply the initial service credit after signature.

Please let me know if that addresses Cascade's expectations; happy to set a 30-minute exec call to finalize.

Best —
Naomi Feldman
Senior Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redline_contract_amendment_v1.docx

From: Jordan Price <jordan.price@cascade.ai>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena@cascade.ai>
Date: Sat, 11 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposed remediation & commercial make-good for Cascade AI incident

Naomi — thank you. The immediate credit approach plus a conditional escalation credit on recurrence is reasonable. Two asks before we sign:
- Can Redwood commit to a post-incident RCA delivery within 5 business days (detailed timeline and mitigations)?
- For the fallback opt-in, we need confirmation that output parity and latency delta between model variants will be documented so our product team can evaluate.

If you can confirm both and share the DocuSign, we'll aim to sign the amendment next week.

Jordan
Head of Product, Cascade AI

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio.alvarez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@cascade.ai>, Jordan Price <jordan.price@cascade.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Jul 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Executive summary & next steps — Cascade AI remediation

Team / Elena & Jordan —

Summary of current status and asks (for leadership visibility):
- Incident: 2026-07-10 05:40-08:10 PT — throughput ~45% down and 5xx spiked to ~8-14% for Cascade AI traffic in us-west-2.
- Mitigation: AZ rebalancing + burst capacity applied; throughput returned to SLA by ~13:30 PT on 7/10. Temporary measures in place.
- Commercial offer: Naomi proposed 12-hour prorated service credit plus conditional escalation credit on recurrence; Cascade indicated this is tentatively acceptable pending RCA and model-variant parity docs.

Leadership asks (for approval and any exceptions):
1) Approve the proposed commercial package (12-hour credit + conditional escalation credit up to 7 days prorated).
2) Karthik / Ben: commit to RCA delivery by 5 business days (target: 2026-07-17) with kernel traces and storage I/O correlation. Include a short appendix on why auto-fallback was not triggered and options to make opt-in fallback safe for Cascade (QA plan + parity metrics).
3) Naomi: prepare the DocuSign amendment and schedule a 30-min exec review call with Cascade for 7/14 or 7/15.

If approved, I will reply to Cascade to confirm timelines and route the amendment to DocuSign (we will apply the immediate credit after signature as proposed).

Thanks —
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Redwood Inference — logan.wright@redwood.com — +1 (415) 555-0184

source.subject: SDE2 standard offer — base/bonus/equity sign-off
messages:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Jul 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Draft offer for Maya Rao (SDE2) — numbers for sign-off

Hi Logan —

Following up with the finalized numbers we discussed for Maya Rao (SDE2). Candidate just completed the loop last week and is asking for a formal offer today if we can move quickly. Below is the proposed package:

- Base: $155,000
- Target bonus: 10% (annual; prorated for first year)
- Equity: 4,000 RSUs vesting over 4 years (25% cliff)
- Signing bonus: $10,000 (one-time)
- Start date target: 2026-09-01

Attached: offer_breakdown_MayaRao_20260712.pdf and comp_approval_matrix_q3.xlsx for approvals and cost center sign-off.

Ask: can Marissa confirm this fits the comp band for SDE2 (Level 4) and Neha confirm this fits the headcount/cost center budget? If both approve, I'll prepare the DocuSign.

Quick context: candidate is currently at a fintech startup and asked for market-competitive base — we landed roughly mid-band with modest RSU uplift to secure acceptance.

Thanks,
Karthik
Recruiter, Talent Acquisition
Redwood Inference

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Jul 2026 10:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft offer for Maya Rao (SDE2) — numbers for sign-off

Marissa / Neha —

Quick nod please. Hiring manager sign-off is in — we agreed to the mix above to keep cash reasonable while hitting total comp expectations. A couple of specific questions:

1) Marissa: given the candidate's experience (4.5 yrs, two years leading on backend infra), is 4,000 RSUs an appropriate equity grant or should we push to 4,800 RSUs to match internal parity?

2) Neha: is the signing bonus within policy for international transfers and approved for the ENG-Platform cost center (CC: ENG-PLAT-03)? I flagged the start date for early September in case payroll/relocation paperwork is needed.

If you can reply with short approval or recommended adjustments, I can lock the offer today.

Cheers,
Logan
Hiring Manager — Platform
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Jul 2026 12:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft offer for Maya Rao (SDE2) — numbers for sign-off

Logan / Karthik —

Thanks for the detail. From a comp band standpoint:
- Base $155k: mid-to-high for SDE2 in US-west; acceptable.
- Target bonus 10%: standard.
- RSUs: 4,000 is reasonable; parity would be ~4,500 for candidates with lead-ish responsibilities. Given hiring velocity and the signing bonus, I'd keep it at 4,000 unless Logan wants to elevate to 4,800 for retention leverage.
- Signing bonus $10k: within discretionary sign-on range for SDE2 when relocation or counteroffer risk exists.

Approval: I approve the package as-is (base $155k / 10% bonus / 4,000 RSUs / $10k sign-on). If you want a higher RSU grant, provide business justification (ownership scope, retention risk) and I'll re-evaluate.

I added comments on the attached comp_approval_matrix_q3.xlsx (see columns 'Justification' and 'Approver').

-Marissa
Sr. Total Rewards Partner
Redwood Inference

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Jul 2026 15:30:00 -07:00

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>

Subject: Draft offer for Maya Rao (SDE2) — numbers for sign-off

- Base: $155,000
- Target bonus: 10% (annual; prorated for first year)
- Equity: 4,000 RSUs vesting over 4 years (25% cliff)
- Signing bonus: $10,000 (one-time)
- Start date target: 2026-09-01

Attached: offer_breakdown_MayaRao_20260712.pdf and comp_approval_matrix_q3.xlsx for approvals and cost center sign-off.

Ask: can Marissa confirm this fits the comp band for SDE2 (Level 4) and Neha confirm this fits the headcount/cost center budget? If both approve, I'll prepare the DocuSign.

Quick context: candidate is currently at a fintech startup and asked for market-competitive base — we landed roughly mid-band with modest RSU uplift to secure acceptance.

Thanks,
Karthik
Recruiter, Talent Acquisition
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Draft offer for Maya Rao (SDE2) — numbers for sign-off

Marissa / Neha —

Quick nod please. Hiring manager sign-off is in — we agreed to the mix above to keep cash reasonable while hitting total comp expectations. A couple of specific questions:

1) Marissa: given the candidate's experience (4.5 yrs, two years leading on backend infra), is 4,000 RSUs an appropriate equity grant or should we push to 4,800 RSUs to match internal parity?

- Signing bonus $10k: within discretionary sign-on range for SDE2 when relocation or counteroffer risk exists.
