source.subject: Onboarding playbook and regional support evidence handoff
messages:
From: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: marketplace@cloudsuite.com
Date: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the intro last week — excited to get Redwood into the CloudSuite Marketplace. To keep the listing and co-sell timeline tight, our listing team needs a short package of artifacts and a clarified support coverage statement. Can you confirm the items below and share the files where possible?

Must-have artifacts for listing validation:
- Branding asset pack (logos: PNG/SVG, approved marketing copy)
- Claims evidence (SOC2 summary, latency/cost blurb for product page)
- Support matrix (hours, timezone coverage, escalation contacts)
- Marketplace-specific evidence (AWS vendor form, Azure co-sell checklist, GCP listing confirmation)

Questions from the marketplace ops side:
1) Do you commit to 24x7 first-response for Production-level customers, or is support tiered by plan?
2) For APAC customers, do you have an on-call rotation that covers APAC hours or do you rely on automatic routing to a US team?

Timeline: our intake sprint closes next Thursday (2026-07-15). If we can get the files by EOD 2026-07-09 we can guarantee a spot in the next publication cycle.

I attached our intake template to this email for completeness (marketplace intake template v1).

Best,
Aisha Kumar
Partner Ops Manager | CloudSuite Inc.
Aisha.k@cloudsuite.com

Attachment: marketplace_intake_template_v1.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 14:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Ben (sales) and Marissa (marketplace ops) to own the artifacts and evidence. Quick answers to your questions up front:

- Support model: Redwood offers tiered support. For Dedicated/Enterprise customers (what we expect most marketplace listings to point to) we provide 24x7 incident response with a documented SLA. For self-serve hosted API customers we provide business-hours support with paid 24x7 add-on.
- APAC coverage: we operate a primary APAC on-call rotation (Asia-Pacific hours) for high-tier customers; routing for lower tiers goes to a US-based follow-the-sun queue.

Marissa & Ben — can you confirm the exact SLA wording we should publish on the listing and attach the evidence files? I started a shared drive folder here: https://drive.google.com/redwood/onboarding-playbook — please drop final files there.

Requested items from our side:
- redwood_listing_evidence_checklist.xlsx (attached)
- aws_evidence_template.pdf (AWS vendor evidence filled where relevant)
- support-matrix.csv (shows coverage by tier and region)

Proposed timeline: provide artifacts by EOD Friday (2026-07-09) so Aisha can intake next week. If any of you foresee delays, call out the blocker and propose a two-day alternate.

Thanks —
Logan Wright
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

> On Tue, Jul 7, 2026 at 10:15 AM Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com> wrote:
> > Thanks again for the intro last week — excited to get Redwood into the CloudSuite Marketplace. To keep the listing and co-sell timeline tight, our listing team needs a short package of artifacts and a clarified support coverage statement. Can you confirm the items below and share the files where possible?

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 8 Jul 2026 09:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Aisha / Logan — quick note: we should confirm co-sell eligibility window on the marketplace side (any co-sell badge requirements from CloudSuite?) and whether this listing can be flagged for co-sell outreach once live. From a go-to-market perspective we want the listing to include the co-sell CTA and a sales contact with HubSpot deal id HBSP-48392.

APAC support follow-up: can Marissa confirm that for Enterprise customers we have APAC on-call (yes/no)? If yes, please paste the exact SLA blurb we can include on page copy. If no, we should propose a managed 24x7 add-on.

Also, if anyone sees missing evidence items (privacy/data location notes, encryption at rest certs) call it out now.

Thx,
Ben Carter
Channel Sales Lead | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

> On Tue, Jul 7, 2026 at 2:02 PM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Ben (sales) and Marissa (marketplace ops) to own the artifacts and evidence. Quick answers to your questions up front:

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Jul 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Thanks, team — attaching the completed checklist and the region-specific evidence snippets. Full deliverables are in the shared drive link Logan posted. Summary below and next steps:

Deliverables attached/in-drive:
- redwood_listing_evidence_checklist.xlsx (completed) — listing fields, marketing copy, approved logos
- aws_evidence_template.pdf — filled with SOC2 summary, data residency notes, and AWS-specific vendor ID
- support-matrix.csv — definitive SLA table (rows: Tier / Region / Response window / Escalation contact)

SLA blurb for listing (APAC & global):
"Redwood Inference provides 24x7 incident response and a 1-hour P1 response time for Enterprise/Dedicated customers worldwide. For self-serve customers, standard support is provided during business hours with optional 24x7 coverage via our Premium Support add-on."

APAC on-call: yes — we operate a staffed regional rotation for Enterprise customers (APAC hours). The contact route is via our pager system and the published escalation email (escalations@redwood.ai) — those details are included in support-matrix.csv.

Notes for CloudSuite intake team:
- AWS: vendor evidence included in aws_evidence_template.pdf. Please confirm if you need additional AWS security questionnaires.
- Azure/GCP: we included equivalent evidence pointers in the drive; Azure requires a copy of the service description which is in /drive/azure_evidence/service_description_v2.docx.
- Marketing copy: approved phrase for product listing is: "Redwood Inference — predictable, low-latency LLM inference across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments." Please use that exact phrasing.

Attachments sent: redwood_listing_evidence_checklist.xlsx (xlsx), aws_evidence_template.pdf (pdf), support-matrix.csv (csv).

If you confirm receipt and the APAC SLA wording, we can mark the intake complete and schedule co-sell enablement sync next week.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Marketplace Operations Lead | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

-----Original Message-----
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Sent: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 14:02:00 -0700
To: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

(Quoted for context)

- Branding asset pack (logos: PNG/SVG, approved marketing copy)
- Claims evidence (SOC2 summary, latency/cost blurb for product page)
- Support matrix (hours, timezone coverage, escalation contacts)
- Marketplace-specific evidence (AWS vendor form, Azure co-sell checklist, GCP listing confirmation)

Questions from the marketplace ops side:
1) Do you commit to 24x7 first-response for Production-level customers, or is support tiered by plan?
2) For APAC customers, do you have an on-call rotation that covers APAC hours or do you rely on automatic routing to a US team?

Timeline: our intake sprint closes next Thursday (2026-07-15). If we can get the files by EOD 2026-07-09 we can guarantee a spot in the next publication cycle.

I attached our intake template to this email for completeness (marketplace intake template v1).

Best,
Aisha Kumar
Partner Ops Manager | CloudSuite Inc.
Aisha.k@cloudsuite.com

Attachment: marketplace_intake_template_v1.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 7 Jul 2026 14:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Ben (sales) and Marissa (marketplace ops) to own the artifacts and evidence. Quick answers to your questions up front:

- Support model: Redwood offers tiered support. For Dedicated/Enterprise customers (what we expect most marketplace listings to point to) we provide 24x7 incident response with a documented SLA. For self-serve hosted API customers we provide business-hours support with paid 24x7 add-on.
- APAC coverage: we operate a primary APAC on-call rotation (Asia-Pacific hours) for high-tier customers; routing for lower tiers goes to a US-based follow-the-sun queue.

Marissa & Ben — can you confirm the exact SLA wording we should publish on the listing and attach the evidence files? I started a shared drive folder here: https://drive.google.com/redwood/onboarding-playbook — please drop final files there.

Requested items from our side:
- redwood_listing_evidence_checklist.xlsx (attached)
- aws_evidence_template.pdf (AWS vendor evidence filled where relevant)
- support-matrix.csv (shows coverage by tier and region)

Proposed timeline: provide artifacts by EOD Friday (2026-07-09) so Aisha can intake next week. If any of you foresee delays, call out the blocker and propose a two-day alternate.

Thanks —
Logan Wright
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

> On Tue, Jul 7, 2026 at 10:15 AM Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com> wrote:
> > Thanks again for the intro last week — excited to get Redwood into the CloudSuite Marketplace. To keep the listing and co-sell timeline tight, our listing team needs a short package of artifacts and a clarified support coverage statement. Can you confirm the items below and share the files where possible?


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 8 Jul 2026 09:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Aisha / Logan — quick note: we should confirm co-sell eligibility window on the marketplace side (any co-sell badge requirements from CloudSuite?) and whether this listing can be flagged for co-sell outreach once live. From a go-to-market perspective we want the listing to include the co-sell CTA and a sales contact with HubSpot deal id HBSP-48392.

APAC support follow-up: can Marissa confirm that for Enterprise customers we have APAC on-call (yes/no)? If yes, please paste the exact SLA blurb we can include on page copy. If no, we should propose a managed 24x7 add-on.

Also, if anyone sees missing evidence items (privacy/data location notes, encryption at rest certs) call it out now.

Thx,
Ben Carter
Channel Sales Lead | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

> On Tue, Jul 7, 2026 at 2:02 PM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Ben (sales) and Marissa (marketplace ops) to own the artifacts and evidence. Quick answers to your questions up front:


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kumar <aisha.k@cloudsuite.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Jul 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood listing onboarding — initial evidence & support coverage ask

Thanks, team — attaching the completed checklist and the region-specific evidence snippets. Full deliverables are in the shared drive link Logan posted. Summary below and next steps:

Deliverables attached/in-drive:
- redwood_listing_evidence_checklist.xlsx (completed) — listing fields, marketing copy, approved logos
- aws_evidence_template.pdf — filled with SOC2 summary, data residency notes, and AWS-specific vendor ID
- support-matrix.csv — definitive SLA table (rows: Tier / Region / Response window / Escalation contact)

SLA blurb for listing (APAC & global):
"Redwood Inference provides 24x7 incident response and a 1-hour P1 response time for Enterprise/Dedicated customers worldwide. For self-serve customers, standard support is provided during business hours with optional 24x7 coverage via our Premium Support add-on."

APAC on-call: yes — we operate a staffed regional rotation for Enterprise customers (APAC hours). The contact route is via our pager system and the published escalation email (escalations@redwood.ai) — those details are included in support-matrix.csv.

Notes for CloudSuite intake team:
- AWS: vendor evidence included in aws_evidence_template.pdf. Please confirm if you need additional AWS security questionnaires.
- Azure/GCP: we included equivalent evidence pointers in the drive; Azure requires a copy of the service description which is in /drive/azure_evidence/service_description_v2.docx.
- Marketing copy: approved phrase for product listing is: "Redwood Inference — predictable, low-latency LLM inference across hosted, dedicated, and private deployments." Please use that exact phrasing.

Attachments sent: redwood_listing_evidence_checklist.xlsx (xlsx), aws_evidence_template.pdf (pdf), support-matrix.csv (csv).

If you confirm receipt and the APAC SLA wording, we can mark the intake complete and schedule co-sell enablement sync next week.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Marketplace Operations Lead | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

From: Emily Zhao <emily@vanguardcloud.com>

Subject: VanguardCloud onboarding docs - COI and W9 attached

Hi Logan / Neha —

Attached are our current onboarding docs to get the PO and billing set up. Files: VanguardCloud_Certificate_of_Insurance.pdf and VanguardCloud_W9.pdf. A couple of notes from our side:

- The COI lists the general liability limits and the insurer contact for verification, but it currently does not include Redwood Inference as an Additional Insured.
- We can request an endorsement from our broker; it typically takes 1–2 business days.
- Billing remit: VanguardCloud, 412 Cypress Ave, Suite 2, San Mateo, CA 94401.

If you can confirm the exact endorsement wording and any additional line items you need on the COI (e.g., waiver of subrogation, policy period snapshot), we’ll ask the broker to issue the endorsement and resend.

Also attaching W9 for PO creation.

Thanks,
Emily Zhao
Head of Partnerships
VanguardCloud
emily@vanguardcloud.com
(415) 555-0172

Attachments: VanguardCloud_Certificate_of_Insurance.pdf (pdf), VanguardCloud_W9.pdf (pdf)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Emily Zhao <emily@vanguardcloud.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: VanguardCloud onboarding docs - COI and W9 attached

Thanks Emily — quick clarifying items so we can avoid back-and-forth with procurement/risk:

1) Endorsement language: our legal/procurement requires an Additional Insured Endorsement (CG 20 10 or equivalent) naming “Redwood Inference, Inc., its officers, directors, employees and agents” as Additional Insured for ongoing operations.
2) Waiver of Subrogation: If available on your GL policy, please include waiver of subrogation in favor of Redwood Inference.
3) Policy dates: please confirm the current policy effective and expiration dates in the endorsement.
4) Subcontractors: please confirm whether subcontractors perform any covered services and if so provide a subcontractor list (if none, just state none).

Neha / Ben — once Vanguard sends the endorsement PDF that names Redwood Inference, procurement will get the PO going. Ben, can you confirm any additional billing fields we need on our side so Emily can pass them to finance?

Emily — thanks for the W9, that is helpful. Once we have endorsement + confirmed policy dates we should be able to move quickly.

—Logan

> (see attached)

Date: Wed, 01 Jul 2026 11:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: VanguardCloud onboarding docs - COI and W9 attached

Emily — appreciate the quick turnaround on the initial docs. For PO and invoice processing we’ll need:

- A completed W9 (we have the one you sent, good).
- Endorsement PDF that explicitly names Redwood Inference, Inc. as Additional Insured (as Logan noted).
- Remit-to details to be present on the invoice (company name, address, and ACH or wire instructions if you want electronic payment).

Timing: Once the endorsement is received and confirms policy dates, we can issue a conditional PO number within 24–72 business hours. Final PO will be posted to the vendor portal: https://procurement.redwood.com/vendor-portal/VanguardCloud.

If you prefer, we can create a provisional PO (PO-2026-7893) to allow you to submit an invoice immediately; final payment will process after paperwork verification. Let me know if you want the provisional PO.

Senior Procurement Specialist
Redwood Inference

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanguard Insurance Services <insure@vanguardins.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Jul 2026 09:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: VanguardCloud onboarding docs - COI and W9 attached

Hi Ben / Logan —

Broker has issued the Additional Insured endorsement and I’ve attached VanguardCloud_Endorsement_AI.pdf to this thread along with an insurer contact card. Summary:

- Endorsement: Names “Redwood Inference, Inc., its officers, directors, employees and agents” as Additional Insured (CG 20 10 equivalent).
- Waiver of subrogation: Not available on current policy; insurer declined to include.
- Policy period: 2026-05-01 to 2027-05-01 — shown on endorsement and COI.
- Subcontractors: None.

If the endorsement PDF looks good on your end, please issue the provisional PO so our finance team can start invoicing. If you need the insurer to reissue with waiver language we can attempt, but timeline is uncertain.

Attachments: VanguardCloud_Endorsement_AI.pdf (pdf), VanguardCloud_Insurer_Contact.vcf (vcf)

Best,
Emily
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Emily Zhao <emily@vanguardcloud.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 02 Jul 2026 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: VanguardCloud onboarding docs - COI and W9 attached

Emily — thank you for the endorsement and insurer contact. Quick note: the endorsement PDF currently lists our legal name as “Redwood Inference LLC.” For our records and to close risk review we need the named Additional Insured to be exactly “Redwood Inference, Inc.” (comma and Inc.). Can you ask the broker to issue a corrected endorsement with that exact naming?

If the broker can’t reissue within 24 hours, Ben will proceed to create the provisional PO (PO-2026-7893) and we’ll accept invoices, but final PO issuance will be subject to receipt of the corrected endorsement.

Thanks for moving quickly on this — once the small naming fix is in place we’ll mark the vendor as active in the portal.

—Neha
Head of Vendor Risk & Compliance
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Request: one-off pricing safeguard for LedgerCorp expansion
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@ledgercorp.com>, James O'Neill <james.oneill@ledgercorp.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-off pricing safeguard for LedgerCorp expansion

Marissa / Karthik — quick summary and ask. LedgerCorp (Elena / James) are progressing to executive review but have pushed back on standard packaging. Their asks: a 12-month committed Dedicated pool, extended retention (90 days) for KV cache for their ML pipelines, and an up-front enterprise discount to hit procurement thresholds. Commercial is proposing a one-time "pricing safeguard": 35% off list for Year 1 in exchange for a 12-month commit + accelerated invoice (net 30) and a joint case study.

Why this is sensitive: the committed GPU capacity combined with retention increases unit cost materially; this is outside our standard packaging and discount bands. They want confirmation today for their CFO.

Attachments: LedgerCorp_MutualActionPlan_v2.pdf, LedgerCorp_SOW-draft.docx (draft SOW includes milestones / acceptance).

Ask: can we get VP/CFO sign-off for a one-off pricing safeguard (35% off for 12 months) or alternatively approve a 25% capped discount + milestone-based credits? If we need to split approvals (commercial vs finance vs legal), please advise the minimal signoff path.

I copied Elena/James so they see we're triaging. If Marcus needs to be looped in directly I can forward.

Thanks — Logan
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-off pricing safeguard for LedgerCorp expansion

Thanks Logan. Reading the MAP and SOW — this is borderline for our standard discount policy. We can consider a one-time concession, but this requires Marcus (VP Finance) sign-off plus legal redlines on retention.

Immediate suggestions:
- If we must move fast, propose a 30% discount instead of 35% and add a performance milestone (1st invoice reduction contingent on passing security onboarding).
- Require procurement to sign the MAP with acceptance criteria tied to usage thresholds.

I'll prep a short memo for Marcus outlining the unit economics impact and proposed guardrails. Karthik, please add any risk mitigations (SLA credits, fallback model variants) we should include.

Marissa
Director, Commercial Operations
Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 26 Jun 2026 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-off pricing safeguard for LedgerCorp expansion

Logan / Marissa — quick technical / commercial note from my side: the 90-day retention increases KV cache storage and will raise our average cost-per-token for the customer by ~22% assuming their expected usage profile. I agree with Marissa — recommend the following compromise to present to Marcus and client:

source.subject: Request: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review
messages:
From: Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: compliance@pinebrookhealth.com
Date: 2026-06-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Ben —

Thanks for taking this. Pinebrook is kicking off an external SOC 2 readiness review and our auditor has asked for a snapshot of 'admin activity' (console actions, privilege grants/revokes, role changes, API key lifecycle) covering 2025-10-01 through 2026-05-31. They want:

- A single export (or an attestable bundle) with time-ordered admin events
- Chain-of-custody proof (signed manifest, checksums) and timezone normalization notes
- Any redaction options for customer-identifiable fields and how sampling is performed

Primary constraints: we need delivery by 2026-06-25 and a quick handshake on transport (SFTP or pre-authenticated GCS link). Can you confirm what you can provide and what the expected file shapes are?

Lisa Murphy
Director, Compliance
Pinebrook Health
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: compliance@pinebrookhealth.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Hi Lisa —

Thanks — we can produce a time-ordered export and an attested manifest. Quick clarifying notes/questions to speed the handoff:

1) Scope: you listed console actions & API key lifecycle. Do you also want operator session metadata (login IPs, MFA outcomes) and workspace-level policy changes?

2) Redaction: we can redact email addresses and customer identifiers at export time, or provide full export and a redaction map. Which does the auditor prefer?

3) Transport & signing: we typically deliver via SFTP (mutual key) or an encrypted GCS/S3 presigned object, plus a signed CSV manifest that includes file checksums (sha256) and an HMAC over the manifest for attestation. We can also timestamp and notarize the manifest if required.

4) Format: default export is newline-delimited JSON (ndjson) with a small summary CSV manifest. Can provide parquet on request.

I'll loop in Kimberly (security product) to confirm the export window and retention carve-outs; Logan will coordinate transfer ops.

Action: Kimberly, can you confirm redaction defaults and whether we need a sampled subset or full export for Pinebrook?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales (Security Liaison)
Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-22T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Lisa / Ben —

We can do a full export for the window requested. A few implementation details so the auditor can sign-off without follow-ups:

- Event set: admin actions include: role.create/role.update/role.delete, policy.attach/detach, api_key.create/revoke/rotate, session.login, session.logout, privilege.grant/privilege.revoke, console.command.exec, and system:config.change. Each record includes event_ts (UTC), actor_id (internal id), actor_display, target_resource, resource_type, change_summary, request_id, and trace_id.

- Timezones: we canonicalize to UTC in the export. We will include original tz offsets where available (e.g., originating IP geolocation) in the event metadata.

- Redaction approach: by default we PII-mask email addresses and IPs in the exported bundle and provide a sealed 'redaction_matrix.xlsx' describing applied transforms. If the auditor requires raw fields, we can produce an auditor-only bundle under a separate NDA/handshake.

- Sampling vs full: for SOC 2 auditors prefer full or deterministic slices. We'll provide the full export by default, but we can also include a deterministic sampling manifest if Pinebrook prefers a smaller subset for initial review.

- Checksums & attestation: we'll produce sha256 checksums per file + a signed manifest (RSA-2048) and an HMAC wrapping of the manifest per Pinebrook's shared secret.

Logan — please prepare SFTP credentials (or presigned GCS) and include sftp_handshake_instructions.txt. We'll attach the export_manifest_template.csv to show the schema.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Product Security Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-23T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Team / Lisa —

SFTP is ready. Options:

- Option A: Mutual-key SFTP (preferred). We provide host key fingerprint and you drop an upload user key. Transfer will land in /incoming/pinebrook/2026-06-25/ and we rotate keys after delivery.

- Option B: Encrypted presigned GCS link (24h TTL).

Attached: sftp_handshake_instructions.txt (stub) contains the fingerprint, preferred ciphers, and required ACLs.

Delivery plan:
- 2026-06-24 03:00 UTC: Export job run and verify manifest generation
- 2026-06-24 04:15 UTC: Upload to /incoming/pinebrook/2026-06-25/
- 2026-06-24 04:30 UTC: Post-transfer verification (checksum & signed manifest)

Marissa — can you schedule the export job and include the redaction_matrix.xlsx in the bundle? Also include a brief README that describes timezone normalization and field mapping for the auditor.

Logan Wright
Security Operations Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T15:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Lisa —

Confirming: we've scheduled the export and will deliver the following bundle to your SFTP path (mutual-key):

- pinebrook_admin_events_20251001_20260531.ndjson (gzipped) — full event set, UTC timestamps
- export_manifest_20260624.csv — per-file checksums (sha256) and row counts
- export_manifest_20260624_signed.txt — RSA2048 signature of the manifest
- redaction_matrix.xlsx — documentation of masked fields
- readme_timezones_and_mapping.txt — explanation of canonicalization and original tz fields

We will also provide a deterministic sample (first 1,000 events) as separate file for initial auditor review. If you need raw, unmasked fields, we'll proceed under an auditor-only NDA and ship a sealed bundle.

Estimated handoff: upload completes 2026-06-24T04:20:00Z and we'll email the signed manifest + verification steps to compliance@pinebrookhealth.com. If you prefer GCS instead of SFTP, tell us now and we'll switch the presigned flow.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Marissa Cole
Evidence & Audit Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: export_manifest_template.csv, redaction_matrix.xlsx, sftp_handshake_instructions.txt

4) Format: default export is newline-delimited JSON (ndjson) with a small summary CSV manifest. Can provide parquet on request.

I'll loop in Kimberly (security product) to confirm the export window and retention carve-outs; Logan will coordinate transfer ops.

Action: Kimberly, can you confirm redaction defaults and whether we need a sampled subset or full export for Pinebrook?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales (Security Liaison)
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-22T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Lisa / Ben —

We can do a full export for the window requested. A few implementation details so the auditor can sign-off without follow-ups:

- Event set: admin actions include: role.create/role.update/role.delete, policy.attach/detach, api_key.create/revoke/rotate, session.login, session.logout, privilege.grant/privilege.revoke, console.command.exec, and system:config.change. Each record includes event_ts (UTC), actor_id (internal id), actor_display, target_resource, resource_type, change_summary, request_id, and trace_id.

- Timezones: we canonicalize to UTC in the export. We will include original tz offsets where available (e.g., originating IP geolocation) in the event metadata.

- Redaction approach: by default we PII-mask email addresses and IPs in the exported bundle and provide a sealed 'redaction_matrix.xlsx' describing applied transforms. If the auditor requires raw fields, we can produce an auditor-only bundle under a separate NDA/handshake.

- Sampling vs full: for SOC 2 auditors prefer full or deterministic slices. We'll provide the full export by default, but we can also include a deterministic sampling manifest if Pinebrook prefers a smaller subset for initial review.

- Checksums & attestation: we'll produce sha256 checksums per file + a signed manifest (RSA-2048) and an HMAC wrapping of the manifest per Pinebrook's shared secret.

Logan — please prepare SFTP credentials (or presigned GCS) and include sftp_handshake_instructions.txt. We'll attach the export_manifest_template.csv to show the schema.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Product Security Manager
Redwood Inference

To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>

SFTP is ready. Options:

- Option A: Mutual-key SFTP (preferred). We provide host key fingerprint and you drop an upload user key. Transfer will land in /incoming/pinebrook/2026-06-25/ and we rotate keys after delivery.

- Option B: Encrypted presigned GCS link (24h TTL).

Attached: sftp_handshake_instructions.txt (stub) contains the fingerprint, preferred ciphers, and required ACLs.

Delivery plan:
- 2026-06-24 03:00 UTC: Export job run and verify manifest generation
- 2026-06-24 04:15 UTC: Upload to /incoming/pinebrook/2026-06-25/
- 2026-06-24 04:30 UTC: Post-transfer verification (checksum & signed manifest)

Marissa — can you schedule the export job and include the redaction_matrix.xlsx in the bundle? Also include a brief README that describes timezone normalization and field mapping for the auditor.

Logan Wright
Security Operations Engineer
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>

Confirming: we've scheduled the export and will deliver the following bundle to your SFTP path (mutual-key):

- pinebrook_admin_events_20251001_20260531.ndjson (gzipped) — full event set, UTC timestamps
- export_manifest_20260624.csv — per-file checksums (sha256) and row counts
- export_manifest_20260624_signed.txt — RSA2048 signature of the manifest

Estimated handoff: upload completes 2026-06-24T04:20:00Z and we'll email the signed manifest + verification steps to compliance@pinebrookhealth.com. If you prefer GCS instead of SFTP, tell us now and we'll switch the presigned flow.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Marissa Cole
Evidence & Audit Operations
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Coordinating architect deep-dive — sunrise/sunset overlap options
messages:
From: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: HorizonHealth Architects <architects@horizonhealth.io>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 08:20:00 -0500
Subject: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the intro last week. Our solutions and infra architects (CC'd) would like a technical deep-dive focused on your runtime's KV cache behavior and multi-region failover. We need about 75 minutes (35–40m demo + 30m Q&A + buffer). Attendees are in Boston (US/Eastern), Berlin (CET), and Singapore (SGT).

Can you propose a few windows that give at least a 60–75 minute overlap across the three sites? DST is a little messy for us this quarter so any guidance on safe windows would help. We can be flexible on the exact day next week (Jun 28–Jul 2).

Also — please share a short agenda and any pre-read we should circulate.

Best,
Tom
Tom Hargreaves
Director, Platform Engineering
HorizonHealth

---
Attachment: horizon-infra-topics.pdf (PDF stub)
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Logan / Marissa — heads up, HorizonHealth asking for a 75-min deep-dive covering KV cache + cross-region failover. Attendees: Boston (EDT), Berlin (CEST?), Singapore (SGT). They suggested dates Jun 28–Jul 2 but said DST is messy.

Quick notes:
- We should avoid US late-afternoon which is late-night in SG. Best overlap windows typically are: early US morning / late EU afternoon / evening SG, or late US evening / early EU morning / morning SG — both have tradeoffs for on-call.
- Proposal: provide two safe multi-day options and one compromise that sacrifices one region's convenience (we can rotate for future sessions).

Can you draft three concrete time slots (UTC) with conversion table and attach the agenda + a short pre-read? I can take the external send once we align.

Thanks — Ava

--
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: request-notes.txt (text stub)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 13:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Ava / Marissa — draft slots below (I included UTC and local conversions; attaching a quick CSV matrix). I tried to pick times that give >= 70 minutes overlap.

Option A (US-anchored morning):
- UTC 13:00–14:15 (Boston 09:00–10:15 EDT, Berlin 15:00–16:15 CEST, Singapore 21:00–22:15 SGT)
Pros: Boston morning, Berlin afternoon; con: Singapore evening.

Option B (EU-anchored afternoon):
- UTC 15:30–16:45 (Boston 11:30–12:45 EDT, Berlin 17:30–18:45 CEST, Singapore 23:30–00:45 SGT)
Pros: Berlin comfortable; con: Singapore late-night shortfall for some.

Option C (APAC-early compromise):
- UTC 04:30–05:45 (Boston 00:30–01:45 EDT, Berlin 06:30–07:45 CEST, Singapore 12:30–13:45 SGT)
Pros: Singapore midday; con: Boston overnight, Berlin early morning.

I recommend we lead with Option A (most friendly to US and EU, tolerable evening for SG if they can do it once). If they prefer rotating convenience we can propose a follow-up with Option C as the standard for future hands-on labs.

Attached: timezone-overlap-matrix.csv (rows: UTC, Boston, Berlin, Singapore) and a two-slide agenda PDF for the deep-dive.

--
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

Attachments: timezone-overlap-matrix.csv, redwood-deepdive-agenda.pdf
From: Tom Hargreaves <tom.h@horizonhealth.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: HorizonHealth Architects <architects@horizonhealth.io>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 10:45:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Thanks, Logan and Ava — this is really helpful. We reviewed the three options internally with the Berlin and Singapore architects. Short answers:
- Option A (UTC 13:00) looks best for this session — Berlin can make that in their afternoon; Boston is easy; Singapore is willing to join at 21:00 SGT for a one-off.
- Can we schedule for Tue Jun 29, UTC 13:00–14:15 (BST/CEST note: Berlin will be UTC+2)? Please mark calendar invite for 75 minutes and include the agenda + a link to a shared test cluster we can use for the KV cache demo.

Two small asks: 1) Can you add an abbreviated timezone note in the invite body (local times for attendees)? 2) One of our architects asked if you have a fallback window (Option C) in case someone in SG must step out; they'd prefer an AM Singapore fallback.

If you send a calendar invite, I'll confirm attendees and the test-cluster credentials request with our infra team.

Best,
Tom

--
Tom Hargreaves
Director, Platform Engineering
HorizonHealth

On Sun, 21 Jun 2026 at 10:12 -0700 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Logan / Marissa — heads up, HorizonHealth asking for a 75-min deep-dive covering KV cache + cross-region failover...

Cc: HorizonHealth Architects <architects@horizonhealth.io>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 10:45:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Architect deep-dive — multi-region availability

Thanks, Logan and Ava — this is really helpful. We reviewed the three options internally with the Berlin and Singapore architects. Short answers:
- Option A (UTC 13:00) looks best for this session — Berlin can make that in their afternoon; Boston is easy; Singapore is willing to join at 21:00 SGT for a one-off.
- Can we schedule for Tue Jun 29, UTC 13:00–14:15 (BST/CEST note: Berlin will be UTC+2)? Please mark calendar invite for 75 minutes and include the agenda + a link to a shared test cluster we can use for the KV cache demo.

Two small asks: 1) Can you add an abbreviated timezone note in the invite body (local times for attendees)? 2) One of our architects asked if you have a fallback window (Option C) in case someone in SG must step out; they'd prefer an AM Singapore fallback.

If you send a calendar invite, I'll confirm attendees and the test-cluster credentials request with our infra team.

Best,
Tom

--
Tom Hargreaves
Director, Platform Engineering
HorizonHealth

source.subject: Pricing sign-off needed — Acme Biotech discount request (impact to Q3 commit)
messages:
From: Samir Kahn <samir.k@acmebio.com>
To: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@acmebio.com
Date: 2026-06-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request for 25% pricing concession to close

Hi Elliot,

Thanks again for the demo last week — team is impressed with latency and the VPC options. We have one internal hurdle: our security team requires a signed PO this month to hit our internal budget window. To get the deal greenlit we need a one-time 25% discount on list pricing for the first 12 months.

Can Redwood confirm this concession? If not, what’s the maximum creative discount you can offer quickly? We have a target close date of 6/25.

Attached: Acme_Order_Requirements.pdf (procurement summary)

Best,
Samir
Samir Kahn
Head of Procurement, Acme Biotech

From: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Samir Kahn <samir.k@acmebio.com>
Date: 2026-06-18T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for 25% pricing concession to close

Hi team,

Looping in Deal Desk and Finance to evaluate Acme's 25% request and to confirm how this would be treated in our Q3 forecast. Quick context: this is an early-stage commercial account, expected to start as a single-product pilot with scope to expand. Samir, thanks for the procurement packet — that helped.

Questions for deal desk/finance:
- If we approve 25% (one-time for 12 months), does Finance allow treating this as a commit for Q3, or must we classify it as upside?
- If we push back to 20%, can we still treat it as committed revenue (vs upside)?
- Any precedence for one-off concession approvals that don't require VP-level sign-off?

I attached an internal quote (RBW_quote_v1.pdf) with two price ladders: list, 20% discount, and 25% discount. We need a decision before EOD 6/19 to keep Acme in the current buying window.

Next steps proposed: Rishi to advise approval path; Laura to confirm forecast treatment; if VP sign-off is required, propose an expedited review.

Thanks,
Elliot
Elliot Price
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Samir Kahn <samir.k@acmebio.com>
Date: 2026-06-18T11:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for 25% pricing concession to close

Elliot —

Short answer: 25% for this deal size needs VP commercial approval per our standard concession matrix. We can approve up to 20% at my level with the standard deal desk form completed. I’ve attached the dealdesk_approval_form.xlsx — please fill the expected ARR, ACV, margin impact, and legal constraints.

Operational/forecast note for Finance: approving 25% is material (~$85k ACV delta vs list) and under current policy we recommend treating anything >20% as "upside" until VP sign-off and PO receipt are in Handshake. 20% or less with PO and signed order form can be considered commitable in the quarter.

If we want to move faster: Elliot, offer 20% now to align with our commit policy and keep the conversation live. If Acme pushes back, we can route for VP review — but that will likely add 48–72 hours.

Attachment: dealdesk_approval_form.xlsx (completion required for >20%)

—Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
Head of Deal Desk, Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Samir Kahn <samir.k@acmebio.com>
Date: 2026-06-18T14:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for 25% pricing concession to close

Team —

I ran quick math on the Q3 commit impact. Assumptions: deal ACV at list = $340,000; 20% discount = $272,000; 25% discount = $255,000.

Forecast treatment recommendation:
- 25% discount: classify as "upside" pending VP sign-off + signed PO in hand. Do not count toward committed Q3 quota.
- 20% discount: acceptable to treat as committed if we receive signed PO and all procurement paperwork before quarter close (6/30). We need confirmation that procurement timeline supports PO arrival by 6/30.

If we mark this as upside at 25%, it reduces Q3 committed revenue by ~$85k relative to list. That gap will need to be offset by cover from other deals or reclassed pipeline.

If you want, Revenue Ops (Marissa) and Sales Ops (Logan) can run a micro-sensitivity in the forecast workbook to show coverage scenarios. I attached the quick adjustment CSV with the two scenarios.

Attachment: forecast_adj_0618.csv

Laura Bennett
Director, FP&A — Redwood Inference

From: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Kahn <samir.k@acmebio.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-19T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request for 25% pricing concession to close

Samir —

Thanks for the transparency. To keep the procurement window intact, we can offer a 20% one-time concession with the caveat that we need PO signed by 6/30 to treat as committed revenue for Q3. Rishi — please confirm you'll approve 20% at your level once the dealdesk form is back. Laura — please treat the 20% scenario as commitable contingent on PO; Marissa/Logan — please surface any operational blockers for a 6/30 PO.

If Acme absolutely requires 25% we will escalate to VP for a final decision; I’ll prepare the deal documentation in parallel so we don’t lose momentum.

Elliot
Elliot Price
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachment: RBW_quote_v1.pdf (updated with 20% option)

source.subject: Post-call recap + pick a 30m demo slot — AuroraEdge
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@auroraedge.com>
Cc: Tom Reed <tom.reed@auroraedge.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap + pick a 30m demo slot — AuroraEdge

Hi Samira / Tom —

Great speaking with you both today. Quick recap from our discovery: AuroraEdge is primarily focused on driving down inference cost for your batch scoring pipeline and adding an infrequently-used realtime endpoint for the support chatbot. Key requirements we heard:
- Predictable p95 latency at 150–200ms for the realtime endpoint
- Cost per 1M tokens target below your current baseline (approx $85)
- Private deployment option for HIPAA data in the mid-term

Next step: a 30-minute product intro (high-level demo + brief infra fit) with our solutions engineer to validate architecture and rough cost numbers. Please pick one of the windows below that works for your team — I included times in PT since that’s what we used on the call. If none fit, tell us a couple of blockers and we’ll expand options.

Option A — Tue Jun 22, 11:00–11:30 AM PT
Option B — Tue Jun 22, 4:00–4:30 PM PT
Option C — Wed Jun 23, 9:30–10:00 AM PT (Marcus suggested this as a late-morning slot)

Who we should see on your side: product/PO (for use-case alignment) + infra/SRE (for deployment constraints). We’ve attached a one-page discovery summary and a short agenda.

Attachments: discovery-summary-AuroraEdge.pdf (1 page)

Thanks — I’ll hold the three slots tentatively for 48 hours. If you want, you can also book directly: https://calendly.com/redwood/30min-demo

—
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai | +1 (555) 213-4470
From: Samira Khan <samira@auroraedge.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Reed <tom.reed@auroraedge.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + pick a 30m demo slot — AuroraEdge

Logan — thanks for the recap and the clear options. Tom and I are available for Option B (Tue Jun 22, 4:00 PM PT). Quick notes:
- Please include an infra/SRE-focused person — we’ll have Priyanka Shah (our SRE lead) join if the time works.
- Can you confirm the demo will touch on private deployment constraints (networking/KMS) even briefly? That was one of the deciding factors for our architecture committee.

I’m looping Priyanka once I hear which option is locked so she can calendar it. If Marcus has a preference for the later or earlier slot for his prep, let us know.

On Tue Jun 22 at 4:00 PM PT works best for us.

Thanks,
Samira
Director of Product — AuroraEdge
samira@auroraedge.com | +1 (415) 555-9200

Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:51:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + pick a 30m demo slot — AuroraEdge

Thanks Samira — glad Option B looks promising. Marcus (cc’d) will lead the demo and cover the infra/playbook slice. Marcus, can you confirm you’re OK with Tue Jun 22 at 4:00 PM PT? If you prefer a slightly earlier slot, I can push for Option A or propose Wed morning.

Priyanka / AuroraEdge team — if it helps with calendar coordination, here’s a quick poll link (two preferred + one flex): https://calendly.com/redwood/30min-demo — we’ll reserve the slot once someone books or replies here.

Agenda (30m):
- 0–5m: quick intros & goals
- 5–15m: live walkthrough (realtime + batch flows)
- 15–25m: infra fit — networking, KMS, SLOs
- 25–30m: next steps & follow-ups (price/pilot options)

Also attaching a short technical one-pager Marcus will reference during the demo.

—
Marissa Cole
Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

source.subject: Portal access: MSA terms mismatch and SLA exception - HealthCore onboarding
messages:
From: Susan Barlow <susan.barlow@healthcore.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Ops <procureops@healthcore.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: HealthCore onboarding — portal registration + MSA redline

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We're ready to get HealthCore into your procurement portal (Ariba) but we've run into two blockers on our side that I wanted to raise early: 

1) The MSA we returned has a handful of redlines (attached) where our legal team requires a 30-day remedy window for outages and a more prescriptive data-handling section. Your order form in the portal currently references Redwood's standard SLA and data appendix which do not line up with our redlines. Can you confirm how you handle MSA redline exceptions when registration is initiated through Ariba?

2) Ariba mapping: our Proc Ops team is seeing that Redwood's portal expects a PO acknowledgement flag (PO_ACK_REQUIRED) and an invoice remit field that doesn't map cleanly from our PO feed. If that field is required to progress activation, we'll likely need a temporary exception or a field-mapping workaround.

I've looped in our procurement mailbox (procureops@healthcore.com) for any necessary file transfers. Attached: HealthCore_MSA_redline_v3.pdf and a short checklist of required fields.

Can you advise next steps and whether we should pause registration until legal signs off, or proceed with a provisioning hold while legal finishes review?

Best,
Susan Barlow
Sr. Procurement Manager
HealthCore Systems
Attachments: HealthCore_MSA_redline_v3.pdf, Ariba_registration_checklist.xlsx

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Susan Barlow <susan.barlow@healthcore.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HealthCore onboarding — portal registration + MSA redline

Susan — thanks for the heads up and for sending the redlines. Appreciate the clarity.

Quick summary of how Redwood typically handles this: for portal-driven onboarding we can do one of two flows — (A) gate activation until legal executes an MSA (preferred for enterprise customers with non-standard terms), or (B) allow registration to proceed but hold provisioning (no model keys issued) until countersignature. We can also accept temporary SLA exceptions on a per-deal basis if approved by legal and the contracts team.

Action items I'm proposing:
- I attached the redline you sent and flagged the clauses that conflict with our standard portal order form.
- Kimberly, can you confirm whether the 30-day remedy window is something we can accept (and if so, any suggested language)?
- Marissa, can ops stand up a temporary mapping that writes HealthCore's PO_ACK to our PO_ACK_REQUIRED flag so registration doesn't fail? If not, what's the minimum gating field set?
- Mateo, help coordinate the HubSpot/DocuSign record so we can track the legal exception approval.

If we want to move faster, we can provision a staging account (limited throughput) while legal runs the redline. That keeps testing live but prevents production invoicing.

On Mon, Jun 15, 2026 at 09:12 AM Susan Barlow <susan.barlow@healthcore.com> wrote:
> Hi Logan,
> 
> Thanks again for the demo last week. We're ready to get HealthCore into your procurement portal (Ariba) but we've run into two blockers on our side that I wanted to raise early: 
> 
> 1) The MSA we returned has a handful of redlines (attached) where our legal team requires a 30-day remedy window for outages and a more prescriptive data-handling section. Your order form in the portal currently references Redwood's standard SLA and data appendix which do not line up with our redlines. Can you confirm how you handle MSA redline exceptions when registration is initiated through Ariba?
> 
> 2) Ariba mapping: our Proc Ops team is seeing that Redwood's portal expects a PO acknowledgement flag (PO_ACK_REQUIRED) and an invoice remit field that doesn't map cleanly from our PO feed. If that field is required to progress activation, we'll likely need a temporary exception or a field-mapping workaround.
> 
> I've looped in our procurement mailbox (procureops@healthcore.com) for any necessary file transfers. Attached: HealthCore_MSA_redline_v3.pdf and a short checklist of required fields.
> 
> Can you advise next steps and whether we should pause registration until legal signs off, or proceed with a provisioning hold while legal finishes review?
> 
> Best,
> Susan

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Susan Barlow <susan.barlow@healthcore.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 08:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HealthCore onboarding — portal registration + MSA redline

Hi all — I reviewed HealthCore_MSA_redline_v3.pdf. Short answer: a 30-day remedy window is negotiable but we need specific outage definitions and an exceptions approval from Legal Ops (we try to avoid open-ended remedy periods). Suggested approach: adopt HealthCore's remedy window but tie it to a defined SLA table and a capped remedy liability (I recommend language below).

Suggested redline language (for the order form appendix):
"If an outage is defined as a complete inability for Customer to access Redwood's hosted inference endpoints for longer than the Service Availability threshold specified in the SLA, Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to remedy the outage within thirty (30) days. Customer's sole and exclusive remedy shall be the pro rata service credit set forth in the SLA; liability shall not exceed the fees paid in the prior twelve (12) months."

If that is acceptable to HealthCore, Legal Ops can sign off and we can insert an exception record into the portal metadata so Ariba mapping does not block registration.

Logan — please create the HubSpot ticket and attach the signed exception when it is available. Marissa — ops will need the exact field name to map; I can mark this as an approved exception once countersign is complete.

On Mon, Jun 15, 2026 at 11:03 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Susan — thanks for the heads up and for sending the redlines. Appreciate the clarity.
> 
> Quick summary of how Redwood typically handles this: for portal-driven onboarding we can do one of two flows — (A) gate activation until legal executes an MSA (preferred for enterprise customers with non-standard terms), or (B) allow registration to proceed but hold provisioning (no model keys issued) until countersignature. We can also accept temporary SLA exceptions on a per-deal basis if approved by legal and the contracts team.
> 
> Action items I'm proposing:
> - I attached the redline you sent and flagged the clauses that conflict with our standard portal order form.
> - Kimberly, can you confirm whether the 30-day remedy window is something we can accept (and if so, any suggested language)?
> - Marissa, can ops stand up a temporary mapping that writes HealthCore's PO_ACK to our PO_ACK_REQUIRED flag so registration doesn't fail? If not, what's the minimum gating field set?
> - Mateo, help coordinate the HubSpot/DocuSign record so we can track the legal exception approval.
> 
> If we want to move faster, we can provision a staging account (limited throughput) while legal runs the redline. That keeps testing live but prevents production invoicing.

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Susan Barlow <susan.barlow@healthcore.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: HealthCore onboarding — portal registration + MSA redline

Susan / Logan — looping back with ops next steps and an escalation path so we don't block the deal.

1) Temporary provisioning: We can provision a staging account today (no billing, limited token throughput). That will allow HealthCore to validate integrations and test EDI without live invoicing. ETA: 24 hours after we receive a signed acceptance of the exception language (Kimberly's suggested clause).

2) Ariba mapping workaround: our engineering team can add a one-off mapping that populates Redwood_PO_ACK_REQUIRED from HealthCore's PO_ACK field. This is a config change in the gateway and does not require code release. I'll push the change to staging once we confirm the exact Ariba field key. Action: ProcureOps (procureops@healthcore.com) please share a sample PO payload (XML).

3) Exception approval & DocuSign: Kimberly, please confirm final sign-off language and we will attach the DocuSign envelope to the HubSpot company record (HubSpot URL above). Once attached, Ops will flip the portal exception flag and finish registration.

Escalation if legal sign-off delayed >72 hours: I will escalate to VP of Revenue Ops (cc'd internally) to request a temporary activation waiver for a test window (30 days).

On Tue, Jun 16, 2026 at 08:27 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi all — I reviewed HealthCore_MSA_redline_v3.pdf. Short answer: a 30-day remedy window is negotiable but we need specific outage definitions and an exceptions approval from Legal Ops (we try to avoid open-ended remedy periods). Suggested approach: adopt HealthCore's remedy window but tie it to a defined SLA table and a capped remedy liability (I recommend language below).
> 
> Suggested redline language (for the order form appendix):
> "If an outage is defined as a complete inability for Customer to access Redwood's hosted inference endpoints for longer than the Service Availability threshold specified in the SLA, Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to remedy the outage within thirty (30) days. Customer's sole and exclusive remedy shall be the pro rata service credit set forth in the SLA; liability shall not exceed the fees paid in the prior twelve (12) months."
> 
> If that is acceptable to HealthCore, Legal Ops can sign off and we can insert an exception record into the portal metadata so Ariba mapping does not block registration.
> 
> Logan — please create the HubSpot ticket and attach the signed exception when it is available. Marissa — ops will need the exact field name to map; I can mark this as an approved exception once countersign is complete.
> 
Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Senior Onboarding Manager, Redwood Inference
Attachments: portal_screenshot.png, Ariba_registration_checklist.xlsx

source.subject: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Anna Gomez <anna@finora.ai>, contracts@finora.ai
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)

Hi all,

Finora is ready to move forward on the 3-year prepay (RDW-3241) but they have a fiscal-year constraint that requires the total prepay to be invoiced across three separate fiscal years. Summary of commercial terms we agreed in principle:
- Total committed prepay: $1,500,000 over 36 months (3 years).
- Term discount: 18% applied to list across the term (commercial ask).
- Customer invoicing preference: Year 1 (FY26) invoice $600,000 on July 1, 2026; Year 2 (FY27) invoice $450,000 on Jan 15, 2027; Year 3 (FY28) invoice $450,000 on Jan 15, 2028.
- Usage ramp expectation: 30% of steady-state in first quarter, to hit full consumption by Q3 of year 1.
- PO: PO-7782 (Anna confirmed).

Ask: Marissa / Marcus — can you confirm how we should book these split invoices from a revenue recognition and ledger perspective? Specifically:
1) When cash hits in July (600k) do we record the full amount as deferred revenue and amortize monthly over 36 months, or do we need to treat the applied term discount differently (e.g., contra-revenue amortized)?
2) Are there any issues with recognizing revenue that spans fiscal years when the invoices themselves fall into different fiscal years (we expect recognition monthly, but customer needs the invoices split for budget reasons)?
3) GL/AR labeling needed on the invoice so Finora's AP team will route correctly (they asked for a single-line invoice referencing PO-7782 and SOW RDW-3241).

I attached the SOW and the pricing schedule the customer reviewed. Neha — can you confirm the final discounted invoice amounts for each date above (we’ll need the exact invoice lines for finance)?

Thanks — I want to have a clear finance ops plan before we route the SOW for signature.

—Logan
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Anna Gomez <anna@finora.ai>, contracts@finora.ai
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 13:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)

Thanks Logan — great summary. Short answers up front: we can support split invoicing and still recognize revenue monthly over the service period, but we need to be explicit on discount treatment and invoice lines. Notes / requests below: 

1) Booking approach (recommended):
- When payment is received we book a Deferred Revenue liability for the cash amount (full cash received).
- Revenue should be recognized ratably over the service period (monthly) unless there's a distinct performance obligation that dictates otherwise. For this subscription/license prepay, ratable recognition is appropriate.
- Term discount: treat as an upfront contract discount. Recommended accounting treatment is to record the discount as a reduction to transaction price and recognize revenue net of the discount over the term (i.e., amortize the discount as a contra-revenue effect across the same monthly schedule).

2) Split invoices across fiscal years:
- No issue from a rev-rec perspective; recognition follows the service period, not the invoice date. However, the timing of cash receipts will impact cash/AR and deferred revenue balances by fiscal year. We should note the FY26 cash inflow (600k) separately for forecasting.

3) GL/AR / invoice line guidance:
- Invoice to contain a single invoice line: "RDW Platform Subscription — 36 months — SOW RDW-3241 — PO-7782" with the total invoice amount and a separate line in our AR system for the applied term discount (description: "Term discount — 18% — RDW-3241").
- Suggested GL mappings: Cash -> 1000; Deferred Revenue -> 2200; Subscription Revenue -> 4000; Contra-Revenue/Discount -> 4105. I attached a brief rev-rec mapping file with codes.

Open items I need from sales to finalize the invoices and recognition schedule:
- Confirm the exact invoice amounts (post-discount) for the three invoice dates.
- Confirm official contract start date (SOW lists 2026-07-01 as start; please confirm).
- Any jurisdiction / withholding / tax notes (Finora is US-based but confirm if any state-level withholding applies).

If we get the amounts and signed SOW today I can prepare invoice drafts and the deferred revenue schedule for the close.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Manager, Revenue Accounting
Redwood Inference

Attachment: pricing_schedule_Finora_RDW-3241.xlsx (pricing tab, discount calculations)

From: Anna Gomez <anna@finora.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, contracts@finora.ai
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)

Hi Logan / Marissa — thanks. Confirming Finora's preference:
- Invoice schedule ok as proposed. Please issue invoices as follows:
  • 2026-07-01: $600,000 (reference PO-7782)
  • 2027-01-15: $450,000 (reference PO-7782)
  • 2028-01-15: $450,000 (reference PO-7782)

We accept the 18% term discount reflected in the pricing sheet (Anna — I copied contracts to confirm any legal wording needed in the SOW). Please issue the July invoice as a one-line invoice referencing the SOW and PO. We will route for payment within our standard 30-day terms.

—Anna
Anna Gomez
Head of Platform Partnerships
Finora.ai

On Mon, Jun 15, 2026 at 09:12 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi all,
> 
> Finora is ready to move forward on the 3-year prepay (RDW-3241) but they have a fiscal-year constraint that requires the total prepay to be invoiced across three separate fiscal years. Summary of commercial terms we agreed in principle:...

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Anna Gomez <anna@finora.ai>, contracts@finora.ai
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)

Thanks Marissa / Anna. Quick commercial confirm: the $1,500,000 list becomes $1,230,000 post 18% discount across the term. Proposed invoice amounts (post-discount) we intend to send:
- 2026-07-01: $492,000
- 2027-01-15: $369,000
- 2028-01-15: $369,000

Rationale: we applied the 18% term discount pro rata to each invoice amount rather than front-loading it to year 1. The customer didn't require the discount front-loaded but if finance prefers a different presentation we can adjust.

Also: please confirm whether the discount should be displayed as a separate line on the invoice (per Marissa's suggestion) or embedded into the single-line amount (Finora prefers a single-line invoice; contracts can confirm).

One more ask: Marcus — can Revenue Ops update the subscription id and schedule in our billing system once Marissa confirms the GL mapping? I will route the signed SOW after contracts confirm wording.

-Neha
Neha Kapoor
Sr. Account Executive
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Anna Gomez <anna@finora.ai>, contracts@finora.ai
Date: Wed, 17 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)

All — good thread. Operational notes from RevOps/Revenue Recognition side: 

1) Agreed approach: book Deferred Revenue on cash receipt (GL 2200) and amortize revenue monthly over 36 months (GL 4000). Discount should be recognized as contra-revenue (GL 4105) amortized on the same monthly schedule. This preserves correct ARR and revenue run-rate metrics.

2) Invoice presentation: because Finora strongly prefers a single-line invoice, we'll issue a single line but include an invoice memo showing the discount calculation (for internal AR mapping). Finance will ingest the memo and post the contra-revenue mapping in the ERP.

3) Billing system steps (RevOps action items):
- Create subscription id SUB-RDW-3241-FINORA with start date 2026-07-01.
- Enter invoicing schedule and attach PO-7782 and SOW.
- Upload pricing_schedule_Finora_RDW-3241.xlsx to the billing record.
- Add revrec mapping: revrec_mapping_RDW_GL.txt (attached).

4) Reporting impact: FY26 cash +600k will show as increased cash and deferred revenue; recognized revenue in FY26 will be the proportion of months in FY26 (Jul–Dec) that relate to the 36-month service period. We will include a waterfall in the close pack for the controller.

Next steps: Marissa to prepare invoice drafts and deferred revenue amortization schedule once she receives the signed SOW and your confirmation on invoice display (single-line + memo vs explicit discount line). Neha — please route signed SOW to contracts@redwood.com and cc finance.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: revrec_mapping_RDW_GL.txt
--- End of thread ---

1) When cash hits in July (600k) do we record the full amount as deferred revenue and amortize monthly over 36 months, or do we need to treat the applied term discount differently (e.g., contra-revenue amortized)?
2) Are there any issues with recognizing revenue that spans fiscal years when the invoices themselves fall into different fiscal years (we expect recognition monthly, but customer needs the invoices split for budget reasons)?
3) GL/AR labeling needed on the invoice so Finora's AP team will route correctly (they asked for a single-line invoice referencing PO-7782 and SOW RDW-3241).

I attached the SOW and the pricing schedule the customer reviewed. Neha — can you confirm the final discounted invoice amounts for each date above (we’ll need the exact invoice lines for finance)?

Thanks — I want to have a clear finance ops plan before we route the SOW for signature.

Subject: Re: Prepay invoicing split + ramp / rev-rec guidance for Finora (RDW-3241)

Thanks Logan — great summary. Short answers up front: we can support split invoicing and still recognize revenue monthly over the service period, but we need to be explicit on discount treatment and invoice lines. Notes / requests below: 

1) Booking approach (recommended):
- When payment is received we book a Deferred Revenue liability for the cash amount (full cash received).
- Revenue should be recognized ratably over the service period (monthly) unless there's a distinct performance obligation that dictates otherwise. For this subscription/license prepay, ratable recognition is appropriate.
- Term discount: treat as an upfront contract discount. Recommended accounting treatment is to record the discount as a reduction to transaction price and recognize revenue net of the discount over the term (i.e., amortize the discount as a contra-revenue effect across the same monthly schedule).

2) Split invoices across fiscal years:
- No issue from a rev-rec perspective; recognition follows the service period, not the invoice date. However, the timing of cash receipts will impact cash/AR and deferred revenue balances by fiscal year. We should note the FY26 cash inflow (600k) separately for forecasting.

3) GL/AR / invoice line guidance:
- Invoice to contain a single invoice line: "RDW Platform Subscription — 36 months — SOW RDW-3241 — PO-7782" with the total invoice amount and a separate line in our AR system for the applied term discount (description: "Term discount — 18% — RDW-3241").
- Suggested GL mappings: Cash -> 1000; Deferred Revenue -> 2200; Subscription Revenue -> 4000; Contra-Revenue/Discount -> 4105. I attached a brief rev-rec mapping file with codes.

- Confirm official contract start date (SOW lists 2026-07-01 as start; please confirm).
