source.subject: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>; IT-Security@medsecure.com
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Thanks again for the time earlier, Daniel — quick recap + proposed next steps from our end.

Call recap
- Goal: scope a Private / VPC or air-gapped POC for MedSecure clinical ingestion pipeline.
- Primary compliance drivers: HIPAA (PHI in scope), GDPR (EU patient data residency) and SOC 2 evidence for vendor onboarding.
- Preferred topology: strongly leaning toward network-isolated VPC with egress control; air-gapped option discussed if regulatory requires no outbound connectivity.

What were attaching (initial evidence bundle)
- redwood-soc2-summary-2026.pdf: short SOC 2 Type II coverage and auditor summary.
- vendor-security-questionnaire-template.xlsx: our questionnaire we typically send to customers to accelerate answers; we can also ingest theirs.

Immediate asks for MedSecure
1) Can you share current SOC / compliance posture: SOC 2 Type II (if available) or expected timeline?
2) Confirm GDPR residency requirements (which EU region): do you require dedicated region + data residency?
3) HIPAA specifics to call out: covered entity vs business associate, any BAA draft you prefer?
4) Please upload your current network/infra diagram (VPC, proxies, egress controls) so Soojin can review isolation constraints — we referenced a couple of patterns on the call.

Proposed next steps
- Daniel: send the network diagram and any procurement questionnaire you already have (we can fill).
- Redwood: Soojin will run a short technical qualification and propose one architecture sketch (VPC + egress-proxy) and an air-gapped variant.
- Redwood: Marissa will own the vendor questionnaire coordination and follow up on mapping evidence.

Potential meeting: we can do a 60-minute architecture workshop Wed 6/17 or Thu 6/18 (PST). What works?

Thanks — Ill follow up with a calendar invite when you confirm.

—
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: redwood-soc2-summary-2026.pdf (pdf), vendor-security-questionnaire-template.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: IT-Security@medsecure.com
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 16:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Thanks Logan — good recap. A few quick notes:

- SOC2: we currently have a SOC 2 Type II in progress with an expected report in Q3 2026; we do have interim evidence (policies, SCIM audit logs, vulnerability scan reports) we can share now.
- GDPR: EU residency required for a subset of patients in Frankfurt (eu-central-1). We need assurance that data for those patients never leaves the region.
- HIPAA: MedSecure is a covered entity; Redwood would be a BA. We have a standard BAA template well attach.

Ill upload our high-level network diagram and the procurement questionnaire we use to the Drive link you provided by EOD tomorrow. The diagram will show our on-prem ingest, a DMZ, and an outbound proxy that restricts egress.

A couple of clarifying Qs for you:
1) For the air-gapped option — when you say "air-gapped" are you proposing totally isolated compute with manual data ingress/egress (sneakernet/managed transfer), or a walled VPC with egress-only via a controlled proxy? Were leaning toward the latter but want to understand tradeoffs.
2) Encryption at rest: do you support customer-managed keys (BYOK) in private deployments? If so, how is key rotation handled operationally?

Ill attach the BAA and our diagram once uploaded.

Best,
Daniel
Director, IT Security | MedSecure Health Systems
daniel.park@medsecure.com | +1 (206) 555-4444

Attachments pending: MedSecure-BAA-draft.docx, medsecure-network-diagram-v1.png
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Thanks — thats helpful context. Quick answers:

- Air-gapped options: we support both models.
  * Walled VPC (recommended first): network isolation, strict egress via an outbound proxy (TLS inspection or fixed IP egress), and optional NAT/proxy allowlist. Allows more rapid POC and incremental automation.
  * True air-gapped: fully isolated compute with manual data ingress/egress. Higher ops overhead and slower iteration, but sometimes required for regulated on-prem deployments.

- Customer-managed keys (BYOK): yes, in Private deployments we integrate with customer KMS (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, or on-prem HSM). Key rotation is orchestrated via our ops playbook and can be scheduled or triggered manually; we can provide the KMS integration doc in the architecture workshop.

Ive attached the vendor questionnaire again and a short sample controls-mapping doc that shows how Redwood maps SOC 2 controls to common evidence artifacts (link below). Soojin will review your diagram and produce two sketches: VPC-walled and an air-gapped variant.

Proposed meeting: Wed 6/17 10:00 AM PST — happy to host an architecture workshop (60m). Please confirm and well send invite.

—
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachments: vendor-security-questionnaire-template.xlsx (xlsx)
Related: https://redwood-inference.docs/samples/controls-mapping
From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 09:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Confirmed — Wed 6/17 10:00 AM PST works. I uploaded the diagram and our BAA draft to the Drive link (https://drive.google.com/medsecure/poc-uploads).

Note: our preference is the walled VPC with a managed egress proxy (we want to avoid full manual transfers where possible). Well expect details on how you handle outbound allowlists, certificate pinning, and any central logging/export of audit logs.

Marissa — Ill start filling the questionnaire where I can; I may ping you for clarifications.

Thanks,
Daniel

On Tue, Jun 16, 2026 at 08:10 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Thanks — that's helpful context. Quick answers:
> 
> - Air-gapped options: we support both models.
>   * Walled VPC (recommended first): network isolation, strict egress via an outbound proxy (TLS inspection or fixed IP egress), and optional NAT/proxy allowlist. Allows more rapid POC and incremental automation.
>   * True air-gapped: fully isolated compute with manual data ingress/egress. Higher ops overhead and slower iteration, but sometimes required for regulated on-prem deployments.
> 
> - Customer-managed keys (BYOK): yes, in Private deployments we integrate with customer KMS (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, or on-prem HSM). Key rotation is orchestrated via our ops playbook and can be scheduled or triggered manually; we can provide the KMS integration doc in the architecture workshop.
> 
> —
> Logan Wright
> Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
>

Attachments pending: MedSecure-BAA-draft.docx, medsecure-network-diagram-v1.png


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Thanks — thats helpful context. Quick answers:

- Air-gapped options: we support both models.
  * Walled VPC (recommended first): network isolation, strict egress via an outbound proxy (TLS inspection or fixed IP egress), and optional NAT/proxy allowlist. Allows more rapid POC and incremental automation.
  * True air-gapped: fully isolated compute with manual data ingress/egress. Higher ops overhead and slower iteration, but sometimes required for regulated on-prem deployments.

- Customer-managed keys (BYOK): yes, in Private deployments we integrate with customer KMS (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, or on-prem HSM). Key rotation is orchestrated via our ops playbook and can be scheduled or triggered manually; we can provide the KMS integration doc in the architecture workshop.

Ive attached the vendor questionnaire again and a short sample controls-mapping doc that shows how Redwood maps SOC 2 controls to common evidence artifacts (link below). Soojin will review your diagram and produce two sketches: VPC-walled and an air-gapped variant.

Proposed meeting: Wed 6/17 10:00 AM PST — happy to host an architecture workshop (60m). Please confirm and well send invite.

—
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachments: vendor-security-questionnaire-template.xlsx (xlsx)
Related: https://redwood-inference.docs/samples/controls-mapping


From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 09:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Confirmed — Wed 6/17 10:00 AM PST works. I uploaded the diagram and our BAA draft to the Drive link (https://drive.google.com/medsecure/poc-uploads).

Note: our preference is the walled VPC with a managed egress proxy (we want to avoid full manual transfers where possible). Well expect details on how you handle outbound allowlists, certificate pinning, and any central logging/export of audit logs.

Marissa — Ill start filling the questionnaire where I can; I may ping you for clarifications.

Thanks,
Daniel

text:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>; IT-Security@medsecure.com
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Thanks again for the time earlier, Daniel — quick recap + proposed next steps from our end.

Call recap
- Goal: scope a Private / VPC or air-gapped POC for MedSecure clinical ingestion pipeline.
- Primary compliance drivers: HIPAA (PHI in scope), GDPR (EU patient data residency) and SOC 2 evidence for vendor onboarding.
- Preferred topology: strongly leaning toward network-isolated VPC with egress control; air-gapped option discussed if regulatory requires no outbound connectivity.

What were attaching (initial evidence bundle)
- redwood-soc2-summary-2026.pdf: short SOC 2 Type II coverage and auditor summary.
- vendor-security-questionnaire-template.xlsx: our questionnaire we typically send to customers to accelerate answers; we can also ingest theirs.

Immediate asks for MedSecure
1) Can you share current SOC / compliance posture: SOC 2 Type II (if available) or expected timeline?
2) Confirm GDPR residency requirements (which EU region): do you require dedicated region + data residency?
3) HIPAA specifics to call out: covered entity vs business associate, any BAA draft you prefer?
4) Please upload your current network/infra diagram (VPC, proxies, egress controls) so Soojin can review isolation constraints — we referenced a couple of patterns on the call.

Proposed next steps
- Daniel: send the network diagram and any procurement questionnaire you already have (we can fill).
- Redwood: Soojin will run a short technical qualification and propose one architecture sketch (VPC + egress-proxy) and an air-gapped variant.
- Redwood: Marissa will own the vendor questionnaire coordination and follow up on mapping evidence.

Potential meeting: we can do a 60-minute architecture workshop Wed 6/17 or Thu 6/18 (PST). What works?

Thanks — Ill follow up with a calendar invite when you confirm.

—
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: redwood-soc2-summary-2026.pdf (pdf), vendor-security-questionnaire-template.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@medsecure.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: IT-Security@medsecure.com
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 16:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: security qualification, SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA and isolation options

Thanks Logan — good recap. A few quick notes:

- SOC2: we currently have a SOC 2 Type II in progress with an expected report in Q3 2026; we do have interim evidence (policies, SCIM audit logs, vulnerability scan reports) we can share now.
- GDPR: EU residency required for a subset of patients in Frankfurt (eu-central-1). We need assurance that data for those patients never leaves the region.
- HIPAA: MedSecure is a covered entity; Redwood would be a BA. We have a standard BAA template well attach.

Ill upload our high-level network diagram and the procurement questionnaire we use to the Drive link you provided by EOD tomorrow. The diagram will show our on-prem ingest, a DMZ, and an outbound proxy that restricts egress.

A couple of clarifying Qs for you:
1) For the air-gapped option — when you say "air-gapped" are you proposing totally isolated compute with manual data ingress/egress (sneakernet/managed transfer), or a walled VPC with egress-only via a controlled proxy? Were leaning toward the latter but want to understand tradeoffs.
2) Encryption at rest: do you support customer-managed keys (BYOK) in private deployments? If so, how is key rotation handled operationally?

Ill attach the BAA and our diagram once uploaded.

Best,
Daniel
Director, IT Security | MedSecure Health Systems
daniel.park@medsecure.com | +1 (206) 555-4444

Attachments pending: MedSecure-BAA-draft.docx, medsecure-network-diagram-v1.png

source.subject: SF Onsite: VIP Arrival Lounge Flow + Rider Ops
messages:
From: James Smith <james.smith@claritech.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-12T08:15:00-07:00
Subject: SF onsite: arrival logistics and rider concerns

Hi Logan —

Quick note ahead of next week's workshop: our execs (10 total) are flying in on the 15th, arriving between 3:30–5:15pm. A few asks from our side:

- Can Redwood provide a dedicated arrival lounge / staging area where riders can wait? Ideally heated indoor space near the loading zone.
- Two execs bring personal security with separate cars (driver + minivan). We need clear curbside staging and a driver check-in point.
- Dietary heads-up: 2 vegetarian, 1 pescatarian, 1 nut allergy.

Appreciate it if you can confirm a point person for drivers and kitchen handoff. Also: we expect some last-minute schedule changes; is there a single phone line for on-day ops?

Thanks —
James
James Smith
Head of Partnerships, ClariTech
james.smith@claritech.com
+1 415-555-0119

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: James Smith <james.smith@claritech.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Michael Ortiz <michael@harborsidecatering.com>, Kate Lin <kate@prism-transport.com>
Date: 2026-06-12T12:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SF onsite: arrival logistics and rider concerns

Hi James — thanks for the heads-up and the dietary callouts. Looping in Vanessa (hospitality) and Grace (venue ops) plus our vendors so we can lock down the flow.

Summary of immediate plan and asks:

- Arrival lounge: we can reserve the north mezzanine conference (heated, private) as the arrival lounge. See attached: arrival-lounge-map.pdf (will send to drivers).
- Curbside & driver check-in: designated driver lane at Gate B, with a staffed check-in table and placards. I propose drivers check in, receive a rider placard, and then park on Level P2 for staging. We'll provide a driver briefing sheet (attached).
- Security cars: we will reserve two adjacent curb spaces and a valet hold for up to 30 minutes to allow secure transfer.
- Food handoff: Harbor Catering (Michael) will stage in Kitchen 2; expected handoff to front-of-house at 6:00pm. Michael — can you confirm you have 2 FOH runners at 5:45pm and a plated timeline?

Next steps / immediate asks:
1) Michael / Kate: confirm driver & kitchen staffing by EOD Monday (6/14).
2) Vanessa / Grace: confirm signage and placard prints — can we have 10 guest placards + 4 driver placards?
3) James: please send final guest list + arrival windows by 6/14 so we can publish the driver schedule.

On-day contact: use my cell (415-555-0232) as primary ops line; Vanessa will take overflow (vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com). For drivers, Kate will be the dispatcher.

Attachments: arrival-lounge-map.pdf; driver-briefing-sheet.docx

Thanks —

Logan Wright
Event Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0232

From: Michael Ortiz <michael@harborsidecatering.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Kate Lin <kate@prism-transport.com>, James Smith <james.smith@claritech.com>
Date: 2026-06-13T09:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SF onsite: arrival logistics and rider concerns

Logan — all good from catering side. Quick confirmations:

- Menu: attached is harbor-menu-v2.pdf (includes clear veg/pesc options and nut-free dessert swap).
- Staffing: 2 FOH runners confirmed for 5:30–7:30pm; head chef will be on-site for final plating at 5:15pm.
- Kitchen staging request: we need a 6ft staging table outside Kitchen 2 for final garnish. Will need a FOH path of 6ft to the north mezzanine elevator.

A couple of constraints to flag:
- If traffic delays push arrivals past 6:30pm we will pivot to boxed arrival trays for first wave; please confirm preferred fallback.
- We will label all plates with guest initials if you provide the final seating map by noon on 6/15.

Happy to hop on a 15-min sync if that helps.

—Michael
Michael Ortiz
Director of Events, Harborside Catering
michael@harborsidecatering.com | +1 415-555-0788

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: James Smith <james.smith@claritech.com>, Michael Ortiz <michael@harborsidecatering.com>, Kate Lin <kate@prism-transport.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-15T16:30:00-07:00
Subject: Final: SF arrival & driver ops (day-of runbook)

Thanks team — finalizing the day-of runbook below. This is the authoritative sequence for tonight's dinner. Please read, confirm, or flag changes immediately.

1) 15:00–17:15: Guest arrivals (waves). Drivers use Gate B curbside; check-in at Driver Desk (Gate B). Drivers receive rider placard and parking permit for P2 staging.
2) 17:00: Vendor load-in complete. Harbor Catering to confirm Kitchen 2 staging table setup.
3) 17:30: FOH runners at mezzanine to accept plated trays for first 6 guests. If arrival > 18:30, fallback = boxed arrival trays (Michael confirmed).
4) 17:45: Security transfers for two execs with private vehicles — valet hold at adjacent curb; Security escort will walk to north mezzanine elevator.
5) 18:00: Kitchen handoff complete — FOH takes over; event lead (Vanessa) does guest table confirmation.
6) On-day contacts: Logan W. (primary) +1 415-555-0232; Vanessa O. (secondary) +1 415-555-0199; Kate L. driver dispatch +1 415-555-0456.

Driver briefing highlights (excerpt from driver-briefing-sheet.docx):
- Drivers must remain with vehicle during check-in and move to P2 when instructed.
- No idling at Gate B for more than 10 minutes.
- Emergency lane is maintained at all times.

On quoting previous for clarity:
> On Sat, Jun 13, 2026 at 9:00 AM Michael Ortiz <michael@harborsidecatering.com> wrote:
> > If traffic delays push arrivals past 6:30pm we will pivot to boxed arrival trays for first wave; please confirm preferred fallback.

Fallback decision: boxed arrival trays as of 18:30.

Attachments: arrival-lounge-map.pdf; driver-briefing-sheet.docx; harbor-menu-v2.pdf

Looking forward to a smooth handoff — will report post-event.

Logan Wright
Event Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
+1 415-555-0232

Director of Events, Harborside Catering
michael@harborsidecatering.com | +1 415-555-0788



From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: James Smith <james.smith@claritech.com>, Michael Ortiz <michael@harborsidecatering.com>, Kate Lin <kate@prism-transport.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-15T16:30:00-07:00
Subject: Final: SF arrival & driver ops (day-of runbook)

Thanks team — finalizing the day-of runbook below. This is the authoritative sequence for tonight's dinner. Please read, confirm, or flag changes immediately.

1) 15:00–17:15: Guest arrivals (waves). Drivers use Gate B curbside; check-in at Driver Desk (Gate B). Drivers receive rider placard and parking permit for P2 staging.
2) 17:00: Vendor load-in complete. Harbor Catering to confirm Kitchen 2 staging table setup.
3) 17:30: FOH runners at mezzanine to accept plated trays for first 6 guests. If arrival > 18:30, fallback = boxed arrival trays (Michael confirmed).
4) 17:45: Security transfers for two execs with private vehicles — valet hold at adjacent curb; Security escort will walk to north mezzanine elevator.
5) 18:00: Kitchen handoff complete — FOH takes over; event lead (Vanessa) does guest table confirmation.
6) On-day contacts: Logan W. (primary) +1 415-555-0232; Vanessa O. (secondary) +1 415-555-0199; Kate L. driver dispatch +1 415-555-0456.

Driver briefing highlights (excerpt from driver-briefing-sheet.docx):
- Drivers must remain with vehicle during check-in and move to P2 when instructed.
- No idling at Gate B for more than 10 minutes.
- Emergency lane is maintained at all times.

Fallback decision: boxed arrival trays as of 18:30.

Looking forward to a smooth handoff — will report post-event.

source.subject: Overcharged April usage + credit note / VAT evidence request
messages:
From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@prysmhealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Prysm Health Billing <billing@prysmhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Discrepancy on April invoice (overcharged committed rate)

Hi Logan,

I was going through our April invoices and noticed line items billed at your standard on-demand rate instead of the contracted committed rate from our Q1 agreement (see attached Invoice_2026-04-30.pdf). This resulted in roughly a $21,340 overcharge for April.

We need two things to close this out: 1) confirmation you will issue a credit or corrected invoice and 2) VAT/tax documentation we can share with our AP team for cross-border withholding. Prysm is registered in the EU for VAT; we may need a credit note that includes your VAT breakdown so our local team can process correctly.

Can you confirm next steps and an ETA? We have a payment run on June 20 and would like to reconcile before then.

Thanks,
Laura Chen
Finance Manager
Prysm Health

Attachment: Invoice_2026-04-30.pdf
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@prysmhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discrepancy on April invoice (overcharged committed rate)

Hi Laura — thanks for flagging and sending the invoice.

We’ll prioritize this. Immediate plan:
- I’ve asked Finance (Marissa) to run a reconciliation and propose a credit amount.
- We’ll prepare either a credit note or reissued invoice per your preference; please confirm which you prefer (credit note applied to next invoice vs. corrected invoice issued retroactively).
- For VAT/withholding, can you send Prysm’s VAT registration certificate (or the WHT exemption if applicable)? That will help us decide whether to issue a VAT-inclusive credit note or a net credit plus separate VAT invoice.

Marissa/Ben/Naomi — can you take the lead on calculating the exact overcharged amount and suggest the accounting path (credit memo vs corrected invoice)? Laura would like reconciliation before her June 20 payment run.

Logan
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@prysmhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 08:46:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Discrepancy on April invoice (overcharged committed rate) — reconciliation attached

Forwarding recon work. I ran a line-item reconciliation against the committed-rate schedule and the usage ledger. Key notes in recon_2026-06-12.xlsx:
- Gross overcharge (April): $21,340.00 (billed at on-demand vs contracted committed rate).
- VAT treatment: Prysm is VAT-registered in EU; we should issue a credit note with VAT lines reversed if they provide certificate. If they do not provide, we can still issue a goodwill credit but will need to treat VAT per local rules.
- Withholding: Prysm mentioned cross-border withholding; I flagged that with tax and they suggested we net the credit and issue a separate VAT adjustment if needed.

Requesting direction:
1) Issue a credit note dated June 15 for $21,340 (credit type: service correction) with VAT shown as 0.00 pending Prysm VAT cert, or
2) Reissue corrected April invoice (less disruptive for their AP but more work to void original invoice in accounting).

Given Laura’s payment run on June 20, option 1 (credit note applied to next invoice) is the fastest. Once Laura confirms VAT cert, we’ll add VAT lines to the credit note and provide prysm_vat_certificate.pdf back to you.

Attachment: recon_2026-06-12.xlsx

Marissa Cole
Senior Accounting Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@prysmhealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 07:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discrepancy on April invoice (overcharged committed rate) — recon attached

Hi Marissa/Logan — thanks for the quick turnaround.

We prefer a separate credit note applied against our next invoice (option 1). Attached is Prysm's VAT registration certificate and the AP contact who will need the credit note format for reconciliation.

AP contact: Elena Morales <elena.morales@prysmhealth.com> (please cc her on the credit note).

Attachment: prysm_vat_certificate.pdf

Laura
Finance Manager
Prysm Health
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@prysmhealth.com>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@prysmhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 16:22:00 -0700
Subject: Credit note issuance and next steps — Prysm Health

Hi Laura and Elena — confirm we will proceed with a credit note applied to the next invoice as requested. Summary and actions: 
- Credit note amount: $21,340.00 (service correction for April overbilling).
- Credit note date: 2026-06-15.
- VAT handling: prysm_vat_certificate.pdf received; Marissa will include a VAT breakdown on the credit note showing 0.00 VAT reversal per certificate and include our VAT statement for their AP.
- Delivery: credit note PDF and a short cover email will be sent to Elena Morales (elena.morales@prysmhealth.com) by EOD 2026-06-16.
- Documentation: we will attach recon_2026-06-12.xlsx and Invoice_2026-04-30.pdf to the credit note package.
- Administrative: Elena, to apply the credit to your June payment run, please confirm the invoice number you want it applied against (or we can apply to the next invoice automatically).

Marissa — please issue the credit memo in our accounting system and upload the final PDF to the HubSpot deal record. Ben — please track this as a goodwill adjustment and mark it in the revenue adjustments tracker. Naomi — flag in billing ops for the credit application.

If everything looks good I’ll send the DocuSign amendment (small one-line service correction) to Laura for signature; not strictly necessary but preferred for audit trail. Link: https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=abcd-1234

Thanks all — will follow up once the credit note is uploaded.

Logan Wright
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
Attachment: (will include recon_2026-06-12.xlsx, Invoice_2026-04-30.pdf in final package)

source.subject: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@deltaindustries.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@deltaindustries.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)

Hi Sarah / Tom — thanks again for carving out time today. Quick written recap so the sponsor team has a single source of truth ahead of your board liaison later this month.

Key pain points we heard
- Latency spikes on peak daily inference (search/assist workflows) causing >300ms tail p50->p95 regressions during promos
- Rising token costs on high-volume pipelines with bursty traffic; lack of clear cost controls per route
- Audit and residency requirements for PII-derived embeddings — legal wants retention + key management clarity

Desired outcomes (what success looks like)
1) Consistent p95 inference latency under 250ms for typical 4K-context queries in production
2) Predictable unit economics: <40% variance month-over-month and visibility to per-route cost
3) Clear compliance posture: SOC2-ready evidence and ability to define retention windows + KMS integration

Procurement path & timeline (suggested)
- Week 0: Exec readout with sponsor + procurement (decision criteria and budget ask)
- Week 1–3: 3-week pilot (isolated tenant, production-like traffic sample, metrics dashboard)
- Week 4: Pilot readout -> procurement issues term sheet / SOW -> signature (target: 30–45 days total)

Top risks we flagged (and proposed mitigations)
- Data residency: offer Private (VPC) installer or region-isolated hosting + review of KMS flow (deliverable: security-evidence-snapshot.pdf)
- Latency at scale: recommend small canary with continuous batching + KV cache enabled; ops playbook attached in summary PDF
- Procurement lead time: propose pre-approved SOW template to speed legal review (we can loop your procurement contact)

Immediate asks from your side
- Confirm exec sponsor(s) for the readout (name + preferred date window)
- Provide primary procurement contact and any vendor onboarding checklist we should be aware of

Attachments: redwood-postcall-summary-20260611.pdf (one-pager), redwood-poc-outline-3week.xlsx (proposed pilot metrics), security-evidence-snapshot.pdf

If useful I can draft the 20-min executive readout slide deck and a short one-page decision memo for the board. Suggest we tentatively block 30 Jun or 1 Jul for the exec readout — what works best?

Thanks — looking forward to aligning the sponsor team.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0183
From: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@deltaindustries.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@deltaindustries.com>, procurement@deltaindustries.com
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 17:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)

Logan — thank you for the crisp summary. A few clarifications from our side:

- Sponsor: I'll be the business sponsor and will present the readout; Tom (CTO) will drive the technical evaluation and confirm our latency thresholds.
- Procurement: procurement@deltaindustries.com is our onboarding mailbox; they will require a vendor questionnaire and our standard 60–90 day procurement window unless we can pre-agree SOW terms.
- Compliance: the security team needs a short architecture note showing how PII is isolated and a statement on KMS/HSM integration; the security-evidence-snapshot.pdf will be helpful.

Concerns to surface to your leadership:
- If the pilot requires long-tail tuning with repeated kernel changes it may trigger our change-control process (adds 2 weeks). Please note.
- We need an explicit fallback for degraded models (e.g., lower-capacity variant) to ensure SLAs during peak events.

Next steps for us:
1) Procurement will send vendor onboarding checklist (CC'd) — please respond with docs within 48 hours.
2) Tom to provide an initial sample query workload by Mon, 15 Jun for pilot sizing.
3) I'll confirm sponsor calendar windows for end-June readout by Fri.

Appreciate the one-pager offer — that will help the board.

Best,
Sarah Lim
VP, Product Operations | Delta Industries
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@deltaindustries.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@deltaindustries.com>, procurement@deltaindustries.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)

Thanks, Sarah — perfect. Quick commitments from our side so you have dates to share with procurement and security: 

- Docs: I'll upload the vendor packet, SOC2 snapshot, and the small architecture note to the Drive folder and respond to procurement within 24 hours.
- Pilot sizing: Marissa will pick this up with Tom (Marissa will validate the sample workload and return a recommended GPU profile + throughput target).
- Readout materials: I will draft a 2-slide decision memo + 10-slide readout for the board and share by Tue, 16 Jun for your review.

Proposed timeline (concrete)
- 12 Jun: vendor packet to procurement (we'll ask for fast-track review)
- 15 Jun: workload sample + pilot sizing from Tom
- 16 Jun: readout draft v1 to Sarah + Tom
- 22 Jun: start pilot (subject to procurement sign-off)

Pilot scope (summary)
- 3 weeks, production-like traffic (sample), key metrics: p50/p95 latency, tokens per call, cost per 1k tokens, quality delta vs current model.

If procurement signals a strict window, we can compress the readout review cycle by sharing the decision memo first and pushing the pilot start after signature. I'll coordinate with Ben on any commercial flexibility (credit/intro pricing) if needed.

Action items assigned:
- Logan: vendor packet + decision memo (due 12–16 Jun)
- Marissa: pilot sizing & ops playbook (due 15 Jun)
- Tom: sample query workload (due 15 Jun)

I'll upload the attachments now — search for folder: "Delta Executive Readout" in the Drive link below.

Links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B3cDelta-Executive-Readout

Thanks all,
Logan
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: delta board liaison — potential escalation & commercial note

Marissa / Ben — forwarding the external thread so you have context. Two quick internal items: 

1) Escalation sensitivity: Sarah loops the board; if latency doesn't reach targets in pilot they will escalate to platform leads. Marissa — please prepare an ops runbook with rollback triggers and a short mitigation plan we can present during the readout.

2) Commercial flexibility: Delta procurement tends to run long. Ben — can you pre-clear a one-time pilot credit (up to $12k) and a 3-month intro price band in case procurement asks for a reduction to accelerate sign-off? If not, flag the approved discount ceiling.

I'll share the readout draft once it's ready.

Thanks,
Logan

title: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@deltaindustries.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@deltaindustries.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)

Hi Sarah / Tom — thanks again for carving out time today. Quick written recap so the sponsor team has a single source of truth ahead of your board liaison later this month.

Key pain points we heard
- Latency spikes on peak daily inference (search/assist workflows) causing >300ms tail p50->p95 regressions during promos
- Rising token costs on high-volume pipelines with bursty traffic; lack of clear cost controls per route
- Audit and residency requirements for PII-derived embeddings — legal wants retention + key management clarity

Desired outcomes (what success looks like)
1) Consistent p95 inference latency under 250ms for typical 4K-context queries in production
2) Predictable unit economics: <40% variance month-over-month and visibility to per-route cost
3) Clear compliance posture: SOC2-ready evidence and ability to define retention windows + KMS integration

Procurement path & timeline (suggested)
- Week 0: Exec readout with sponsor + procurement (decision criteria and budget ask)
- Week 1–3: 3-week pilot (isolated tenant, production-like traffic sample, metrics dashboard)
- Week 4: Pilot readout -> procurement issues term sheet / SOW -> signature (target: 30–45 days total)

Top risks we flagged (and proposed mitigations)
- Data residency: offer Private (VPC) installer or region-isolated hosting + review of KMS flow (deliverable: security-evidence-snapshot.pdf)
- Latency at scale: recommend small canary with continuous batching + KV cache enabled; ops playbook attached in summary PDF
- Procurement lead time: propose pre-approved SOW template to speed legal review (we can loop your procurement contact)

Immediate asks from your side
- Confirm exec sponsor(s) for the readout (name + preferred date window)
- Provide primary procurement contact and any vendor onboarding checklist we should be aware of

Attachments: redwood-postcall-summary-20260611.pdf (one-pager), redwood-poc-outline-3week.xlsx (proposed pilot metrics), security-evidence-snapshot.pdf

If useful I can draft the 20-min executive readout slide deck and a short one-page decision memo for the board. Suggest we tentatively block 30 Jun or 1 Jul for the exec readout — what works best?

Thanks — looking forward to aligning the sponsor team.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0183


From: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@deltaindustries.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@deltaindustries.com>, procurement@deltaindustries.com
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 17:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)

Logan — thank you for the crisp summary. A few clarifications from our side:

- Sponsor: I'll be the business sponsor and will present the readout; Tom (CTO) will drive the technical evaluation and confirm our latency thresholds.
- Procurement: procurement@deltaindustries.com is our onboarding mailbox; they will require a vendor questionnaire and our standard 60–90 day procurement window unless we can pre-agree SOW terms.
- Compliance: the security team needs a short architecture note showing how PII is isolated and a statement on KMS/HSM integration; the security-evidence-snapshot.pdf will be helpful.

Concerns to surface to your leadership:
- If the pilot requires long-tail tuning with repeated kernel changes it may trigger our change-control process (adds 2 weeks). Please note.
- We need an explicit fallback for degraded models (e.g., lower-capacity variant) to ensure SLAs during peak events.

Next steps for us:
1) Procurement will send vendor onboarding checklist (CC'd) — please respond with docs within 48 hours.
2) Tom to provide an initial sample query workload by Mon, 15 Jun for pilot sizing.
3) I'll confirm sponsor calendar windows for end-June readout by Fri.

Appreciate the one-pager offer — that will help the board.

Best,
Sarah Lim
VP, Product Operations | Delta Industries


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Lim <sarah.lim@deltaindustries.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@deltaindustries.com>, procurement@deltaindustries.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Board liaison: action matrix, procurement path & risk triage (post-call)

Thanks, Sarah — perfect. Quick commitments from our side so you have dates to share with procurement and security: 

- Docs: I'll upload the vendor packet, SOC2 snapshot, and the small architecture note to the Drive folder and respond to procurement within 24 hours.
- Pilot sizing: Marissa will pick this up with Tom (Marissa will validate the sample workload and return a recommended GPU profile + throughput target).
- Readout materials: I will draft a 2-slide decision memo + 10-slide readout for the board and share by Tue, 16 Jun for your review.

Proposed timeline (concrete)
- 12 Jun: vendor packet to procurement (we'll ask for fast-track review)
- 15 Jun: workload sample + pilot sizing from Tom
- 16 Jun: readout draft v1 to Sarah + Tom
- 22 Jun: start pilot (subject to procurement sign-off)

Pilot scope (summary)
- 3 weeks, production-like traffic (sample), key metrics: p50/p95 latency, tokens per call, cost per 1k tokens, quality delta vs current model.

If procurement signals a strict window, we can compress the readout review cycle by sharing the decision memo first and pushing the pilot start after signature. I'll coordinate with Ben on any commercial flexibility (credit/intro pricing) if needed.

Action items assigned:
- Logan: vendor packet + decision memo (due 12–16 Jun)
- Marissa: pilot sizing & ops playbook (due 15 Jun)
- Tom: sample query workload (due 15 Jun)

Thanks all,
Logan

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Greenroom promo bucket top-up request — BrightStream workshop

Hi Taylor,

Quick one: the BrightStream livestream workshop on Monday has seen a last-minute spike in cohost requests — we expect the greenroom (sponsor demo slots + partner cohosts) to consume more onboarding seats than originally planned. Can BrightStream approve a temporary top-up of 50 promo credits for the greenroom so we don’t have to throttle partner demos?

Attached is a snapshot of the current promo bucket usage (promo_bucket_snapshot.csv) and a one-pager with the planned distribution. If you can sign off, we’ll pull from our community promo pool and tag those credits to the BrightStream workshop (deal BR-2026-0934). For procurement/audit, we’ll also need a countersigned SOW or an approval email to route this through finance.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm +50 credits by EOD Friday
- I loop in Marissa for operations allocation and Naomi for finance confirmation
- We issue the codes and share the redemption sheet with Elena in event ops

Thanks — appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

Naomi, would you be able to approve an internal fund transfer / one-off allocation today? If yes, Jordan — please get BrightStream to countersign the SOW (or send approval mail) so we have paper trail for accounting.

-Marissa

-Naomi

Taylor — great, thanks. Naomi has approved the internal transfer pending either a countersigned SOW or an approval email that cites BR-2026-0934. I’m sending the SOW template over now (attached) and I’ll prep a short approval email you can forward to procurement if that’s faster for BrightStream.

If you’re OK with email approval, please reply to this thread with language like: “BrightStream approves a one-time 50-credit promo top-up for the Greenroom on deal BR-2026-0934. This serves as authorization for Redwood to issue codes and reconcile the allocation.” If you can countersign the SOW, we’ll push DocuSign and schedule code issuance for Friday morning so Elena has the redemption sheet before the event.

Naomi, would you be able to approve an internal fund transfer / one-off allocation today? If yes, Jordan — please get BrightStream to countersign the SOW (or send approval mail) so we have paper trail for accounting.

Logan, FYI — when this is greenlit, ops will need a redemption spreadsheet and the TTL for the codes (we typically set 14 days).

source.subject: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>, security@heliospay.com
Cc: audits@prisma-audits.com
Date: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Team — HeliosPay kicked off an initial SIG/SOC2 checklist review and asked for a set of small, targeted architecture vignettes that show how we isolate inference workloads in two operator modes: 1) Redwood Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) and 2) Redwood Private (customer VPC with on‑prem connector).

They specifically asked for: 
- One-page diagram per vignette (PDF SVG preferred) with annotated data flows and egress controls.
- A short matrix mapping each diagram to SIG controls (segmentation, tenancy, control plane separation) — 1 tab in an XLSX is fine.
- Callouts for KMS/HSM boundaries, logging/audit emission points, and fallback behavior when Dedicated capacity is saturated.

Deliverables I want us to hand to HeliosPay by end of next week (EOD Fri): sketches-v1.pdf (attached), isolation-matrix.xlsx (attached) + a single summary email to their security contact.

Pointers for the vignettes: 
- Dedicated vignette: show tenant-tagged inference queue -> reserved GPU pool -> prefix/KV cache layer -> outbound egress only via customer-approved NAT or private peering. Indicate service mesh control-plane traffic separation.
- Private vignette: show customer VPC with VPC‑peering or dedicated interconnect, sidecar-run inference nodes (optional on-prem gateway), and airgap/bridge option for regulated customers.

Kimberly — can you draw the diagrams and export to PDF + SVG? Rishi — can you produce the SIG mapping sheet and short language for the auditor? Marissa — please own the HeliosPay point of contact communication and signoff. Logan — I'll loop you in if we need to run the sketches by Solutions Eng for implementation nuance.

I attached an early whiteboard export (sketches-v1.pdf) with very rough boxes — use it only as a starting point; avoid copying the wording verbatim for the auditor.

Thanks — short meeting ok today at 11:00 if anyone wants to do a 20-minute alignment.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwoodinference.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0112

Attachments: sketches-v1.pdf (1.2MB)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 09:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Ben — thanks, grabbed this. Quick clarifying Qs before I start: 
1) For Dedicated vignette, do we want to show both the reserved GPU pool with and without external prefix caching (two small panels), or keep it to one canonical flow and call out cache as a note?
2) For the Private vignette, should the diagram indicate optional HSM/KMS in the customer's cloud, or show Redwood KMS + customer-managed keys (we have both patterns in the field)?

I can have first-pass diagrams (PDF + exported SVG layers) by tomorrow EOD if we pick the patterns now. I'll use the provided whiteboard as the layout and tighten the control-plane vs data-plane annotations.

Kimberly Park
Senior Solutions Architect
Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com

On Tue, 09 Jun 2026 at 09:14, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Team — HeliosPay kicked off an initial SIG/SOC2 checklist review and asked for a set of small, targeted architecture vignettes that show how we isolate inference workloads in two operator modes: 1) Redwood Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) and 2) Redwood Private (customer VPC with on‑prem connector).
> 
> They specifically asked for: 
> - One-page diagram per vignette (PDF SVG preferred) with annotated data flows and egress controls.
> - A short matrix mapping each diagram to SIG controls (segmentation, tenancy, control plane separation) — 1 tab in an XLSX is fine.
> - Callouts for KMS/HSM boundaries, logging/audit emission points, and fallback behavior when Dedicated capacity is saturated.
> 
> Deliverables I want us to hand to HeliosPay by end of next week (EOD Fri): sketches-v1.pdf (attached), isolation-matrix.xlsx (attached) + a single summary email to their security contact.
> 
> Pointers for the vignettes: 
> - Dedicated vignette: show tenant-tagged inference queue -> reserved GPU pool -> prefix/KV cache layer -> outbound egress only via customer-approved NAT or private peering. Indicate service mesh control-plane traffic separation.
> - Private vignette: show customer VPC with VPC‑peering or dedicated interconnect, sidecar-run inference nodes (optional on-prem gateway), and airgap/bridge option for regulated customers.
> 
> Kimberly — can you draw the diagrams and export to PDF + SVG? Rishi — can you produce the SIG mapping sheet and short language for the auditor? Marissa — please own the HeliosPay point of contact communication and signoff. Logan — I'll loop you in if we need to run the sketches by Solutions Eng for implementation nuance.
> 
> I attached an early whiteboard export (sketches-v1.pdf) with very rough boxes — use it only as a starting point; avoid copying the wording verbatim for the auditor.
> 
> Thanks — short meeting ok today at 11:00 if anyone wants to do a 20-minute alignment.
> 
> —
> Ben Carter
> Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
> Redwood Inference
> ben_carter@redwoodinference.com
> Phone: +1 (415) 555-0112
> 
> Attachments: sketches-v1.pdf (1.2MB)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 10:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Kimberly / Ben — I can own the SIG mapping tab. A few notes to keep the auditor language crisp: 
- For each vignette please include a short "Assurance statement" box (1–2 lines) that says what guarantees the architecture provides: e.g., "Tenant compute and KV cache are logically isolated by tenant ID and enforced by the runtime; control plane traffic uses a separate management network with mutual TLS."
- Call out the primary evidence items we can attach: network ACL exports, iptables/route snapshots (redacted), KMS key policy snippet, and a sample audit log event showing tenant id. Put these as bullet items in the matrix next to each mapped control.
- KMS guidance: show both patterns. For Private customers who bring keys, note the customer‑managed KMS with cross-account key granting; for hosted Dedicated show Redwood KMS with per-tenant key wrapping (we must not imply customer keys are stored by Redwood when they're not).

I'll draft suggested auditor text for each control (SIG + SIG-CSF snippet) and drop it into isolation-matrix.xlsx. Expect a draft by Thu morning.

Rishi Malhotra
Principal Security Architect
Redwood Inference
rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com

On Tue, 09 Jun 2026 at 09:48, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Ben — thanks, grabbed this. Quick clarifying Qs before I start: 
> 1) For Dedicated vignette, do we want to show both the reserved GPU pool with and without external prefix caching (two small panels), or keep it to one canonical flow and call out cache as a note?
> 2) For the Private vignette, should the diagram indicate optional HSM/KMS in the customer's cloud, or show Redwood KMS + customer-managed keys (we have both patterns in the field)?
> 
> I can have first-pass diagrams (PDF + exported SVG layers) by tomorrow EOD if we pick the patterns now. I'll use the provided whiteboard as the layout and tighten the control-plane vs data-plane annotations.
> 
> Kimberly Park
> Senior Solutions Architect
> Redwood Inference
> kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: security@heliospay.com, audits@prisma-audits.com
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Thanks — attaching the updated files and a short summary we can send to HeliosPay.

Summary draft to HeliosPay (please review):
Hi Gaurav,

Thanks for the review kickoff. Attached are two one-page vignettes showing typical deployment patterns (Dedicated reserved pool + Private VPC interconnect), a SIG mapping matrix linking diagram elements to SIG controls, and a short list of supporting evidence artifacts we can provide on request (redacted network ACLs, KMS policy snippet, sample audit events).

Key notes: 
- Dedicated: tenant compute isolated via runtime tenant IDs; control plane uses dedicated management VPC with mTLS; fallback behavior routes to a lower-cost model variant under capacity pressure (documented in the matrix).
- Private: customer-managed network boundary; optional customer KMS/HSM or cross-account key grants to Redwood; explicit egress controls via customer NAT or private peering.

Requested next steps: please confirm which artifacts you want attached (we've included the matrix and sketches as a starting point). If useful, we can run a 30-minute walkthrough this week with Rishi and Kimberly.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: sketches-v1.pdf, sketches-v1.svg, isolation-matrix.xlsx

On Wed, 10 Jun 2026 at 10:35, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Kimberly / Ben — I can own the SIG mapping tab. A few notes to keep the auditor language crisp: 
> - For each vignette please include a short "Assurance statement" box (1–2 lines) that says what guarantees the architecture provides: e.g., "Tenant compute and KV cache are logically isolated by tenant ID and enforced by the runtime; control plane traffic uses a separate management network with mutual TLS."
> - Call out the primary evidence items we can attach: network ACL exports, iptables/route snapshots (redacted), KMS key policy snippet, and a sample audit log event showing tenant id. Put these as bullet items in the matrix next to each mapped control.
> - KMS guidance: show both patterns. For Private customers who bring keys, note the customer‑managed KMS with cross-account key granting; for hosted Dedicated show Redwood KMS with per-tenant key wrapping (we must not imply customer keys are stored by Redwood when they're not).
> 
> I'll draft suggested auditor text for each control (SIG + SIG-CSF snippet) and drop it into isolation-matrix.xlsx. Expect a draft by Thu morning.
> 
> Rishi Malhotra
> Principal Security Architect
> Redwood Inference
> rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com

kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com

On Tue, 09 Jun 2026 at 09:14, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Team — HeliosPay kicked off an initial SIG/SOC2 checklist review and asked for a set of small, targeted architecture vignettes that show how we isolate inference workloads in two operator modes: 1) Redwood Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) and 2) Redwood Private (customer VPC with on‑prem connector).
> 
> They specifically asked for: 
> - One-page diagram per vignette (PDF SVG preferred) with annotated data flows and egress controls.
> - A short matrix mapping each diagram to SIG controls (segmentation, tenancy, control plane separation) — 1 tab in an XLSX is fine.
> - Callouts for KMS/HSM boundaries, logging/audit emission points, and fallback behavior when Dedicated capacity is saturated.
> 
> Deliverables I want us to hand to HeliosPay by end of next week (EOD Fri): sketches-v1.pdf (attached), isolation-matrix.xlsx (attached) + a single summary email to their security contact.
> 
> Pointers for the vignettes: 
> - Dedicated vignette: show tenant-tagged inference queue -> reserved GPU pool -> prefix/KV cache layer -> outbound egress only via customer-approved NAT or private peering. Indicate service mesh control-plane traffic separation.
> - Private vignette: show customer VPC with VPC‑peering or dedicated interconnect, sidecar-run inference nodes (optional on-prem gateway), and airgap/bridge option for regulated customers.
> 
> Kimberly — can you draw the diagrams and export to PDF + SVG? Rishi — can you produce the SIG mapping sheet and short language for the auditor? Marissa — please own the HeliosPay point of contact communication and signoff. Logan — I'll loop you in if we need to run the sketches by Solutions Eng for implementation nuance.
> 
> I attached an early whiteboard export (sketches-v1.pdf) with very rough boxes — use it only as a starting point; avoid copying the wording verbatim for the auditor.
> 
> Thanks — short meeting ok today at 11:00 if anyone wants to do a 20-minute alignment.
> 
> —
> Ben Carter
> Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
> Redwood Inference
> ben_carter@redwoodinference.com
> Phone: +1 (415) 555-0112
> 
> Attachments: sketches-v1.pdf (1.2MB)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 10:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Kimberly / Ben — I can own the SIG mapping tab. A few notes to keep the auditor language crisp: 
- For each vignette please include a short \"Assurance statement\" box (1–2 lines) that says what guarantees the architecture provides: e.g., \"Tenant compute and KV cache are logically isolated by tenant ID and enforced by the runtime; control plane traffic uses a separate management network with mutual TLS.\"
- Call out the primary evidence items we can attach: network ACL exports, iptables/route snapshots (redacted), KMS key policy snippet, and a sample audit log event showing tenant id. Put these as bullet items in the matrix next to each mapped control.
- KMS guidance: show both patterns. For Private customers who bring keys, note the customer‑managed KMS with cross-account key granting; for hosted Dedicated show Redwood KMS with per-tenant key wrapping (we must not imply customer keys are stored by Redwood when they're not).

I'll draft suggested auditor text for each control (SIG + SIG-CSF snippet) and drop it into isolation-matrix.xlsx. Expect a draft by Thu morning.

Rishi Malhotra
Principal Security Architect
Redwood Inference
rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com

On Tue, 09 Jun 2026 at 09:48, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Ben — thanks, grabbed this. Quick clarifying Qs before I start: 
> 1) For Dedicated vignette, do we want to show both the reserved GPU pool with and without external prefix caching (two small panels), or keep it to one canonical flow and call out cache as a note?
> 2) For the Private vignette, should the diagram indicate optional HSM/KMS in the customer's cloud, or show Redwood KMS + customer-managed keys (we have both patterns in the field)?
> 
> I can have first-pass diagrams (PDF + exported SVG layers) by tomorrow EOD if we pick the patterns now. I'll use the provided whiteboard as the layout and tighten the control-plane vs data-plane annotations.
> 
> Kimberly Park
> Senior Solutions Architect
> Redwood Inference
> kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: security@heliospay.com, audits@prisma-audits.com
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Architectural sketches: isolation vignettes for Dedicated & Private — evidence ask

Thanks — attaching the updated files and a short summary we can send to HeliosPay.

Summary draft to HeliosPay (please review):
Hi Gaurav,

Thanks for the review kickoff. Attached are two one-page vignettes showing typical deployment patterns (Dedicated reserved pool + Private VPC interconnect), a SIG mapping matrix linking diagram elements to SIG controls, and a short list of supporting evidence artifacts we can provide on request (redacted network ACLs, KMS policy snippet, sample audit events).

Key notes: 
- Dedicated: tenant compute isolated via runtime tenant IDs; control plane uses dedicated management VPC with mTLS; fallback behavior routes to a lower-cost model variant under capacity pressure (documented in the matrix).
- Private: customer-managed network boundary; optional customer KMS/HSM or cross-account key grants to Redwood; explicit egress controls via customer NAT or private peering.

Requested next steps: please confirm which artifacts you want attached (we've included the matrix and sketches as a starting point). If useful, we can run a 30-minute walkthrough this week with Rishi and Kimberly.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: sketches-v1.pdf, sketches-v1.svg, isolation-matrix.xlsx

> - For each vignette please include a short \"Assurance statement\" box (1–2 lines) that says what guarantees the architecture provides: e.g., \"Tenant compute and KV cache are logically isolated by tenant ID and enforced by the runtime; control plane traffic uses a separate management network with mutual TLS.\"
> - Call out the primary evidence items we can attach: network ACL exports, iptables/route snapshots (redacted), KMS key policy snippet, and a sample audit log event showing tenant id. Put these as bullet items in the matrix next to each mapped control.
> - KMS guidance: show both patterns. For Private customers who bring keys, note the customer‑managed KMS with cross-account key granting; for hosted Dedicated show Redwood KMS with per-tenant key wrapping (we must not imply customer keys are stored by Redwood when they're not).
> 
> I'll draft suggested auditor text for each control (SIG + SIG-CSF snippet) and drop it into isolation-matrix.xlsx. Expect a draft by Thu morning.

> Rishi Malhotra

1) Can you send a single ZIP with all presenter decks, a 1-page bio for each speaker, and a 2-slide customer-impact example per session by EOD Wed? Our internal comms team will route decks through legal for a quick check.

source.subject: Order Form + MSA intake: template selection, billing exhibit, and timeline
source.content:
Message 1/4
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Ramos <ava.ramos@novatrack.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Order Form + MSA intake: template selection, billing exhibit, and timeline

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Per our discussion, attaching our current Order Form template and a draft billing exhibit that captures usage tiers. A few items we need from you to start the contracting thread with Legal: 

1) Confirm preferred template: we can (A) adopt Redwood Order Form Template v3 and add your SOW as Exhibit A, or (B) take your SOW and map our billing clauses into it. 
2) Party details (legal full name, billing entity, tax ID, primary signer name/email).
3) Any non-standard procurement terms (PO requirements, invoicing cadence, mandatory SLAs).
4) Residency or data handling requirements (do you need EU residency or customer-managed key?).

We have an internal goal to produce an initial redline set within 3 business days after we have party data and your SOW mapped — can you confirm whether that timeline works on your side?

Attachments: OrderForm_Template_v3.docx (Redwood), Exhibit-Billing-Draft.docx (Redwood).

If helpful I can loop in Kimberly (contracts) now for a short intake call.

Best,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
Attach: OrderForm_Template_v3.docx; Exhibit-Billing-Draft.docx

---

Message 2/4
From: Ava Ramos <ava.ramos@novatrack.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Contracts Team <legal@novatrack.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 12:43:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Order Form + MSA intake: template selection, billing exhibit, and timeline

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the docs. Attaching our SOW (Novatrack_SOW_V1.pdf). A few quick answers: 
- We prefer option B: use our SOW as the primary doc and map your billing clauses into it.
- Party info below: Novatrack, Inc. (legal entity), billing: Novatrack, Inc. EIN 12-3456789. Primary signer: Ava Ramos (ava.ramos@novatrack.com).
- We do require vendor SOC 2 Type II evidence and a short questionnaire (I can share the PDF).
- Data residency: US only for now; no EU residency requirement.
- PO: yes, we typically issue a PO; invoice net 30.

We can do a 30-min intake call tomorrow afternoon if that helps accelerate.

Attachments: Novatrack_SOW_V1.pdf, Novatrack_Security_Questionnaire.pdf (shared with Contracts team).

Thanks,
Ava Ramos
Head of Business Ops, Novatrack

On Mon, Jun 8, 2026 at 9:12 AM Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Ava —
> 
> Thanks again for the demo last week...

---

Message 3/4 (internal)
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order Form + MSA intake: template selection, billing exhibit, and timeline

Logan / Marissa / Karthik —

Thanks for the ping and for the attachments. Recommendation and immediate asks from Legal: 

Recommendation: Use Novatrack SOW as primary doc (their preference B). We will: (1) fold our billing exhibit into their SOW as Exhibit B, (2) keep Redwood MSA references for liability/DP, and (3) propose a short Appendix for usage-based tiers. 

Priority items to resolve before initial redline:
- Confirm invoicing cadence / PO handling (they said PO + net30; we should mark billing contact and e-invoice requirement).
- SOC 2 evidence: request our security team to provide the SOC 2 report and a short summary. I'll add it to the risk file and request the minimal questionnaire answers.
- Residency: US only — no additional clauses needed, but confirm no cross-border subprocessors.

Proposed timeline (internal):
- Today: Legal to prepare initial redline mapping (change log + clause map). Owner: Kimberly.
- +1 business day after receiving Novatrack_Security_Questionnaire: Security to respond with SOC2 summary. Owner: Karthik (cc security).
- +3 business days: deliver initial redlines to Logan for commercial review, then send to customer.

Checklist for intake packet (for our side):
- [ ] Party legal name / billing entity (provided)
- [ ] Signed SOW or draft SOW (attached)
- [ ] Security questionnaire (attached)
- [ ] PO/invoicing notes (net30 + PO)
- [ ] Identify required Exhibits (Billing + SOW)

Please assign who will own uploading the redlines into the HubSpot deal record (link please). Also flag any required concessions up-front (we should identify if they want SLA changes).

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference

---

Message 4/4
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Ava Ramos <ava.ramos@novatrack.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Contracts Team <legal@novatrack.com>
Date: Tue, 9 Jun 2026 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order Form + MSA intake: template selection, billing exhibit, and timeline

Hi Ava —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and for sharing the SOW and questionnaire. Per Kimberly's note we'll map your billing into Exhibit B and push a short Appendix for usage tiers so billing is explicit. Next steps: 

1) Kimberly's team will produce an initial redline and clause map by EOD tomorrow (9 Jun).
2) Karthik will coordinate SOC2 evidence — expect a one-page summary from Security within 24 hours after they receive the questionnaire copy.
3) I'll schedule a 30-min intake call for 10 Jun 2026 at 11:00 AM PT to walk through billing tiers and PO flow — please confirm that works for you.

If you want us to mark any items as non-negotiable (SLA targets, liability caps, termination rights), flag them on the call so we can escalate early.

Doc links: HubSpot deal (https://hubspot.company/deals/DEAL-73829) — I'll upload the initial redline there and include a DocuSign envelope link when ready.

Logan

source.subject: Next steps: clarify payer, economic buyer, and finance signoff
messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aperturehealth.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@aperturehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jun 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Next steps: clarify payer, economic buyer, and finance signoff

Thanks again for taking the time today — great conversation. Quick recap + the items we need to lock down to move to quote/procurement:

- Usage profile: you mentioned peak batch ingestion + 100k real-time calls/day. Please confirm estimated monthly token volume (ballpark OK).
- Budget range: you said there's a FY27 allocation for conversational infra — can you confirm the budget band (e.g., $150k-$350k / $350k-$650k)?
- Economic buyer and finance approver: who signs contracts vs who signs POs? We need the name, title, and preferred billing entity.
- Target $/1M tokens: useful for pricing scenarios — any internal target (e.g., $800-$1,200 per 1M tokens)?

I've attached our quick 1M-token scenario workbook and my discovery notes so you can see the numbers I want to run once you confirm the two points above.

Next steps I propose:
1) You confirm budget band + economic buyer/finance contact (reply to this thread)
2) We (Redwood) produce a short commercial matrix & a draft MSA + SOW for review (aiming end of week)
3) Lauren will open a quote and route to finance once we have payer entity

If easier, Carlos — can you confirm who owns the budget and who we should route the commercial to?

Thanks —
Jonas Weber
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: redwood-1m-scenarios.xlsx (spreadsheet), discovery-notes.pdf (notes)
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aperturehealth.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@aperturehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jun 2026 17:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Next steps: clarify payer, economic buyer, and finance signoff

Jonas — thanks for the recap and the workbook. Quick answers from our side: 

- Monthly token expectation: we're comfortable saying 20M–35M tokens/month in year 1 depending on throttles. Peak month could be ~45M tokens.
- Budget band: leadership has earmarked $300k–$450k for the initial project in FY27 (covers infra + model access).
- Economic buyer: Carlos Mendes, Head of Platform (he signs off on vendor selection).
- Finance approver / PO owner: I manage vendor onboarding but PO routing goes to Aisha Shah (Finance Ops). I'll introduce Aisha once Carlos signs the selection.
- Target $/1M tokens: internal target to keep OPEX below $1,000 per 1M tokens; ideally around $900 if we can get caching/batching gains.

We need: a short commercial matrix (SaaS vs reserved), a clear billing entity (Aperture Health, Inc.), and a summary of compliance attestations for SOC2 data handling for patient pseudonymized data.

Carlos — can you confirm you are the economic buyer and whether you prefer a committed reserved path vs a usage-first pilot?

Thanks —
Priya Shah
Director, ML Platforms | Aperture Health
priya.shah@aperturehealth.com
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aperturehealth.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@aperturehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jun 2026 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Next steps: clarify payer, economic buyer, and finance signoff

Appreciate the quick clarification, Priya — very helpful. Carlos — thanks in advance for confirming your preference on committed vs pilot; that will determine the commercial path we sketch.

I'll kick off the commercial matrix and draft MSA/SOW based on the $300k–$450k band and the ~20–35M/month token range and target ~$900/1M token guidance.

Looping in Lauren (sales ops) and Kimberly (commercial/legal) to prepare:
- short commercial matrix (SaaS consumption vs 12-month reserved capacity scenarios)
- preliminary quote that routes to finance (need billing entity & PO info)
- compliance checklist for SOC2 scope and data handling notes

I'll attach a one-page mutual-action-plan to this thread for sign-off on timeline and deliverables. Looking to get you a formal quote + docs by Thurs EOD if we have payer/PO info by Wed.

Jonas

On Mon, Jun 7, 2026 at 3:02 PM Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks again for taking the time today — great conversation. Quick recap + the items we need to lock down to move to quote/procurement:
> ...
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aperturehealth.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@aperturehealth.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Jun 2026 09:11:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Next steps: clarify payer, economic buyer, and finance signoff

Thanks — looping finance (Logan) to prepare routing once we have the billing entity and PO cadence. A few items we'll need from Aperture to generate the formal quote and route to procurement: 

- Legal billing entity (exact legal name) and Fed/Tax ID (if applicable)
- Point of contact for procurement and whether Aperture will accept a standard Redwood MSA or needs redlines up front
- Preferred billing cadence (monthly/quarterly/annual) and whether a PO will be issued for reserved capacity

Action items for Redwood (we will take these):
- Create a commercial matrix (Jonas/Kim will draft pricing scenarios based on the 20–35M token range)
- Draft quote and attach standard MSA + SOW (I'll open quote in CPQ and route to Logan for review)
- Include a line item for SOC2 evidence and a short notes section for pseudonymized patient data handling

Priya/Carlos — if you can reply with the exact billing entity name and confirm whether Aisha Shah will be the finance approver we can move forward with the quote.

Lauren Bishop
Sr. Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com

Attachments: mutual-action-plan.docx (for timeline)

source.subject: Net45 proposal, staged invoices and UK VAT recovery alignment
messages:
From: Simon Lewis <simon.lewis@novaway.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: finance@novaway.com
Date: Wed, 03 Jun 2026 14:12:00 +0100
Subject: RE: pricing and contract questions (Net terms)

Hi Logan,

Thanks for the call earlier — finance pushed back on Net30 for the pilot. They asked whether Redwood can consider Net45 or Net60, and whether we can provide staged invoices tied to milestone acceptance. Also, because some of our work will be delivered into the UK entity, they need VAT invoices showing the supplier tax ID and the correct invoice entity.

Can you send a proposal for a payment cadence we can show to our CFO? Ideally something that includes:
- Deposit or prepay (if required)
- Milestone invoices and amounts
- Any credit threshold or early-pay discount you can offer

Appreciate a quick turn — we need to present options by EOD Thursday.

Best,
Simon
Finance Director
Novaway Ltd

--
attachment: Novaway_Questions_0603.docx (docx)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Simon Lewis <simon.lewis@novaway.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 03 Jun 2026 10:45:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: RE: pricing and contract questions (Net terms)

Simon — thanks for the note. Quick summary below and an attached draft invoice schedule for your review.

High-level proposal (pilot):
- Deposit: 20% of contracted pilot value due on SOW signature (applied to first invoice).
- Net terms: Net45 after invoice date (we can stretch to Net60 with a small fee; see optional tier).
- Milestones: 3 invoices tied to delivery checkpoints (kickoff/dev-complete/production handoff).
- Credit threshold: For Net45 we can provide a running credit line of $75k; for Net60 the credit line drops to $35k or alternative of 30% deposit.
- Early-pay: 1% discount if paid within 10 days.

VAT / tax routing notes:
- We will issue invoices from Redwood US by default but can route UK-facing invoices from our UK subsidiary if you supply the UK VAT registration for Novaway and confirm the billing entity.
- For VAT recovery we need: Novaway VAT number, billing entity name and address, and the customer contact for tax. We will include supplier tax ID on all invoices.

Next steps:
1) Please confirm preferred net window (Net45 or Net60 + fee).
2) Send VAT registration and billing entity details.
3) I'll loop in Marcus from Finance to finalize the credit threshold.

Attached: Redwood-Invoice-Schedule.xlsx (proposed amounts & dates), Draft SOW (pdf).

Thanks — I can update and send a clean PDF for your CFO.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Head of Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

--
attachment: Redwood-Invoice-Schedule.xlsx (xlsx), Novaway_SOW_v2.pdf (pdf)
From: Simon Lewis <simon.lewis@novaway.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: finance@novaway.com
Date: Thu, 04 Jun 2026 09:20:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Re: RE: pricing and contract questions (Net terms)

Logan — thanks for the clear proposal. Two immediate points from our side:

1) CFO prefers Net60 for pilot cashflow reasons, but will accept Net45 if we get a larger credit corridor.
2) We can supply the UK VAT registration and billing entity details.

To move quickly: can your team produce an alternate schedule that shows both:
- Option A: Net60, no deposit, credit corridor $30k (with an operations holdback on final 10% until PO sign-off).
- Option B: Net45, 20% deposit, credit corridor $80k, 1% early-pay discount.

From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
