Subject: Fw: Interested — pricing CTA (PayFlux) — budget indicated

Forwarding a warm CTA that hit the pricing page form this morning. Looks like a self-serve signup (trial) plus explicit budget selection (>$50k). Original message below — attaching the lead form and lead metadata. Please pick up or reassign.

Attachments: payflux-lead-form.json (lead fields + plan), screenshot-pricing-cta.png (stub)

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 at 07:58 AM -0700
To: pricing@redwood.ai
Subject: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Hey Redwood team,

We just kicked the tires on the self-serve trial and hit the pricing CTA — looks promising. We’re evaluating inference platforms for a mid-market rollout and have budget for FY27. Would like a short demo next week to see performance on short-form chat and embeddings use cases.

Company: PayFlux
Role: Head of Eng (me)
Employees: 120
Estimated monthly inference spend: $5k-$10k to start (scaling)

Thanks,
Samir
From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Hi Samir — great to meet you, and thanks for trying the trial. I’m Logan on the commercial team; happy to run a focused demo tailored to your use cases (short-form chat latency and embeddings at scale).

Quick options:
- 30-min technical demo + Q&A (recommended)
- 60-min walkthrough with ops and pricing deep dive

Pick a time that works for you: https://calendly.com/logan-wright/redwood-demo-30
Or send a few times and I’ll send a calendar invite.

If you can, include a short note on the most important SLOs (latency vs. throughput) and any compliance needs. I’ll loop in our solutions engineer if you want a deeper kernel/quantization discussion.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference
logan_wright@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142
From: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@payflux.com
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Hi Logan — thanks for the quick reply. I grabbed your 10:00 AM PT slot on Tuesday from Calendly but procurement asked me to loop them in early — they may want a short pricing checklist before the call.

A few prep notes so we can make the demo efficient:
- Primary workload: short customer-support style chat (avg 40 tokens/request; occasional 2–3k token context for escalation)
- Expected concurrency initially: 30–40 qps at peak, ramping over quarters
- Compliance: SOC2 + retention controls are a must

Also — is there a quick ballpark for committed reserved capacity discounts vs hosted pay-as-you-go? If you can include a pricing reference that’d be helpful.

Samir
Head of Engineering | PayFlux
From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Thanks for the context, Samir — that helps a lot. I saw your Calendly booking for Tue 10:00 AM PT (inv sent). I’ve updated the invite to include a 15-minute pricing + procurement pre-brief at the start so your procurement team can join briefly if needed.

Attached is a short agenda (redwood-demo-agenda.pdf) — proposed:
- 10:00–10:10: quick intros & goals
- 10:10–10:30: demo of short-form chat + embedding inferencing (live replay of sample workload)
- 10:30–10:40: deployment & compliance overview (SOC 2, retention controls) — Ben will cover reserved capacity economics
- 10:40–10:15: Q&A + next steps (MOPs: trial extension, canary rollout plan)

Regarding discounts: we offer committed Dedicated capacity pricing for customers targeting predictable throughput — I’ll have Ben share typical tiers and an initial estimate during the call. As a ballpark, customers with predictable monthly committed spend often see 20–40% effective savings versus pay-as-you-go, depending on sequence-length distribution and batching profile. We’ll quantify this for PayFlux after a short evaluation of your expected token mix.

HubSpot link (for your reference): https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/98765

If anything changes, shout. Looking forward to it.

—
Logan Wright
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood-demo-agenda.pdf (stub)
From: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Aisha Khan <aisha@payflux.com>, procurement@payflux.com
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 15:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Perfect — thanks Logan. I moved the invite slightly to 10:30 AM PT to avoid an internal sprint standup; hope that’s OK. Aisha (Head of Product) will join us. Procurement is looped for the pricing segment.

Agenda request: can you include a short slide on tenancy options (hosted vs dedicated vs private) and any data residency constraints? We’re based in the US but have EU customers so curious about regional routing.

See you Tuesday at 10:30.

Samir

Hey Redwood team,

We just kicked the tires on the self-serve trial and hit the pricing CTA — looks promising. We’re evaluating inference platforms for a mid-market rollout and have budget for FY27. Would like a short demo next week to see performance on short-form chat and embeddings use cases.

Company: PayFlux
Role: Head of Eng (me)
Employees: 120
Estimated monthly inference spend: $5k-$10k to start (scaling)

Thanks,
Samir


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Hi Samir — great to meet you, and thanks for trying the trial. I’m Logan on the commercial team; happy to run a focused demo tailored to your use cases (short-form chat latency and embeddings at scale).

Quick options:
- 30-min technical demo + Q&A (recommended)
- 60-min walkthrough with ops and pricing deep dive

Pick a time that works for you: https://calendly.com/logan-wright/redwood-demo-30
Or send a few times and I’ll send a calendar invite.

If you can, include a short note on the most important SLOs (latency vs. throughput) and any compliance needs. I’ll loop in our solutions engineer if you want a deeper kernel/quantization discussion.

Cheers,
Logan Wright
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference
logan_wright@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142


From: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@payflux.com
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Hi Logan — thanks for the quick reply. I grabbed your 10:00 AM PT slot on Tuesday from Calendly but procurement asked me to loop them in early — they may want a short pricing checklist before the call.

A few prep notes so we can make the demo efficient:
- Primary workload: short customer-support style chat (avg 40 tokens/request; occasional 2–3k token context for escalation)
- Expected concurrency initially: 30–40 qps at peak, ramping over quarters
- Compliance: SOC2 + retention controls are a must

Also — is there a quick ballpark for committed reserved capacity discounts vs hosted pay-as-you-go? If you can include a pricing reference that’d be helpful.

Samir
Head of Engineering | PayFlux


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Thanks for the context, Samir — that helps a lot. I saw your Calendly booking for Tue 10:00 AM PT (inv sent). I’ve updated the invite to include a 15-minute pricing + procurement pre-brief at the start so your procurement team can join briefly if needed.

Attached is a short agenda (redwood-demo-agenda.pdf) — proposed:
- 10:00–10:10: quick intros & goals
- 10:10–10:30: demo of short-form chat + embedding inferencing (live replay of sample workload)
- 10:30–10:40: deployment & compliance overview (SOC 2, retention controls) — Ben will cover reserved capacity economics
- 10:40–10:15: Q&A + next steps (MOPs: trial extension, canary rollout plan)

Regarding discounts: we offer committed Dedicated capacity pricing for customers targeting predictable throughput — I’ll have Ben share typical tiers and an initial estimate during the call. As a ballpark, customers with predictable monthly committed spend often see 20–40% effective savings versus pay-as-you-go, depending on sequence-length distribution and batching profile. We’ll quantify this for PayFlux after a short evaluation of your expected token mix.

HubSpot link (for your reference): https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/98765

If anything changes, shout. Looking forward to it.

—
Logan Wright
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood-demo-agenda.pdf (stub)


From: Samir Khatri <samir@payflux.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Aisha Khan <aisha@payflux.com>, procurement@payflux.com
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 15:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood — demo + pricing

Perfect — thanks Logan. I moved the invite slightly to 10:30 AM PT to avoid an internal sprint standup; hope that’s OK. Aisha (Head of Product) will join us. Procurement is looped for the pricing segment.

Agenda request: can you include a short slide on tenancy options (hosted vs dedicated vs private) and any data residency constraints? We’re based in the US but have EU customers so curious about regional routing.

See you Tuesday at 10:30.

Samir

source.subject: Convertible credit ladder & commitment alignment — StellarNet renewal options
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lo <sarah.lo@stellarnet.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Convertible credit ladder & commitment alignment — StellarNet renewal options

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for the conversation last week. Based on our kickoff call and your usage profile, I sketched three renewal structures that try to balance predictability for StellarNet and flexibility as your usage grows:

1) Annualized multi-year (3yr) with step-up commit: predictable lower unit price with pre-negotiated annual increases tied to committed throughput. Includes a 10% burst buffer each year.

2) Convertible credit ladder (proposal attached): an initial committed bucket plus a ladder of progressive credits that convert into prepaid capacity if usage exceeds thresholds. Credits act as both discounting and a short runway to absorb spikes without immediate contract rework.

3) Single-year co-term with seasonal burst addenda: co-term to your fiscal year with defined burst windows and true-up at year-end.

I've attached a spreadsheet that shows example unit economics and invoice cadence for each. Quick asks: which of these feels most aligned with StellarNet's procurement preferences, and who should be the primary procurement contact for contract redlines?

If helpful, I'm available for a 30m call Wednesday afternoon to walk through the credit ladder logic — it is a bit different from our standard ramp.

Thanks,
Logan
Logan Wright
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192
Redwood Inference

Attachments: 2026-06-01_stellarnet_term_options.xlsx (spreadsheet)
From: Sarah Lo <sarah.lo@stellarnet.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: StellarNet Procurement <procurement@stellarnet.com>, Ethan Zhao <ezhao@stellarnet.com>
Date: 2026-06-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Convertible credit ladder & commitment alignment — StellarNet renewal options

Logan — thanks for the clear framing and the spreadsheet. I like the convertible credit ladder idea conceptually; it gives us flexibility during the product launch season next year. A few clarifying points from our side: 

- We need co-terming to our fiscal calendar (starts 10/01) so any multi-year option should be alignable to that date.
- Procurement will need clear language on how credits convert and whether converted credits are refundable or only applied to future capacity.
- We would want an option for a short trial burst in Q1 post-renewal (1–2 weeks) up to 150% of committed throughput.

I'll introduce our procurement lead (procurement@stellarnet.com) and legal (ezhao@stellarnet.com) on the next note. Can you provide a one-page summary we can share internally that outlines: conversion triggers, billing mechanics, and a representative P&L impact?

Thanks — Sarah
Sarah Lo
Head of AI Product, StellarNet

-----Original Message-----
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Sent: Tuesday, June 1, 2026 9:12 AM
To: Sarah Lo <sarah.lo@stellarnet.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Subject: Convertible credit ladder & commitment alignment — StellarNet renewal options
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-02T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: StellarNet renewal — need pricing + contract guidance

Team — forwarding Sarah's reply (below). She's asking for a one-pager that explains the convertible credit ladder mechanics and conversion triggers, and procurement/legal want clarity on refundability and billing. Key constraints: co-term to 2026-10-01, trial burst window (1–2 weeks at 150% of committed), and want conversion to be automatic if usage crosses threshold for >30 days.

Can we put together: a) short one-pager for the customer, b) proposed contract language for the conversion mechanics, c) an invoice cadence example showing how credits would amortize if converted into prepay?

Marissa — would you lead customer comms. Karthik — pricing/legal language. Neha — finance true-up invoicing and GAAP-friendly treatment.

Thanks,
Logan

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Sarah Lo <sarah.lo@stellarnet.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: StellarNet Procurement <procurement@stellarnet.com>, Ethan Zhao <ezhao@stellarnet.com>
Date: 2026-06-01 11:03 AM
Subject: Re: Convertible credit ladder & commitment alignment — StellarNet renewal options

(see above)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-03T10:51:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: StellarNet renewal — proposed language and pricing examples

Agreed on splits of work — attaching a redline that sketches the convertible credit ladder mechanics and sample language for automatic conversion. High-level points: 

- Conversion trigger: rolling 30-day average utilization > 110% of committed throughput for a continuous 10 business-day window, OR cumulative overage > 25% of committed in a quarter.
- Converted credits: become prepaid capacity applied on a pro-rata basis to the remaining term; non-refundable but can be reallocated across teams under the same contract.
- Trial burst: one 14-day burst window per 12-month period up to 150% committed; invoiced monthly with true-up for any usage beyond credited burst.
- Co-terming: offer an alignment amendment so the renewal effective date becomes 2026-10-01; billing schedules shown in the attached sample.

Pricing impact table (excerpt): first-year blended unit price ~9% lower on 3yr ladder vs single-year co-term, with effective protection for 18 months before step-ups begin. Precise numbers in the redline.

I recommend we present the one-pager to procurement and include the redline as draft legal language. If they push for refundability, we can consider a capped small refundable balance (<=5% of committed) for enterprise customers — propose to reserve to exec approval.

Attachment: karthik_convertible_credit_ladder_redline.docx

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Sr. Director, Pricing & Commercial Ops | Redwood Inference
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lo <sarah.lo@stellarnet.com>, StellarNet Procurement <procurement@stellarnet.com>, Ethan Zhao <ezhao@stellarnet.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-04T16:58:00-07:00
Subject: Consolidated proposal: convertible ladder + co-term alignment (one-page + next steps)

Hi Sarah / team — thanks for the input and for looping procurement/legal in. Attached is a concise one-page summary and a proposed path forward:

- Structure: 36-month convertible credit ladder (co-term amendment to 2026-10-01) with annual step-down pricing and one 14-day trial burst permitted in year one.
- Conversion mechanics: automatic conversion triggers as discussed (rolling 30-day avg > 110% OR cumulative quarterly overage > 25%). Converted credits are non-refundable, applied pro-rata to remaining term, reassignable within the account.
- Billing / invoicing: monthly invoices with an end-of-quarter true-up. If credits convert mid-quarter they will be amortized across remaining monthly invoices in that quarter. Finance has confirmed GAAP-friendly treatment for prepaid application (cc Neha).
- Next steps: confirm procurement contact (procurement@stellarnet.com), legal to provide redlines on the attached language within 5 business days, and schedule a 30-minute call to finalize demo burst window scheduling.

I'll attach the one-pager and the redline now — please review and let me know availability for a call next week. If procurement needs alternative refund language, Karthik suggested keeping refundable exposure <=5% of committed spend and escalating to exec approval.

Thanks,
Logan

Attachments: term_summary_StellarNet_20260604.pdf (overview), karthik_convertible_credit_ladder_redline.docx (redline)

To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@bluewire.ai>
Cc: Henry Cole <henry.cole@bluewire.ai>
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Intro + demo — Bluewire inference use case

Hi Alicia —

Thanks again for the time on Monday. Based on our conversation I put together a short demo plan that shows:
- end-to-end inference latency on a 2K token instruction
- cost comparison (batching + quantization)
- a brief look at prefix caching for customer flows

I'd love to run a 30–40 minute walkthrough with your engineering and product leads next week. A few windows that work for me (PT):
- Tue 5/26 10:00–10:40
- Wed 5/27 13:00–13:40
- Thu 5/28 09:00–09:40

Attached is the short demo deck and a draft SOW for reference. If none of those times work, tell me a couple that do and I'll send a calendar invite.

Best,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Account Executive

From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@bluewire.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.w@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + demo — Bluewire inference use case

Hi Logan —

Thanks for sending this over and for the clear plan. Heads up: product has just reprioritized our roadmap for the next two quarters. The PM who was championing the LLM/inference pilot (Markus) is being pulled to a compliance initiative and our VP has reprioritized headcount into data-platform work. In addition, a couple of our key folks are on an extended customer roadshow and will be out through mid-June.

For those reasons we need to pause scheduling the demo for now. We still want to keep Redwood on our radar — we think the use case is compelling — but we need to deconflict timing internally first. Please keep this thread open and check back in late June or early July.

Apologies for the last-minute change — we know this is not ideal.

—Alicia
Alicia Gomez
Director of Product, Bluewire AI

From: Logan Wright <logan.w@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.o@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.m@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 09:21:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Bluewire demo — prospect deprioritized (travel + roadmap)

Vanessa / Rishi —

Forwarding Alicia's note. Short summary: product reallocated headcount and core team is traveling; they want to pause scheduling until late June / early July. Prospect explicitly asked we check back then.

Questions:
1) Do we want to offer a short warm-credit (sandbox tokens / 30-day trial) to keep them engaged while they sort priorities?
2) Any objections to marking this deal as "nurture / back-burner" in HubSpot and setting an automated reminder for 2026-07-05?

I'll leave the SOW and deck attached for the thread (keeping for reference).

Thanks,
Logan

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.o@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.w@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.m@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Bluewire demo — prospect deprioritized (travel + roadmap)

Logan —

Thanks for the heads up and for the suggested next steps. I agree we should not push for a full demo slot while they are stretched. Two quick asks:

- Mark deal DW-4321 as "nurture/back-burner" in HubSpot and set a task for 2026-07-06 to re-engage (assign to you).
- Offer a small sandbox credit (I authorize up to $1,500 equivalent) and a 60-day touchpoint. If they accept, log credit in HubSpot notes and set an internal follow-up for 30 days.

Please do not send extra collateral-heavy material; keep outreach light and timed. If Alicia asks for any security materials (SOC2, data handling), loop in Rishi and he can handle the doc pack.

Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference

From: Logan Wright <logan.w@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@bluewire.ai>, Henry Cole <henry.cole@bluewire.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.o@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Jun 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + demo — Bluewire inference use case

Hi Alicia —

Totally understood and thank you for the quick note. We can absolutely pause scheduling until your team is less pulled (travel + roadmap reprioritization makes total sense).

To stay helpful without adding noise, two options that might be useful while you regroup:
1) I can provision a small sandbox credit (no strings) so your engineers can run a couple of quick latency/cost tests on sample flows. Vanessa approved up to $1,500.
2) We can set a tentative 30-minute check-in for the week of 2026-07-06 — I will only hold a tentative slot and will not push if your PMs are still blocked.

If either of those sounds good, reply here and I'll take care of the setup. We'll keep the SOW and the short demo deck on file and won't send additional collateral unless you request it.

Thanks again, and hope the roadshow goes well.

Best,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Re: Cancellation fee offset + EU withholding credit reissue
messages:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: billing@clearsky.health
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the quick onboarding help last month. I'm reviewing our May invoice and have two items I need your help with:

1) We were charged a $3,000 cancellation fee for an early termination of a test cluster we spun down May 10. That charge feels unexpected since we followed the migration checklist and provided 30 days' notice. Can you confirm the contract clause?

2) Our AP team is asking for an EU withholding statement for the 20% tax they withheld on the payout to our reseller in Germany. We need any supporting evidence Redwood can provide so we can claim the credit locally.

I've attached the invoice (ClearSky_invoice_May2026.pdf) and the payment remittance showing the withholding. Can we get a credit memo for the cancellation fee if that charge shouldn't have applied? If not, can you explain the assessment timing?

Appreciate it — happy to hop on a short call if useful.

Best,
Elena
Sr. Finance Manager
ClearSky Health

---
Attachment: ClearSky_invoice_May2026.pdf (PDF, 210KB)
Attachment: payment_remit_may.csv (CSV, referenced)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Elena —

Thanks for the heads up and for sending the docs. Quick summary from our side after an initial review:

- The $3,000 line is a pro-rated early-termination fee that was auto-applied when the test cluster was deprovisioned May 10. The contract does include a clause for prorated termination during the committed period (Section 4.2). That said, I can see how the timing could be confusing given the migration logs.

- Regarding the EU withholding: we don't normally provide tax receipts for withholdings on reseller payouts because Redwood treats those as upstream partner settlements. However, we do provide a withholding confirmation letter and a credit memo in cases where the withholding was applied in error or when the reseller provides evidence.

Next steps I propose:
- Marissa (cc'd) to validate the termination log and confirm whether the charge should be reversed. If it should be reversed we will issue a goodwill credit memo.
- Karthik (cc'd) in Finance to gather a withholding confirmation letter and work with Elena on any evidence exchange required for their AP team.

Can you confirm if ClearSky has the reseller contract with the partner who handled the payout? If so, please share the remittance with the partner name redacted if needed. We'll treat any sensitive attachments as restricted.

I'll mark this as high priority and aim to come back with a resolution by Wednesday.

Regards,
Logan Wright
Head of Customer Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: (referenced) ClearSky_invoice_May2026.pdf

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jun 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Logan / Elena —

I pulled the termination events from the provisioning logs and the customer request. Notes below:

- ClearSky submitted a termination via the Console on April 9 (Ticket #RT-9981). Our system logged a 30-day notice window starting April 10; the cluster was deprovisioned May 10 as expected.
- The early-termination fee was applied automatically because the subscription was a 12-month committed plan and the customer had a remaining prorated commitment. That matches the contract language.

However: after checking the migration request, the customer selected the "migration-exemption" checkbox and attached a migration confirmation from our Solutions team (ticket Sol-612). That exemption flag failed to propagate to billing when the provisioning event executed — looks like an edge-case bug in the migration->billing sync path.

Given that evidence, I recommend the following remedial actions:
1) Issue a full credit for the $3,000 cancellation fee as goodwill (credit memo attached).
2) Reissue the May invoice without the cancellation line and mark the original as superseded.
3) Produce a withholding confirmation letter (Karthik) once ClearSky provides the reseller proof/shows the partner name.

Elena — I'll attach a draft credit memo (redwood_credit_memo_CSD-4821.pdf) for your review. If that looks good we'll process it against their account and flag the payment for AP to offset.

Marissa
Sr. Manager, Billing Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: redwood_credit_memo_CSD-4821.pdf (PDF, 45KB)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 03 Jun 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Elena / team —

Thanks for the quick confirmation, Elena. For the EU withholding, here's what we can provide to help ClearSky reclaim the withheld tax:

- A withholding confirmation letter that states Redwood did not withhold at the invoice level; rather the 20% was applied by your reseller/payee in the EU. The letter will include the invoice number, payment date, and a note that Redwood will cooperate with any audit requests.
- If the reseller provided evidence showing the withholding was applied in error (bank remittance or partner settlement statement), we'll issue a compensating credit for the withheld amount to help you net to the correct figure.

Please upload the reseller remittance (or share a redacted copy) to the Drive link below and I will prepare the formal letter. If you prefer, we can also package both the credit memo and withholding confirmation into a single bundle for AP.

Upload link: https://drive.redwood.ai/shared/clearsky/credit-memo-draft

I'll also attach the withholding evidence zip (withholding_evidence_ClearSky.zip) to our internal ticket and start the paperwork. Expect the formal letters by EOD Friday once we have the reseller doc.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Finance Manager, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: withholding_evidence_ClearSky.zip (ZIP, referenced)

1) We were charged a $3,000 cancellation fee for an early termination of a test cluster we spun down May 10. That charge feels unexpected since we followed the migration checklist and provided 30 days' notice. Can you confirm the contract clause?

2) Our AP team is asking for an EU withholding statement for the 20% tax they withheld on the payout to our reseller in Germany. We need any supporting evidence Redwood can provide so we can claim the credit locally.

I've attached the invoice (ClearSky_invoice_May2026.pdf) and the payment remittance showing the withholding. Can we get a credit memo for the cancellation fee if that charge shouldn't have applied? If not, can you explain the assessment timing?

Appreciate it — happy to hop on a short call if useful.

Best,
Elena
Sr. Finance Manager
ClearSky Health

---
Attachment: ClearSky_invoice_May2026.pdf (PDF, 210KB)
Attachment: payment_remit_may.csv (CSV, referenced)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Elena —

Thanks for the heads up and for sending the docs. Quick summary from our side after an initial review:

- The $3,000 line is a pro-rated early-termination fee that was auto-applied when the test cluster was deprovisioned May 10. The contract does include a clause for prorated termination during the committed period (Section 4.2). That said, I can see how the timing could be confusing given the migration logs.

- Regarding the EU withholding: we don't normally provide tax receipts for withholdings on reseller payouts because Redwood treats those as upstream partner settlements. However, we do provide a withholding confirmation letter and a credit memo in cases where the withholding was applied in error or when the reseller provides evidence.

Next steps I propose:
- Marissa (cc'd) to validate the termination log and confirm whether the charge should be reversed. If it should be reversed we will issue a goodwill credit memo.
- Karthik (cc'd) in Finance to gather a withholding confirmation letter and work with Elena on any evidence exchange required for their AP team.

Can you confirm if ClearSky has the reseller contract with the partner who handled the payout? If so, please share the remittance with the partner name redacted if needed. We'll treat any sensitive attachments as restricted.

I'll mark this as high priority and aim to come back with a resolution by Wednesday.

Regards,
Logan Wright
Head of Customer Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: (referenced) ClearSky_invoice_May2026.pdf

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jun 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Logan / Elena —

I pulled the termination events from the provisioning logs and the customer request. Notes below:

- ClearSky submitted a termination via the Console on April 9 (Ticket #RT-9981). Our system logged a 30-day notice window starting April 10; the cluster was deprovisioned May 10 as expected.
- The early-termination fee was applied automatically because the subscription was a 12-month committed plan and the customer had a remaining prorated commitment. That matches the contract language.

However: after checking the migration request, the customer selected the "migration-exemption" checkbox and attached a migration confirmation from our Solutions team (ticket Sol-612). That exemption flag failed to propagate to billing when the provisioning event executed — looks like an edge-case bug in the migration->billing sync path.

Given that evidence, I recommend the following remedial actions:
1) Issue a full credit for the $3,000 cancellation fee as goodwill (credit memo attached).
2) Reissue the May invoice without the cancellation line and mark the original as superseded.
3) Produce a withholding confirmation letter (Karthik) once ClearSky provides the reseller proof/shows the partner name.

Elena — I'll attach a draft credit memo (redwood_credit_memo_CSD-4821.pdf) for your review. If that looks good we'll process it against their account and flag the payment for AP to offset.

Marissa
Sr. Manager, Billing Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: redwood_credit_memo_CSD-4821.pdf (PDF, 45KB)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@clearsky.health>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 03 Jun 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice + cancellation fee question

Elena / team —

Thanks for the quick confirmation, Elena. For the EU withholding, here's what we can provide to help ClearSky reclaim the withheld tax:

- A withholding confirmation letter that states Redwood did not withhold at the invoice level; rather the 20% was applied by your reseller/payee in the EU. The letter will include the invoice number, payment date, and a note that Redwood will cooperate with any audit requests.
- If the reseller provided evidence showing the withholding was applied in error (bank remittance or partner settlement statement), we'll issue a compensating credit for the withheld amount to help you net to the correct figure.

Please upload the reseller remittance (or share a redacted copy) to the Drive link below and I will prepare the formal letter. If you prefer, we can also package both the credit memo and withholding confirmation into a single bundle for AP.

Upload link: https://drive.redwood.ai/shared/clearsky/credit-memo-draft

I'll also attach the withholding evidence zip (withholding_evidence_ClearSky.zip) to our internal ticket and start the paperwork. Expect the formal letters by EOD Friday once we have the reseller doc.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Finance Manager, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: withholding_evidence_ClearSky.zip (ZIP, referenced)

source.subject: PO field mandates, proposed net90 carveout & weekly invoice buckets
messages:
From: Amrita Saini <amrita.saini@kestrelhealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@kestrelhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: PO requirements + discount banding question

Hi Logan,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Kestrel's procurement team reviewed the SOW and we have two gating items before we can route for our AP approval:

1) PO field enforcement: our ERP needs a unique Purchase Order line attribute called "CostCenter|BU" and a 16-char PO reference. Can you confirm the PO header/line fields Redwood requires on invoices?

2) Net terms: we typically run net90 for enterprise contracts but have a carveout path for essential platform credits to be net30. We want to avoid prepayments but can consider a 20% prepay if a monthly invoice cadence is not available.

Also a packaging question on discounts — the pricing exhibit references a volume discount at $200k ARR tiers. Is there flexibility to split that across entities (one PO per BU) without losing aggregate discount eligibility?

Can you loop in finance/ops so we can get a concrete PO template and an invoice cadence that maps to our weekly AP runs?

Best,
Amrita
Procurement Manager
Kestrel Health

Attachments: Kestrel_PO_template_v3.xlsx (excel)
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Amrita Saini <amrita.saini@kestrelhealth.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 08:23:00 -0700
Subject: Re: PO requirements + discount banding question

Hi Amrita — thanks for the clear note. Looping in Vanessa (finance lead) and Ben (sales ops) to align.

Short answers up front:
- PO fields: we can accept a 16-char PO ref in the header and will consume a CostCenter|BU line attribute. We'll ask accounting to map that to our ledger tag.
- Net terms: standard deal flow for this tier is net60; net90 requires finance approval. We can offer a net90 carveout for non-recurring onboarding fees but ask that platform usage charges remain net30 or be handled by weekly invoices with a 20% prepay holdback.
- Discount packaging: we can preserve aggregate discount eligibility across multiple POs if there is a master contract referencing an umbrella commitment (please include the master agreement ID on each PO).

Action items:
1) Vanessa to confirm whether we can operationally accept weekly invoice buckets and still apply volume discounts.
2) Ben to provide an invoice format sample that includes CostCenter|BU and the 16-char PO reference.
3) Logan/Amrita to iterate on the prepay vs cadence trade-off by EOD Wednesday.

Thanks —
Logan
Senior Sales Manager
Redwood Inference

On Sat, May 30, 2026 at 09:12 AM Amrita Saini <amrita.saini@kestrelhealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Logan,
> 
> Thanks again for the demo last week...
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Amrita Saini <amrita.saini@kestrelhealth.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: PO requirements + discount banding question

Hi Amrita,

Thanks for the detail. I ran this past accounting and ops — here are the constraints and options from our side: 

Constraints:
- Our billing system requires either a single-monthly invoice or weekly buckets to reconcile weekly AP runs. Daily/irregular invoices create reconciliation overhead and decline to apply credit memos consistently.
- Discounts are evaluated by contract/purchase commitment ID, not by individual PO. If multiple POs reference the same commitment ID (master agreement), we can aggregate spend for discount tiers.
- Net90 on recurring usage is elevated risk: cashflow and collections teams will require credit references and a contract amendment.

Options we can operationalize quickly:
A) Weekly invoice buckets + net30 for platform usage: We issue weekly consolidated statements per BU; Kestrel pays net30 on the weekly bucket. No prepay required; discount tiers apply across buckets if POs reference the master commitment ID.

B) Monthly invoice + 20% prepay: We invoice monthly; Kestrel makes a 20% prepayment at the start of each month to cover usage spikes, net30 on remainder. This preserves the current revenue recognition model for Redwood and is easier for our AR team.

C) Net90 carveout for one-time onboarding fees only: Onboarding/integration service invoices can be net90; platform usage must either be net30 or covered by prepay.

If Kestrel needs net90 on platform usage, we will need a 3rd-party credit review and a signed amendment (2-3 week turnaround).

Next steps proposed:
- If weekly buckets are acceptable, Ben will attach an invoice template that includes the CostCenter|BU line attribute and the master commitment ID.
- If you prefer net90, please send the credit contact and we will begin the review.

Ben, attaching the sample invoice and our pricing exhibit for Amrita to review (attached).

Regards,
Vanessa Ortiz
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-pricing-exhibit-v2.pdf, sample_invoice_weekly_buckets.csv
From: Amrita Saini <amrita.saini@kestrelhealth.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@kestrelhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 15:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: PO requirements + discount banding question

Thanks Vanessa — this is helpful and aligns with our AP model. Kestrel prefers option A (weekly invoice buckets + net30 for platform usage). A couple quick clarifications before we proceed: 

1) For multi-BU discounts: we'll send separate POs per BU. Please confirm the exact master commitment ID format you need (is it the Redwood contract number plus a dash and BU code?).
2) Can you confirm the exact header/line names for the CostCenter|BU attribute so our ERP can populate them automatically?
3) Can Redwood accept the 16-char PO reference in both header and the invoice line (our ERP populates header only by default; we can add the line attribute if you confirm the name).

If you can confirm these two small items today, we'll route the POs and sign the ticketing amendment for weekly buckets.

Best,
Amrita
Procurement Manager
Kestrel Health

On Tue, Jun 2, 2026 at 11:05 AM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Amrita,
> 
> Thanks for the detail. I ran this past accounting and ops...

source.subject: HIPAA deployment — activation fee vs recurring ops billing (CareWell)
messages:
Date: Fri, 22 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
From: Marta Silva <marta.silva@carewell.health>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@carewell.health>
Subject: Compliance packaging questions for HIPAA pilot

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the product walkthrough yesterday. We are moving the HIPAA pilot through procurement and need a few clarifications before I can sign off:

- For on-prem/VPC deployments, how do you typically split costs between a one-time activation/implementation fee and ongoing charges (HSM seats, audit-log retention)?
- We prefer capex-friendly accounting where possible — can implementation costs be amortized as credits against recurring invoices?
- Can audit-log retention be billed separately per TB/month or rolled into a "compliance" line item?

Can you send an illustrative split and any sample SOW line items? Procurement would like something they can attach to the PO.

Best,
Marta Silva
Head of Procurement, CareWell Health

(attachment: initial-requirements.pdf)

Date: Fri, 22 May 2026 12:05:00 -0700
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Silva <marta.silva@carewell.health>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Subject: Re: Compliance packaging questions for HIPAA pilot

Marta — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Ben (pricing) and Laura (legal/SOW) to propose two clean options you can take to Procurement. Attached is a quick workbook with the two constructs.

Option A — Implementation as one-time (capex):
- One-time activation & on-prem setup: $95k (covers infra onboarding, HSM provisioning, 2 weeks on-site work unless remote)
- HSM seats: $1,200/month/seat (billed monthly)
- Audit log retention: $0.18/GB/month for 90-day rolling retention; tiered pricing below 5TB
- Credits: we can offer a one-time credit equal to 30% of the activation fee if you commit to 12 months of Dedicated capacity

Option B — Amortized credits (more opex-friendly):
- Implementation invoiced as a credit: $95k amortized over 24 months as a monthly credit line (reduces recurring by ~$3,958/mo)
- HSM seats: $1,350/month/seat (slightly higher to offset amortization)
- Audit log retention: invoiced as separate line (preferred for your finance)

Questions for you:
- Do you need separate invoice lines for SOC/audit evidence and HSM seats (Procurement asked explicitly)?
- Any internal cap on one-time vendor fees we should be aware of?

Attaching: audit-log-econ.xlsx (worksheets: optionA, optionB), HSM-seat-pricing.csv

— Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Sales — Compliance & Regulated Accounts
Redwood Inference
(office) 415-555-0132

Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 08:37:00 -0700
From: Marta Silva <marta.silva@carewell.health>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@carewell.health>
Subject: Re: Compliance packaging questions for HIPAA pilot

Logan / Ben / Laura — thanks, this is helpful. Procurement gave feedback after seeing the workbook:

- They prefer amortization but capped such credits so the vendor is still paid up front for implementation costs on our balance sheet. Would Redwood accept a structure where 50% is invoiced up-front (one-time) and 50% is applied as monthly credits over 18 months?
- For HSM seats, Procurement needs a SKU-level price and wants to see a 2-50 seat tier schedule with committed discounts at 12/24 month terms.
- Audit logs: they want these as a distinct line with the option to pre-purchase retention (annual bundle) or pay-as-you-go.
- SOC2 evidence: can you attach the latest SOC2 Type II and a short statement on retention geography for the audit logs? We need that for our security review.

Also: can you add language in the SOW to indicate a single point of contact for incident escalation during the pilot?

If you can update the workbook with the 50/50 split and a basic seat tier table we can move to PO.

Regards,
Marta

Date: Tue, 26 May 2026 11:14:00 -0700

title: HIPAA deployment — activation fee vs recurring ops billing (CareWell)
preview:
Date: Fri, 22 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
From: Marta Silva <marta.silva@carewell.health>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@carewell.health>
Subject: Compliance packaging questions for HIPAA pilot

Hi Logan —

Thanks for the product walkthrough yesterday. We are moving the HIPAA pilot through procurement and need a few clarifications before I can sign off:

- For on-prem/VPC deployments, how do you typically split costs between a one-time activation/implementation fee and ongoing charges (HSM seats, audit-log retention)?
- We prefer capex-friendly accounting where possible — can implementation costs be amortized as credits against recurring invoices?
- Can audit-log retention be billed separately per TB/month or rolled into a "compliance" line item?

Can you send an illustrative split and any sample SOW line items? Procurement would like something they can attach to the PO.

Best,
Marta Silva
Head of Procurement, CareWell Health

(attachment: initial-requirements.pdf)

Date: Fri, 22 May 2026 12:05:00 -0700
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Silva <marta.silva@carewell.health>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Subject: Re: Compliance packaging questions for HIPAA pilot

Marta — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Ben (pricing) and Laura (legal/SOW) to propose two clean options you can take to Procurement. Attached is a quick workbook with the two constructs.

Option A — Implementation as one-time (capex):
- One-time activation & on-prem setup: $95k (covers infra onboarding, HSM provisioning, 2 weeks on-site work unless remote)
- HSM seats: $1,200/month/seat (billed monthly)
- Audit log retention: $0.18/GB/month for 90-day rolling retention; tiered pricing below 5TB
- Credits: we can offer a one-time credit equal to 30% of the activation fee if you commit to 12 months of Dedicated capacity

Option B — Amortized credits (more opex-friendly):
- Implementation invoiced as a credit: $95k amortized over 24 months as a monthly credit line (reduces recurring by ~$3,958/mo)
- HSM seats: $1,350/month/seat (slightly higher to offset amortization)
- Audit log retention: invoiced as separate line (preferred for your finance)

Questions for you:
- Do you need separate invoice lines for SOC/audit evidence and HSM seats (Procurement asked explicitly)?
- Any internal cap on one-time vendor fees we should be aware of?

Attaching: audit-log-econ.xlsx (worksheets: optionA, optionB), HSM-seat-pricing.csv

— Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Sales — Compliance & Regulated Accounts
Redwood Inference
(office) 415-555-0132

Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 08:37:00 -0700

To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@carewell.health>
Subject: Re: Compliance packaging questions for HIPAA pilot

Logan / Ben / Laura — thanks, this is helpful. Procurement gave feedback after seeing the workbook:

- They prefer amortization but capped such credits so the vendor is still paid up front for implementation costs on our balance sheet. Would Redwood accept a structure where 50% is invoiced up-front (one-time) and 50% is applied as monthly credits over 18 months?
- For HSM seats, Procurement needs a SKU-level price and wants to see a 2-50 seat tier schedule with committed discounts at 12/24 month terms.
- Audit logs: they want these as a distinct line with the option to pre-purchase retention (annual bundle) or pay-as-you-go.
- SOC2 evidence: can you attach the latest SOC2 Type II and a short statement on retention geography for the audit logs? We need that for our security review.

Also: can you add language in the SOW to indicate a single point of contact for incident escalation during the pilot?

If you can update the workbook with the 50/50 split and a basic seat tier table we can move to PO.

Regards,
Marta

source.subject: Agreement sequencing & go-to-close plan — HelixTX MAP and order form dependencies
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Celine Tran <celine.tran@helixtx.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 16:20:00 +0000
Subject: Agreement sequencing & go-to-close plan — HelixTX MAP and order form dependencies

Celine — thanks again for the time this week. As discussed on the call, we want to put a short mutual action plan that makes procurement and legal sequencing explicit so HelixTX can onboard in time for your Q3 pilot. High-level asks below; details and a draft MAP are attached.

Key points we want to lock:
- Order form targets the pilot SKU and usage tiers — we plan to have a draft ready for review once the MSA commercial terms are agreed.
- DPA: Helix has indicated the DPA needs to be executed prior to any customer data ingestion — we want to clarify whether Helix will require an executed DPA before signing the order form or if an executed DPA is acceptable to be completed in parallel but prior to data transfer.
- Sign order: Redwood prefers: (1) MSA (commercials & liabilities) signed, (2) Order Form countersigned, (3) DPA executed before data onboarding. If Helix procurement requires a different sequence we can adapt — just want the gating explicit in the MAP.

I attached a one-page MAP with milestones and a spreadsheet with suggested dates. Vanessa has sketched a ramp that meets your Q3 pilot window. Can you confirm procurement’s required sequencing and any internal signatory constraints (e.g., legal will not sign order forms until MSA executed)?

Thanks — I’ll follow up with Vanessa and legal to prepare the redline guidance once you confirm.

Best,
Logan Wright
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Helix_MAP_v1.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Celine Tran <celine.tran@helixtx.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Vogel <markus.v@procure.helixtx.com>, Samantha Reed <samantha.reed@helixtx.com>
Date: Sun, 31 May 2026 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Agreement sequencing & go-to-close plan — HelixTX MAP and order form dependencies

Logan — appreciate the MAP draft. Quick answers from procurement/legal side:

- Procurement preference: MSA signed first, then order form. That said, Procurement will accept a countersigned order form if legal has a signed MSA or an explicit signature plan (i.e., signed MSA executed within our internal review window).
- DPA: Our privacy team will not allow any production data ingestion until a DPA is executed. We can proceed with proof-of-concept data under an NDA or using synthetic data, but the DPA execution is a gating item before pilot data flows.
- Signatories: Helix legal requires a fully signed MSA prior to enterprise order acceptance; commercial can provision pilot access earlier with a signed order form + DPA if legal and compliance give a flag.

Procurement asks for the following items to be in the MAP so we can track and commit:
1) Target signature dates for MSA, DPA, Order Form (we need realistic slack for our internal counsel; suggest 7 business days for legal review each).
2) The primary approver names and contact info (countersignature path).
3) Evidence artifacts: SOC 2 Type II report, data retention policy, and subprocessor list.

I’m looping Markus (procurement) and Samantha (privacy) to align on acceptable sequencing. Can you include the SOC2 pointer and a copy of your standard DPA in the MAP?

Thanks,
Celine
Head of Platform Partnerships
Helix Therapeutics

Attachments referenced: N/A
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 14:35:00 +0000
Subject: DRAFT MAP and gating schedule for HelixTX (attached)

Team — I put together a tight MAP and included the procurement gating items Celine requested. Please review — I tried to balance Helix’s 7-business-day review windows with our internal legal slotting. Highlights:

MAP (summary milestones):
- 2026-06-01: Redwood sends MSA draft + redline guidance to Helix. (status: done — attached guidance PDF)
- 2026-06-08: Helix legal initial redline due (7 business days).
- 2026-06-17: Redwood legal reviews Helix redline (5 business days).
- 2026-06-25: Target MSA signature
- 2026-06-26: Order form countersigned (commercial provision for pilot access)
- 2026-06-27: DPA executed (must be in place before pilot data ingestion)

Important sequencing notes:
- We recommend the MSA be the primary contract holder — order form can be executed afterward but technically we can accept order form + executed DPA for provisioning if legal flags are open.
- If Helix requires DPA before any order form signature, we should plan for the DPA negotiation in parallel to avoid dragging the MSA timeline.
- Evidence pack (SOC2, retention, subprocessors) — I added links in the spreadsheet under the evidence column. We should proactively share SOC2 and subprocessor list with Samantha to speed compliance review.

Actionable next steps (short):
- Logan: confirm the dates and send the MAP to Celine with the attachments.
- Karthik/legal: prepare a redline playbook focused on three hot spots: indemnity cap, data processing liability, and termination remedies — I attached a one-pager guiding negotiation priorities.
- Ops: prepare provisioning checklist assuming order form countersigned + DPA executed.

Attachments: MSA_redline_guidance.pdf (pdf), Helix_MAP_v1.xlsx (xlsx)

Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Jun 2026 15:05:00 +0000
Subject: Fwd: Agreement sequencing & go-to-close plan — HelixTX MAP and order form dependencies

Karthik — forwarding the thread and Vanessa’s MAP. Can you advise on the proposed schedule and confirm the negotiation playbook? Specific asks for legal:
1) Review the proposed dates and confirm whether Redwood can commit to the 5-business-day review window after Helix returns redlines. If not, propose realistic turn times.
2) Prepare a redline that keeps data-processing liability limited, but does not block DPA execution in parallel if Helix insists.
3) Flag any clauses that would require executive approval (e.g., changes to indemnity cap or export controls).

Quoted message from Celine Tran: 
> Logan — appreciate the MAP draft. Quick answers from procurement/legal side:
> - Procurement preference: MSA signed first, then order form. That said, Procurement will accept a countersigned order form if legal has a signed MSA or an explicit signature plan (i.e., signed MSA executed within our internal review window).
> - DPA: Our privacy team will not allow any production data ingestion until a DPA is executed. We can proceed with proof-of-concept data under an NDA or using synthetic data, but the DPA execution is a gating item before pilot data flows.
> - Signatories: Helix legal requires a fully signed MSA prior to enterprise order acceptance; commercial can provision pilot access earlier with a signed order form + DPA if legal and compliance give a flag.

Please prioritize — Helix is targeting a late-June pilot kickoff and procurement is driving the timeline.

Thanks,
Logan
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments forwarded: MSA_redline_guidance.pdf (pdf), Helix_MAP_v1.xlsx (xlsx)

- We recommend the MSA be the primary contract holder — order form can be executed afterward but technically we can accept order form + executed DPA for provisioning if legal flags are open.
- If Helix requires DPA before any order form signature, we should plan for the DPA negotiation in parallel to avoid dragging the MSA timeline.
- Evidence pack (SOC2, retention, subprocessors) — I added links in the spreadsheet under the evidence column. We should proactively share SOC2 and subprocessor list with Samantha to speed compliance review.

Actionable next steps (short):
- Logan: confirm the dates and send the MAP to Celine with the attachments.
- Karthik/legal: prepare a redline playbook focused on three hot spots: indemnity cap, data processing liability, and termination remedies — I attached a one-pager guiding negotiation priorities.
- Ops: prepare provisioning checklist assuming order form countersigned + DPA executed.

Vanessa Ortiz

text:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Celine Tran <celine.tran@helixtx.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 May 2026 16:20:00 +0000
Subject: Agreement sequencing & go-to-close plan — HelixTX MAP and order form dependencies

Celine — thanks again for the time this week. As discussed on the call, we want to put a short mutual action plan that makes procurement and legal sequencing explicit so HelixTX can onboard in time for your Q3 pilot. High-level asks below; details and a draft MAP are attached.

Key points we want to lock:
- Order form targets the pilot SKU and usage tiers — we plan to have a draft ready for review once the MSA commercial terms are agreed.
- DPA: Helix has indicated the DPA needs to be executed prior to any customer data ingestion — we want to clarify whether Helix will require an executed DPA before signing the order form or if an executed DPA is acceptable to be completed in parallel but prior to data transfer.
- Sign order: Redwood prefers: (1) MSA (commercials & liabilities) signed, (2) Order Form countersigned, (3) DPA executed before data onboarding. If Helix procurement requires a different sequence we can adapt — just want the gating explicit in the MAP.

I attached a one-page MAP with milestones and a spreadsheet with suggested dates. Vanessa has sketched a ramp that meets your Q3 pilot window. Can you confirm procurement’s required sequencing and any internal signatory constraints (e.g., legal will not sign order forms until MSA executed)?

Thanks — I’ll follow up with Vanessa and legal to prepare the redline guidance once you confirm.

Best,
Logan Wright
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Helix_MAP_v1.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Celine Tran <celine.tran@helixtx.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>

Date: Sun, 31 May 2026 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Agreement sequencing & go-to-close plan — HelixTX MAP and order form dependencies

- Procurement preference: MSA signed first, then order form. That said, Procurement will accept a countersigned order form if legal has a signed MSA or an explicit signature plan (i.e., signed MSA executed within our internal review window).

- Signatories: Helix legal requires a fully signed MSA prior to enterprise order acceptance; commercial can provision pilot access earlier with a signed order form + DPA if legal and compliance give a flag.

Procurement asks for the following items to be in the MAP so we can track and commit:
1) Target signature dates for MSA, DPA, Order Form (we need realistic slack for our internal counsel; suggest 7 business days for legal review each).
2) The primary approver names and contact info (countersignature path).
3) Evidence artifacts: SOC 2 Type II report, data retention policy, and subprocessor list.

I’m looping Markus (procurement) and Samantha (privacy) to align on acceptable sequencing. Can you include the SOC2 pointer and a copy of your standard DPA in the MAP?

Thanks,
Celine
Head of Platform Partnerships
Helix Therapeutics

Attachments referenced: N/A

messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: events@grandplaza.com, concierge@deltatravel.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-28T16:12:00-07:00
Subject: Onsite host landing & access playbook for Redwood x Acme workshop

Hi team —

We’re finalizing the arrival flow for the Redwood x Acme two-day workshop (June 14–15). Primary asks for the venue and travel vendor: please confirm the following and flag any blockers by EOD Monday. I attached a rooming spreadsheet (redwood_acme_rooming_v1.xlsx) with tentative arrivals.

Top-line needs:
- Express check-in for group block (30 rooms) with keycards pre-programmed and a designated table in the lobby for badge pick-up.
- Early luggage drop for 8 late-night arrivals (post-11pm) and express check-in or keycard staging next morning.
- One ground-transport pick-up window from SFO 7:30–9:30am on June 14 (two shuttles). Delta Travel, confirm shuttle capacity and ETA margins.
- Passport collection requirement from the hotel if international vendors check in — we prefer a secure upload link instead of email.

Questions for Grand Plaza: are we able to pre-stage keycards for the late arrivals and hold a small cooler with welcome packets at the check-in desk? Any surcharge for after-hours luggage handling?

Questions for Delta: include transfer rates and confirmation of number of vehicles in the quoted block.

Thanks — I’ll pull together a final list of names tomorrow after the Acme travel team confirms a couple of last-minute changes.

—
Alex Martinez
Events Program Manager, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
+1 415 555 0173
From: Elena Ruiz <events@grandplaza.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-29T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onsite host landing & access playbook for Redwood x Acme workshop

Hi Alex —

Thanks for the summary and the spreadsheet — we received redwood_acme_rooming_v1.xlsx and have tentatively blocked the 30 rooms. Quick answers below: 

- Keycards: yes, we can pre-program up to 50 keycards and stage them behind our concierge desk. We will label by last name and room number. No extra fee for keycard staging.
- Late arrivals & luggage: we can hold luggage overnight at no charge. For arrivals after 11pm, we recommend assigning an on-call evening agent (one-time fee of $150) to expedite check-in; otherwise front desk can process first thing at 6:30am.
- After-hours access to meeting space: we can provide 24/7 access for organizer staff with a staff access badge (no charge) — please provide a final list of staff who need badges.
- Passport policy: we do require passport presentation at check-in for non-US IDs. We cannot accept passport scans via unsecured email; please use a secure upload or have guests present IDs at arrival. We can coordinate a secure upload link if you prefer.

I attached our standard hotel_checkin_instructions.pdf (contains ID/visa requirements, early check-in policy).

If you’d like, I can schedule a short site-call Thursday to walk through the check-in table plan and where to place the welcome packets.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Director of Events, Grand Plaza Hotel
events@grandplaza.com
+1 415 800 2244
Attachment: hotel_checkin_instructions.pdf
From: Marcus Li <concierge@deltatravel.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-29T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onsite host landing & access playbook for Redwood x Acme workshop

Hi Alex/Monica —

We can cover the SFO pick-ups and the smaller executive transfers. Quick notes: 
- Shuttle plan: two 18-seater shuttles reserved for June 14, pick-up window 07:30–09:30 (we’ll do rolling pickups within that window).
- Rates: $280 per shuttle each way; driver gratuity not included. See transport_rates.csv for the full breakdown.
- Vehicle staging & hotel hold: we can stage a vehicle by the arrivals curb for luggage unloading (15-minute hold per vehicle).
- Payment: we need a PO or corporate card on file to confirm reservations.

We can also provide one 6-passenger SUV for VIP transfer (Sara Jackson) if desired — needs confirmation by June 2.

Regards,
Marcus Li
Client Services, Delta Travel Concierge
concierge@deltatravel.com
Attachment: transport_rates.csv
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Li <concierge@deltatravel.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Elena Ruiz <events@grandplaza.com>
Date: 2026-05-29T13:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onsite host landing & access playbook for Redwood x Acme workshop

Thanks Marcus — we’ll use the Redwood corporate card for the shuttle deposit. Card ending 0421 — I’ll forward the authorization via our finance portal.

Re: passports and PII: Elena, please do send your secure upload link and we’ll route passport images from the Acme team through that. For vendors with US IDs we’ll have them present at check-in.

Alex — I confirmed with Acme travel: Sara Jackson wants the VIP SUV. I’ll lock that by EOD Monday and provide a PO number. Laura — can you confirm the list of organizer staff who need 24/7 access badges?

Monica Patel
Senior Program Ops, Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: events@grandplaza.com, concierge@deltatravel.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Sara Jackson <sara.jackson@acmecorp.com>
Date: 2026-05-30T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onsite host landing & access playbook for Redwood x Acme workshop

Perfect — thanks all. Final asks and confirmations now that we have vendor responses: 

1) Grand Plaza (Elena): please pre-program and stage the following by name (late arrivals in bold):
- David Chen — arrives 23:40 (late, luggage hold)
- Sara Jackson — arrives 08:05 (VIP SUV to hotel)
- Priya Natarajan — arrives 00:30 (late, express check-in requested)
- Michael Grant — arrives 22:10 (can self-check but please hold luggage)

Please tag badges for organizer staff: Alex Martinez, Monica Patel, Laura Bennett, Logan Wright — staff badges with 24/7 access.

2) Delta Travel (Marcus): please confirm the two 18-seat shuttles and one 6-passenger SUV for Sara on June 14. Charge the corporate card ending 0421 for the partial deposit; Monica will provide PO number by Monday.

3) Passport handling: Elena — send the secure upload link and we’ll forward the international IDs from our external guests. No passport images via email.

Attachment: final_late_arrivals_list.csv (names, flights, local arrival time). Please confirm once keycards are staged and shuttle deposit is processed.

If there are any schedule conflicts I’ll call — otherwise we’ll proceed with this plan.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Events Program Manager, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
+1 415 555 0173
Attachment: final_late_arrivals_list.csv

source.subject: RFP: provisional ratecard & consumption cushion — AtlasHealth
source.messages_1_subject: RFP: provisional ratecard & consumption cushion — AtlasHealth
source.messages_1_body:
Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the quick sync yesterday. Attached is the pricing appendix portion of our RFP for the pilot and enterprise phase (AtlasHealth_RFP_Pricing_Appendix.pdf). We need a clear, auditable response to the commercial sections so our procurement and finance teams can evaluate the options side-by-side. Key asks: 

- Pilot pricing (3 months, up to 50M tokens) and conversion mechanics if we move to committed capacity.
- Annual committed tiers (volume bands, effective per-token rates).
- Overage calculation and any smoothing/true-up mechanics.
- Credit or cushion allowances for burst months and how they reconcile on invoices.
- Any early-termination or migration credits we can expect.

Could you provide a provisional ratecard + a short example invoice for a typical month with a spike (e.g., committed 250M/month, actual 400M/month) so the team can see the math?

Thanks — we need to include your written response in the packet by EOD 5/26.

Sarah Lopez
Sr. Procurement Manager, AtlasHealth
procurement@atlashealth.com

Attachment: AtlasHealth_RFP_Pricing_Appendix.pdf (PDF, 72KB)
source.messages_2_subject: Re: RFP: provisional ratecard & consumption cushion — AtlasHealth
source.messages_2_body:
Hi Sarah — thanks for sending. We can definitely provide the level of detail you asked for. High-level notes up front:

- We’ll provide a provisional ratecard for the pilot and three committed bands (Small, Medium, Large) with tiered unit pricing and an explicit overage multiplier.
- We propose a consumption cushion mechanism (10% of monthly commit) that absorbs short bursts and is reconciled quarterly during true-up.
- Overage is invoiced at 120% of the committed unit rate (simple and predictable); we include a quarterly true-up with invoice adjustments for net over/under consumption.
- Pilot: 3 months, capped at 50M tokens with a 20% off list discount and immediate conversion credit applied to the first committed invoice if you convert within 60 days.

I’m looping Marissa (Commercials) and Logan (Finance) to draft the detailed appendix and the example invoice. We’ll aim to get a formal PDF response and a sample invoice by 5/26 EOD.

If you’re open to a quick 20-min call tomorrow to walk the team through the sample math, we can make the sample invoice interactive.

—Karthik
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

(Attachment: Draft-Term-Sheet-Redwood-ATLAS-20260519.docx — provisional language)
source.messages_3_subject: Fwd: AtlasHealth RFP — draft commercial terms (need sample invoice + language)
source.messages_3_body:
FYI — Karthik started a provisional reply. Need your help to 1) confirm the unit rates and overage math, and 2) produce a one-page sample invoice showing a committed customer that has a 250M/month commit but spikes to 400M in one month.

Proposed commercial model (draft for finance review):

- Commit bands (annualized):
  * Small: 100M tokens/month committed | Effective unit rate: $0.00065/token | Cushion: 10% of monthly commit
  * Medium: 250M tokens/month committed | Effective unit rate: $0.00052/token | Cushion: 10%
  * Large: 1B tokens/month committed | Effective unit rate: $0.00040/token | Cushion: 10%

- Pilot: 3 months up to 50M tokens total, 20% off the Small band list rate; conversion credit = total pilot spend applied to first committed invoice up to 25% of monthly committed fee.

- Overage: charged at 120% of the effective unit rate for the band that the account is committed to. Overage is calculated on actual tokens beyond the committed volume after applying cushion credits.

- True-up cadence: quarterly. Cushion is consumed monthly; any cushion used is netted at quarter-end and either invoiced or credited depending on net exposure. Example invoice requested — can you build the numbers and include a worked example table + narrative? Also note any tax or timing impacts we should call out.

Thanks — Marissa
VP, Commercials
source.messages_4_subject: Re: RFP: provisional ratecard & consumption cushion — AtlasHealth
source.messages_4_body:
Karthik / Marissa / Logan — thanks for the draft. The team reviewed the proposed mechanics and a few clarifying questions before we include your appendix in the procurement packet: 

1) For the spike example can you show both 1) billable overage using your 120% multiplier, and 2) an alternate smoothing example where 50% of the spike is amortized across the following two months (we’re modelling both).
2) For the pilot conversion credit — can you confirm whether that credit is refundable if Atlas does not convert, or is it forfeited? Procurement prefers credit to flow only on conversion.
3) Are there any minimum billing invoices (monthly minimums) we should expect on committed capacity?
4) Please include a short clause describing how data and logs used for billing are retained and accessible (SOC / audit-ready invoice trail).

If you can add those 4 items to the sample invoice + a single-page table of final unit rates and the exact language we should publish in the appendix, we’ll slot it into the packet. We still plan to submit on 5/26, so if you can get a final PDF by EOD today that addresses the above we’ll be able to include it.

Sarah
Sr. Procurement Manager, AtlasHealth

---
On Mon, May 19, 2026 at 9:12 AM Sarah Lopez <sarah.lopez@atlashealth.com> wrote:
> (original message and attachment referenced above)

Attachment: AtlasHealth_RFP_Pricing_Appendix.pdf (PDF)

title: RFP: provisional ratecard & consumption cushion — AtlasHealth

messages:

To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T09:02:13-07:00
Subject: BrightGrid — discount confirmation for Q3 forecast

Logan —

Finance reviewing May close and noticed the BrightGrid line in Q3 is recorded at $420k ARR but the deal team has submitted a 35% discount request in the attached worksheet. We need a quick confirmation of approved discount (and effective MR) so FP&A can mark the commit correctly in the Q3 forecast.

Key items: 
- ACV on file (pre-discount): $650,000
- Discount requested: 35% off list for first 12 months (Partner pricing + startup allowance)
- Net ARR reported currently: $420,000 (we think this used a 35% net but it's not reconciled in HubSpot)
- Close date: 2026-06-15 (sales is counting as commit)

Can you confirm whether Deal Desk has approved this or if this should be moved to Upside? Finance needs the status by EOD tomorrow so the forecast commit line can be adjusted. If Deal Desk needs a rationale memo, Laura and Karthik are on the thread.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Sr. Financial Analyst
Redwood Inference

Attachments: discount-request-worksheet.xlsx (spreadsheet stub, 1 sheet)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood-inference.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood-inference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood-inference.com>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T10:18:45-07:00
Subject: Fwd: BrightGrid — discount confirmation for Q3 forecast

Team — forwarding Elena's note. BrightGrid is pushing for a larger commercial concession (35% year 1) in exchange for a 36-month commitment + pilot expansion option in year 2. Sales has the commercial rationale (competitive displacement) but we need Deal Desk review to confirm financial guardrails and whether this stays as Commit in Q3.

My proposed steps: 
1) Karthik, can Deal Desk triage and provide conditional approval or escalation path today? Please include suggested guardrails (cap on TCV, success milestones).
2) Vanessa, can you flag the HubSpot record and set stage to "Commit (pending pricing approval)" if Deal Desk gives a conditional thumbs-up so FP&A doesn't mark it as definite until signed? Laura/Elena: if we get approval, please confirm the net ARR number to record.

I'll loop in the customer once we have a clear position.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Director, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference

Date: 2026-05-21T09:02:13-07:00
Subject: BrightGrid — discount confirmation for Q3 forecast
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood-inference.com>

Date: 2026-05-21T14:07:30-07:00
Subject: RE: BrightGrid — discount confirmation for Q3 forecast

Logan / team —

Thanks for the heads up. Deal Desk reviewed the attached worksheet and BrightGrid's buyer negotiation notes. Based on the displacement rationale and 36-month term, Deal Desk can APPROVE a limited concession with the following conditions: 

- Approved: 30% discount Year 1 only (instead of requested 35%), with standard list pricing restored in Year 2
- Commitment requirement: signed SOW + PO for at least 24 months of committed spend, with a minimum TCV floor of $480k net over first 12 months
- Success milestones: pilot acceptance sign-off (customer QA) within 90 days or discount clawback applies to subsequent invoices
- Finance note: treat this as "Commit - conditional" until signed SOW and PO received; mark forecast at the approved net ARR of $455,000 pending signatures

I've attached a short cover summary for FP&A and Sales Ops to reference. Please escalate to Elena for final forecast entry and to the customer for updated PO. If the customer insists on 35%, escalate to VP Sales for exception.

Karthik Iyer
Deal Desk Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: deal-cover-summary.pdf (one-page stub)

From: Alicia Ramos <alicia.ramos@brightgrid.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood-inference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood-inference.com>
Cc: Marco Chen <marco.chen@brightgrid.com>
Date: 2026-05-22T15:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: BrightGrid — discount confirmation for Q3 forecast

Thanks for taking this through Deal Desk so quickly. BrightGrid is comfortable with 30% Year 1 with the milestone clauses; we'll get the SOW signature counter-signed and route a PO that matches the 24-month minimum this week. Attaching the signed SOW for reference.

A quick ask for forecast accuracy: please confirm the net ARR number you'd like us to reference on the PO (is the $455,000 the correct value to appear on your records?). Once we have that, we'll ensure procurement mirrors it on our PO.

Look forward to closing this out — Marco and I will upload the PO to the DocuSign envelope you shared.

Best,
Alicia Ramos
Head of Procurement
BrightGrid

Attachments: signed-sow-brightgrid.pdf (PDF stub)
