messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference>
To: procurement@lumenix.ai
Cc: kimberly.park@redwood.inference, marcus.lin@redwood.inference
Date: Thu, 20 May 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Lumenix onboarding: DPA signature, SOC2 link, and PO routing

Hi Sarah / Lumenix procurement team,

Thanks for moving quickly on the account setup — excited to get Lumenix live on Redwood. A few procurement/security items we need to close so we can start routing traffic to the dedicated instance: 

- DPA: attached is our standard DPA (redwood-DPA-template-v3.pdf). Can you confirm whether you prefer to countersign via DocuSign or we should route to your legal team for signature?
- SOC 2: please share a link to your most recent SOC 2 report or a SOC 2 assertion (if public) and a current subprocessor list. We need these for our vendor file.
- PO / Invoicing: confirm whether you require a signed PO before go-live and the billing contact / remit-to email. If you issue a PO, please include the exact PO number and the account name on the invoice.

For context: our standard onboarding requires a signed DPA + SOC 2 assertion or equivalent in our vendor record. If you need a redline, loop in Kimberly (cc’d) who handles contract exceptions.

I've attached the DPA and a short onboarding checklist to speed this along. If it's helpful, we can run a 15-min call today to unblock signatures.

Next steps I propose: you confirm preferred signature method + send SOC2/subprocessor list, we send DocuSign envelope if that works, then route invoices to the billing contact you provide.

Thanks —

Logan Wright
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
log an.wright@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0124

Attachments: redwood-DPA-template-v3.pdf, onboarding-checklist.docx

To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Lumenix onboarding: DPA signature, SOC2 link, and PO routing

Hi Logan — thanks, and congrats on getting us set up. Quick answers below and attaching the files you asked for.

- DPA: we can sign by DocuSign. Please send the envelope; our legal reviews are quick for standard templates.
- SOC 2: attached is our SOC 2 Type II assertion (lumenix-soc2-assertion.pdf). It's the summary we publish for vendors; if you need the full report we can provide through our secure portal.
- Subprocessors: attached spreadsheet includes our current subprocessor roster (lumenix-subprocessors-2026.xlsx).
- PO: procurement requires a PO before vendor activation. We typically issue the PO after the DPA is countersigned. Billing contact for Lumenix: accounts-payable@lumenix.ai. Invoice remit-to: Lumenix AI, Attn: AP, 112 Harbor Point Dr, Boston, MA 02110.

We will need the invoice to include the purchase order number LUM-PO-9812 once issued; I will share the PO after DocuSign completes.

If you can send DocuSign today, I’ll route internally to get signatures by EOD tomorrow.

Best,

Sarah Patel
Head of Procurement
Lumenix AI
sarah.patel@lumenix.ai | +1 (617) 555-4432

source.subject: Quick follow-up: Priya Ramanathan — pairwise clarifications + finalize rank
messages:
From: Sarah Cho <sarah@talentbridge.io>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@productco.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Priya Ramanathan — interview loop (summary + scorecards)

Hi team,

Thanks again for taking Priya through the loop yesterday. Attached is the candidate scorecard PDF from the panel (priya_scorecard_v1.pdf) and a quick link to the shared score spreadsheet. A few housekeeping asks before I relay final feedback to ProductCo: 

- Please confirm your final numeric scores in the sheet by EOD PDT today.
- There was some split feedback on system-design depth vs operational tradeoffs — if you can add one short clarifying bullet under your system-design rating that would help us avoid a re-eval.
- We included a tiny pairwise poll (attached CSV) to help resolve close ties if multiple finalists are similar. If someone prefers, reply-all with your one-line pairwise pick (Priya vs other finalist).

Attachments: priya_scorecard_v1.pdf
Link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1a2b3c-scorecards (write access for internal)

Best,
Sarah
Senior Technical Recruiter | TalentBridge

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: "Sarah Cho" <sarah@talentbridge.io>

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Cho <sarah@talentbridge.io>, Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@productco.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T11:17:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Priya Ramanathan — interview loop (summary + scorecards)

Thanks Sarah — forwarding to the group. Short notes from me after re-reading the rubrics/scorecard: 

- I gave Priya a 4 for System Design, but my comment is that she focused on API/contract-level decisions and less on operational scaling patterns (KV cache invalidation, backpressure). It reads higher than her depth on large distributed consistency tradeoffs.
- On Product Sense she was clearly strong (5) — asked the right clarifying questions and prioritized customer metrics well.

Proposal: If we have a tie against another finalist, use pairwise comparison as Sarah suggested. I can populate the pairwise_poll.csv with my pick if others agree.

Deadline: please add brief clarifying bullets (1–2 sentences) next to your System Design rating by 4pm PDT so Sarah can close the loop with ProductCo.

—Logan
Staff SWE, Platform
Redwood Inference

On 2026-05-21 09:03, Sarah Cho wrote:
> Hi team,
> 
> Thanks again for taking Priya through the loop yesterday...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Cho <sarah@talentbridge.io>
Date: 2026-05-21T12:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Priya Ramanathan — interview loop (summary + scorecards)

Quick note — I updated the spreadsheet with my numeric ratings.

System Design: 3 — my comment: "Good API framing, needed more discussion on stateful failure modes and recovery (checkpointing / leader election)."
Product Sense: 4 — liked her prioritization tradeoffs.
Culture / Team Fit: 4

I'll add one sentence under System Design in the sheet to clarify. No strong objection to moving her forward, but leaning behind candidate B on deep infra experience.

—Marissa
PM, Infra Integrations
Redwood Inference

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Cho <sarah@talentbridge.io>, Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@productco.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T14:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Priya Ramanathan — interview loop (summary + scorecards)

I filled my scores as well and added notes. Two short thoughts: 

1) Tie-break recommendation — if Priya and Candidate B land with identical rubric scores, run this quick order: (a) Product-sense weight (40%), (b) System-design depth (35%), (c) Team fit (25%). That matches what ProductCo said they care about.
2) For the system-design ambiguity: I recall Priya gave an example about cache invalidation that was reasonable but not fully fleshed out. If you prefer, I'm happy to ping her for a 20-min follow-up on operational scenarios — but I'd rather avoid another loop if we can resolve internally.

Attached: pairwise_poll.csv (I added my pick preferring Candidate B slightly on infra depth).

—Aditya
Engineering Manager, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene_choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Cho <sarah@talentbridge.io>, Emma Johnson <emma.johnson@productco.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T17:46:00-07:00
Subject: Final: Priya Ramanathan — panel outcome and next steps

Thanks everyone — summary and decision below.

Summary of inputs:
- Marissa: System Design 3 (needs more on failure/recovery)
- Aditya: System Design 3 (cache invalidation example not fully fleshed) + pairwise pick for Candidate B
- Logan: System Design 4 (API focus), Product Sense 5
- Irene: (see sheet) — flagged strong culture fit

Pairwise CSV results: 3 votes Candidate B, 1 vote Priya (Aditya/Marissa/Emma vs me).

Decision: Given ProductCo's stated weighting, we deprioritize Priya for the final onsite — Candidate B moves forward. Action items: 
- Sarah: please close loop with Priya and offer constructive feedback; do not make another on-site ask yet.
- Aditya/Marissa: I'll paste a short consolidated feedback paragraph below for Sarah to use.
- Irene: please confirm whether you'd like a short 15-min call with Priya for coaching (optional).

Consolidated feedback draft (for Sarah):
"Priya demonstrated very strong product sense and customer focus. For system design, we would have liked more concrete discussion of operational tradeoffs for stateful services (e.g., cache invalidation under rolling upgrades and recovery patterns). We encourage focusing on depth in failure modes in future interviews"

Attachments: pairwise_poll.csv, priya_scorecard_v1.pdf

Thanks again — close the loop by EOD.

—Logan
Staff SWE, Platform
Redwood Inference

For system design, we would have liked more concrete discussion of operational tradeoffs for stateful services (e.g., cache invalidation under rolling upgrades and recovery patterns). We encourage focusing on depth in failure modes in future interviews\"

Attachments: pairwise_poll.csv, priya_scorecard_v1.pdf

Thanks again — close the loop by EOD.

—Logan
Staff SWE, Platform
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Clarify atomicity & amortization for committed discount
messages:
From: Danielle Park <dpark@edgeops.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Kim <sara@edgeops.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify packaging & rounding for committed discount

Hi Logan —

Thanks again for the pricing deck last week. We're getting final internal sign-off but have one bookkeeping question before procurement signs the SOW: how exactly are you measuring 'billing units' for the committed-discount tiers? Two specific items: 1) if a single request touches multiple model variants (rerank -> generate), how is that attributed across the context length bands, and 2) how does Redwood round/aggregate short calls — do you round per-request, per-minute, or on a daily aggregate?

Attached is a CSV of our representative week (EdgeOps_usage_q1.csv) — you can see a lot of short fanout calls that we'd expect to be sensitive to rounding. If rounding is per-request to the nearest 1,000 tokens, that could materially change the effective unit price for us.

We also plan to enable a 32k-context model for a subset of users; how does switching model size mid-session affect amortization of the context-based minimums?

Would appreciate a short note or sample calculation we can present to procurement. We may loop them in after that.

Thanks,
Danielle
Director of ML Platform
EdgeOps Inc

---
Attachments: EdgeOps_usage_q1.csv (csv, 42KB)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Danielle Park <dpark@edgeops.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 13:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify packaging & rounding for committed discount

Hi Danielle —

Appreciate the sample usage — that's very helpful. Short answer up front: billing is computed from a series of "snapshotted" token counts at request completion and then amortized to context bands before any rounding/minimum is applied. A couple of clarifications below, plus a simple example from your CSV.

1) Multi-stage requests (rerank -> generate)
- Each RPC is measured independently at completion. If you call a reranker that consumes 120 tokens and then a generator that outputs 980 tokens in a single logical flow, we bill two measurements (120 and 980). For discount-band purposes we then map each measurement to the appropriate context band (e.g., 0-1k, 1k-8k, 8k-32k) and apply the per-band rate. We do not coalesce different model types into a single combined bucket unless you request server-side composition (a single multipart request).

2) Rounding & aggregation cadence
- We do aggregation at invoice-time per UTC day for each model-family and client-id. Within that aggregation window we apply our rounding quantum and minimums. That means we will not round each atomic request to the nearest 1,000 independently; instead we sum the snapshotted token counts for the day, map to bands, then apply quantization (see example). This approach avoids disproportionately penalizing many tiny calls.

3) Model switching mid-session (context amortization)
- For sessions that switch to a larger context model mid-stream, we amortize the context overhead across the session's snapshotted measurements. Practically: the large-model KV cache and context reservation is accounted for by raising the band for measurements taken while the large model was active. We do not retroactively reclassify earlier small-model calls.

Example (from your sample week):
- Day total (generator family): 14,420 tokens across 18 completions -> day-level sum maps to band 8k-32k for the day.
- Quantum: 100-token quantum for generator family. After aggregation, we round the day-level totals up to the nearest 100 and apply committed discount tier to the aggregated units.

Next steps: Kimberly is copied with detailed invoice-line math; Marissa from the contracts team can propose addendum language for mixed-model usage. If that sounds good, we can do a 30-min call with procurement and finance tomorrow.

Best,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

---
References: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/1457
Attachments referenced: EdgeOps_usage_q1.csv

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Danielle Park <dpark@edgeops.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify packaging & rounding for committed discount (sample math)

Hi all —

Following up with concrete arithmetic so procurement can see line-item behavior. I used the sample CSV Danielle sent and created a mock invoice line. Assumptions noted inline.

Assumptions
- Quantum (generator family): 100 tokens
- Quantum (reranker family): 50 tokens
- Minimums: none at request level, daily banding applies
- Committed discount: 25% off base per-token list price once monthly committed threshold 2M tokens is reached

Mock day (generator family)
- Raw measured tokens (sum of completions): 14,420 tokens
- Day-level rounding: round up to nearest 100 -> 14,500 billed tokens
- Units billed (100-token units): 145 units
- Pre-discount cost: 145 * $0.002 (list per-100-token unit) = $0.29
- If this month's cumulative usage pushes them over the tier breakpoint, apply 25% discount to the units that fall into the committed bucket.

Multi-RPC example (rerank + gen flow)
- Reranker snapshots: 120 tokens -> rounds up to 150 tokens at family-level quantum (50) only after day aggregation
- Generator snapshots: 980 tokens -> included in day sum and affects the day's banding

Edge case: very sparse calls (e.g., 1–2 token heartbeats)
- These will be included in the day-level sum and thus are effectively smoothed by aggregation. We do not apply per-request minimums that would bill every heartbeat at a full quantum; that was a change we made last year to support micro-call patterns.

title: Clarify atomicity & amortization for committed discount
preview:
From: Danielle Park <dpark@edgeops.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Kim <sara@edgeops.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:23:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify packaging & rounding for committed discount

Hi Logan —

Thanks again for the pricing deck last week. We're getting final internal sign-off but have one bookkeeping question before procurement signs the SOW: how exactly are you measuring 'billing units' for the committed-discount tiers? Two specific items: 1) if a single request touches multiple model variants (rerank -> generate), how is that attributed across the context length bands, and 2) how does Redwood round/aggregate short calls — do you round per-request, per-minute, or on a daily aggregate?

Attached is a CSV of our representative week (EdgeOps_usage_q1.csv) — you can see a lot of short fanout calls that we'd expect to be sensitive to rounding. If rounding is per-request to the nearest 1,000 tokens, that could materially change the effective unit price for us.

We also plan to enable a 32k-context model for a subset of users; how does switching model size mid-session affect amortization of the context-based minimums?

Would appreciate a short note or sample calculation we can present to procurement. We may loop them in after that.

Thanks,
Danielle
Director of ML Platform
EdgeOps Inc

---
Attachments: EdgeOps_usage_q1.csv (csv, 42KB)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Danielle Park <dpark@edgeops.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 13:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify packaging & rounding for committed discount

Hi Danielle —

Appreciate the sample usage — that's very helpful. Short answer up front: billing is computed from a series of "snapshotted" token counts at request completion and then amortized to context bands before any rounding/minimum is applied. A couple of clarifications below, plus a simple example from your CSV.

1) Multi-stage requests (rerank -> generate)
- Each RPC is measured independently at completion. If you call a reranker that consumes 120 tokens and then a generator that outputs 980 tokens in a single logical flow, we bill two measurements (120 and 980). For discount-band purposes we then map each measurement to the appropriate context band (e.g., 0-1k, 1k-8k, 8k-32k) and apply the per-band rate. We do not coalesce different model types into a single combined bucket unless you request server-side composition (a single multipart request).

2) Rounding & aggregation cadence
- We do aggregation at invoice-time per UTC day for each model-family and client-id. Within that aggregation window we apply our rounding quantum and minimums. That means we will not round each atomic request to the nearest 1,000 independently; instead we sum the snapshotted token counts for the day, map to bands, then apply quantization (see example). This approach avoids disproportionately penalizing many tiny calls.

3) Model switching mid-session (context amortization)
- For sessions that switch to a larger context model mid-stream, we amortize the context overhead across the session's snapshotted measurements. Practically: the large-model KV cache and context reservation is accounted for by raising the band for measurements taken while the large model was active. We do not retroactively reclassify earlier small-model calls.

Example (from your sample week):
- Day total (generator family): 14,420 tokens across 18 completions -> day-level sum maps to band 8k-32k for the day.
- Quantum: 100-token quantum for generator family. After aggregation, we round the day-level totals up to the nearest 100 and apply committed discount tier to the aggregated units.

Next steps: Kimberly is copied with detailed invoice-line math; Marissa from the contracts team can propose addendum language for mixed-model usage. If that sounds good, we can do a 30-min call with procurement and finance tomorrow.

Best,
Logan Wright
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

---
References: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/1457
Attachments referenced: EdgeOps_usage_q1.csv

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Danielle Park <dpark@edgeops.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify packaging & rounding for committed discount (sample math)

Hi all —

Following up with concrete arithmetic so procurement can see line-item behavior. I used the sample CSV Danielle sent and created a mock invoice line. Assumptions noted inline.

Assumptions
- Quantum (generator family): 100 tokens
- Quantum (reranker family): 50 tokens
- Minimums: none at request level, daily banding applies
- Committed discount: 25% off base per-token list price once monthly committed threshold 2M tokens is reached

Mock day (generator family)
- Raw measured tokens (sum of completions): 14,420 tokens
- Day-level rounding: round up to nearest 100 -> 14,500 billed tokens
- Units billed (100-token units): 145 units
- Pre-discount cost: 145 * $0.002 (list per-100-token unit) = $0.29
- If this month's cumulative usage pushes them over the tier breakpoint, apply 25% discount to the units that fall into the committed bucket.

Multi-RPC example (rerank + gen flow)
- Reranker snapshots: 120 tokens -> rounds up to 150 tokens at family-level quantum (50) only after day aggregation
- Generator snapshots: 980 tokens -> included in day sum and affects the day's banding

Edge case: very sparse calls (e.g., 1–2 token heartbeats)
- These will be included in the day-level sum and thus are effectively smoothed by aggregation. We do not apply per-request minimums that would bill every heartbeat at a full quantum; that was a change we made last year to support micro-call patterns.

I've attached a CSV that mirrors an invoice line-by-line so you can see how each request maps to the billed units.

Kimberly Park
Billing Operations, Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap@forgecore.com>
Date: 2026-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Hi Logan,

Thanks for the quick intro on the RFP. As we're lining up the legal/ops items, procurement at ForgeCore wanted to confirm two practical items before we get the PO process live:

1) Standard payment term — our accounting currently runs Net 45 with your peers; ForgeCore is asking if Redwood can offer Net 30 for the first 90 days as a new-supplier pilot to help with cashflow timing.
2) PO intake / invoice lane — we need to know the exact PO header fields required by Redwood (PO number placement, remit email, invoice routing codes) so we can format our e-invoices.

I've attached our vendor intake form and signed W-9 for your records (stubs attached). Can you confirm the required fields for the AP portal and whether a Net 30 pilot is feasible? If Net 30 isn't possible, any accelerated payment options (e.g., early-pay discount) would be helpful to discuss.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Director, Finance Operations
ForgeCore

Attachments: forgecore_vendor_intake_form.pdf (filled), forgecore_w9_signed.pdf
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-17T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Elena,

Thanks — appreciate the attachments and the clear ask. Looping in Monica (vendor onboarding) and David (AP / payments). Quick answers up front:

- We have a standard of Net 45 for new suppliers, but we can evaluate a Net 30 pilot on a case-by-case basis.
- PO header requirements: PO Number, Invoice Date, Supplier VAT/Tax ID, and Redwood remit code (we use RWD-AP-XX). Monica will send the exact intake checklist and the portal mapping.

Monica / David — can you review ForgeCore's intake form and confirm what we need from them to route invoices correctly? Monica, if this looks like a Net 30 pilot candidate, what's the approval path?

I'll hold on a formal agreement until we hear back from you both.

Thanks,
Logan
Head of Procurement Ops
Redwood Inference
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-18T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Hi all,

Thanks for sending the intake form, Elena. I reviewed the stub and here's what we need to complete vendor activation and set PO routing without delays: 

Required items (please attach / confirm):
- Completed vendor intake (we flagged two missing fields: Redwood remit code and final AP contact)
- Signed W-9 (received)
- Bank details or voided check for ACH setup (we can accept secure link to bank file)
- Preferred invoice delivery method: AP portal e-invoice, email to ap@redwoodinference.com, or EDI outbound (if available)

PO / invoice header mapping (for your invoicing system):
- PO Number -> field: PO# (exact string)
- Line-level cost center -> field: CostCenter (if left blank, default to onboarding bucket)
- Invoice email -> ap@redwoodinference.com (for PDF attachments)

About the Net 30 request: we'll need Finance sign-off for a pilot. Please outline expected average monthly invoiced amount and any early-pay discount offers (if you're proposing a discount for earlier payment). If you can provide an example invoice in the requested PO format that will speed testing.

Attached: Vendor intake checklist (portal_field_map.docx) — stub.

Monica Patel
Vendor Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: portal_field_map.docx (stub)
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-19T14:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Hi Monica, Logan —

Thanks for the checklist. Quick answers:

- Monthly volume: typically $85k–$140k depending on deployment months; initial quarter forecast $320k.
- We can submit invoices via your AP portal (preferred) and will include PO#, CostCenter, and a short invoice note with deployment ID.
- Early-pay offer: ForgeCore can accept Net 45 standard but is willing to offer a 0.5% discount for invoices paid Net 30 and 1.5% for Net 10 if Redwood can commit to an electronic-only invoice flow with 24/7 portal submission. We're flexible on the exact discount mechanics if Redwood has a preferred early-pay vendor program.

I've re-attached the completed vendor intake along with a sample invoice in the PO format you requested. Please confirm receipt and next steps (testing window, PO creation cadence, and expected date for the first PO).

Best regards,
Elena

Attachments: forgecore_vendor_intake_form.pdf (completed), sample_invoice_POformat.pdf
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-20T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Elena / Monica / Logan,

Thanks for the clear options. Finance reviewed the forecast and proposed a path forward:

1) Default onboarding term remains Net 45.
2) We will approve a limited Net 30 pilot for the first 90 days for ForgeCore, conditional on:
   - Invoices submitted through the AP portal in the exact PO format (sample invoice accepted)
   - ACH details verified and bank account validated via secure link before first payment
   - A cap of $200k outstanding at any time during the pilot
3) Early-pay discount: rather than tiered Net10/30 pricing, we can offer a 0.5% settlement discount for any invoices paid within 30 days if ForgeCore opts into our FastPay electronic settlement program.

Next steps: Monica to create the vendor record and open Jira PROC-4821 for the PO mapping work. Elena, please upload the bank documentation to the secure intake link and confirm acceptance of the pilot cap. We'll create the first PO within five business days after vendor activation.

If that aligns, I'll route the pilot approval to Legal & Treasury for a brief sign-off.

-David
David Kim
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-18T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Hi all,

Thanks for sending the intake form, Elena. I reviewed the stub and here's what we need to complete vendor activation and set PO routing without delays: 

Required items (please attach / confirm):
- Completed vendor intake (we flagged two missing fields: Redwood remit code and final AP contact)
- Signed W-9 (received)
- Bank details or voided check for ACH setup (we can accept secure link to bank file)
- Preferred invoice delivery method: AP portal e-invoice, email to ap@redwoodinference.com, or EDI outbound (if available)

PO / invoice header mapping (for your invoicing system):
- PO Number -> field: PO# (exact string)
- Line-level cost center -> field: CostCenter (if left blank, default to onboarding bucket)
- Invoice email -> ap@redwoodinference.com (for PDF attachments)

About the Net 30 request: we'll need Finance sign-off for a pilot. Please outline expected average monthly invoiced amount and any early-pay discount offers (if you're proposing a discount for earlier payment). If you can provide an example invoice in the requested PO format that will speed testing.

Attached: Vendor intake checklist (portal_field_map.docx) — stub.

Monica Patel
Vendor Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments: portal_field_map.docx (stub)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-19T14:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Hi Monica, Logan —

Thanks for the checklist. Quick answers:

- Monthly volume: typically $85k–$140k depending on deployment months; initial quarter forecast $320k.
- We can submit invoices via your AP portal (preferred) and will include PO#, CostCenter, and a short invoice note with deployment ID.
- Early-pay offer: ForgeCore can accept Net 45 standard but is willing to offer a 0.5% discount for invoices paid Net 30 and 1.5% for Net 10 if Redwood can commit to an electronic-only invoice flow with 24/7 portal submission. We're flexible on the exact discount mechanics if Redwood has a preferred early-pay vendor program.

I've re-attached the completed vendor intake along with a sample invoice in the PO format you requested. Please confirm receipt and next steps (testing window, PO creation cadence, and expected date for the first PO).

Best regards,
Elena

Attachments: forgecore_vendor_intake_form.pdf (completed), sample_invoice_POformat.pdf

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@forgecore.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-20T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Payment cadence alignment & PO intake for ForgeCore

Elena / Monica / Logan,

Thanks for the clear options. Finance reviewed the forecast and proposed a path forward:

1) Default onboarding term remains Net 45.
2) We will approve a limited Net 30 pilot for the first 90 days for ForgeCore, conditional on:
   - Invoices submitted through the AP portal in the exact PO format (sample invoice accepted)
   - ACH details verified and bank account validated via secure link before first payment
   - A cap of $200k outstanding at any time during the pilot
3) Early-pay discount: rather than tiered Net10/30 pricing, we can offer a 0.5% settlement discount for any invoices paid within 30 days if ForgeCore opts into our FastPay electronic settlement program.

Next steps: Monica to create the vendor record and open Jira PROC-4821 for the PO mapping work. Elena, please upload the bank documentation to the secure intake link and confirm acceptance of the pilot cap. We'll create the first PO within five business days after vendor activation.

If that aligns, I'll route the pilot approval to Legal & Treasury for a brief sign-off.

-David
David Kim
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference

Logan

source.subject: 48-hour pilot top-up request — BrightCart
messages:
From: Samir Rao <sam@brightcart.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: 48-hour pilot top-up request

Hi Hannah,

Quick ask — we're hitting a hard stop on our BrightCart pilot tonight at midnight PT. The team is about to exercise a couple more high-traffic flows and we expect another ~40k tokens of usage, but our trial quota will cut the test off.

Can Redwood grant a one-time 48-hour top-up or token credit so we can complete the last two scenarios? It would let us finish a spiky run that accurately shows latency behavior under load. If there are any sign-offs needed from your side we can take a short form — we just need the extension today.

Appreciate a fast reply.

— Samir
Samir Rao
Head of ML Engineering, BrightCart AI
sam@brightcart.ai

Attachments: none
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <sam@brightcart.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: 48-hour pilot top-up request

Thanks for flagging this, Samir — understood.

Ben / Logan — can you approve a 48-hour one-off top-up (up to 50k tokens) for BrightCart? They need it today to finish performance verification; this is a strategic small pilot and we want to avoid cutting the demo. If approved, I'll send the confirmation and a very short T&Cs note to BrightCart.

Ben: suggested limit = 50,000 tokens; campaign code = BRIGHTCART-48H. If you prefer a dollar cap instead I can convert.

Samir — looping in operations/credits; I will confirm within the hour.

Thanks,
Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Enterprise Sales (West) | Redwood Inference
hannah.schmitt@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

Attachments: trial-extension-approval-form.pdf (form for approvals)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: 48-hour pilot top-up request

Approved.

Authorize 48 hours, up to 50,000 tokens, apply promo code BRIGHTCART-48H. No extra discounts beyond the token credit; ops will apply the credit and tag the deal. I trust Hannah to finalize wording with the customer.

Ben Carter
Senior Director, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachments: 48hr-topup-invoice-stub.pdf (internal stub)

To: Samir Rao <sam@brightcart.ai>, Nina Patel <nina@brightcart.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 10:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: 48-hour pilot top-up request

Hi Samir / Nina — good news: I've approved a one-time 48-hour token top-up for your pilot. Details below and in the attached form.

What we granted
- 48 hours from the time we flip the toggle (we will start it when you confirm ready)
- Up to 50,000 tokens of credit (non-recurring)
- Promo code / internal tag: BRIGHTCART-48H

Next steps for you
1) Reply here to confirm you want us to start the extension now.
2) If you want the credit to start at a specific timestamp, include that time (we operate PT).
3) We will attach a short confirmation and the standard T&Cs. No billing changes.

Operational notes
- This is a one-off pilot extension; if you need recurring capacity we'll discuss a dedicated pilot seat.
- We've copied Ben so ops will apply the credit and tag the HubSpot deal BD-4839.

Attached: trial-extension-approval-form.pdf (signed by Ben internally) — please keep on file.

If you're ready, say "Start now" and I will flip the credit.

Thanks,
Hannah

Attachments: trial-extension-approval-form.pdf
From: Samir Rao <sam@brightcart.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 17:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: 48-hour pilot top-up request

Start now — confirmed. Thank you, Hannah and Ben. This will let us complete the final throughput scenarios.

Samir

Head of ML Engineering, BrightCart AI

source.subject: Starting our MAP & 6-week eval cadence with Brightlane
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@brightlanehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Starting our MAP & 6-week eval cadence with Brightlane

Hi Alicia —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation I wanted to kick off a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) so we have clarity on objectives, owners, and the timeline for a 6-week evaluation. The goal of the MAP is to validate Redwood inference in Brightlane’s anonymized clinical QA workflow and measure latency and cost at scale.

High-level proposal (please review and modify):
- Objectives: validate throughput at 50 req/s, confirm quality vs. current heuristic baseline, and measure per-token cost under a 90th-percentile latency SLO.
- Timeline: 6 weeks (Pilot config + 2-week smoke, 3-week throughput/quality run, 1-week wrap + exec review)
- Roles: Alicia (Champion/Clinical Lead), Samir (Infra/Cloud), Logan (AE), Marissa (CS/enablement), Evelyn (Solutions/technical onboarding)
- Success criteria: 95% of test prompts meet quality threshold, average latency <= 350ms, and cost per 1k tokens within target range we discussed.

Attached is a starter MAP template and a short eval-cadence PDF. Can you confirm the stakeholders and whether the timeline looks feasible from Brightlane’s side? If yes, I’ll send a short mutual-action checklist and propose a 30-min kickoff slot.

Next steps I propose from Redwood:
1) We share a completed MAP template within 48 hours.
2) Evelyn will schedule a technical onboarding session with Samir to validate network/VPC and data handling.
3) Marissa will share an onboarding checklist and eval measurement dashboard access.

Suggested kickoff windows (PST): Tue 5/19 10–11am, Wed 5/20 2–3pm — tell me what works and I’ll calendar it.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this running.

—
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Brightlane_MAP_starter_template.xlsx (xlsx, 56KB); Brightlane_eval-cadence.pdf (pdf, 120KB)

From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@brightlanehealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Patel <spatel@brightlanehealth.com>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 16:06:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Starting our MAP & 6-week eval cadence with Brightlane

Logan —

Thanks, this looks good and aligned with what we discussed. Confirming stakeholders on our side:
- Alicia Gomez (Clinical QA lead) — Champion
- Samir Patel (Cloud Infra) — Technical owner for VPC and security

preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@brightlanehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Starting our MAP & 6-week eval cadence with Brightlane

Hi Alicia —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation I wanted to kick off a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) so we have clarity on objectives, owners, and the timeline for a 6-week evaluation. The goal of the MAP is to validate Redwood inference in Brightlane’s anonymized clinical QA workflow and measure latency and cost at scale.

High-level proposal (please review and modify):
- Objectives: validate throughput at 50 req/s, confirm quality vs. current heuristic baseline, and measure per-token cost under a 90th-percentile latency SLO.
- Timeline: 6 weeks (Pilot config + 2-week smoke, 3-week throughput/quality run, 1-week wrap + exec review)
- Roles: Alicia (Champion/Clinical Lead), Samir (Infra/Cloud), Logan (AE), Marissa (CS/enablement), Evelyn (Solutions/technical onboarding)
- Success criteria: 95% of test prompts meet quality threshold, average latency <= 350ms, and cost per 1k tokens within target range we discussed.

Attached is a starter MAP template and a short eval-cadence PDF. Can you confirm the stakeholders and whether the timeline looks feasible from Brightlane’s side? If yes, I’ll send a short mutual-action checklist and propose a 30-min kickoff slot.

Next steps I propose from Redwood:
1) We share a completed MAP template within 48 hours.
2) Evelyn will schedule a technical onboarding session with Samir to validate network/VPC and data handling.
3) Marissa will share an onboarding checklist and eval measurement dashboard access.

Suggested kickoff windows (PST): Tue 5/19 10–11am, Wed 5/20 2–3pm — tell me what works and I’ll calendar it.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this running.

—
Logan Wright
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

source.subject: PrivInfra CI patterns and L2/L3 on-prem network vetting
messages:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Patel <infra@acme.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: PrivInfra CI patterns and L2/L3 on-prem network vetting

Hi Sanjay / ACME infra team,

Thanks for taking the time to kick off the private deployment scoping with us. Attached are two short artifacts to ground the conversation: ci-patterns.pdf (proposed CI gating patterns for Terraform/Helm/Ansible) and l2l3-network-drawings.v2.png (current network sketch for the on-prem colo at your site).

Top asks ahead of our sync: 
- Can you confirm the expected external egress model (NAT at edge vs. routed public AS)?
- MTU – are we working with jumbo frames (9000) across the fabric or limited to 1500?
- Do you require dedicated VLANs for inference clusters or can we reuse existing tenant VLANs?
- Any existing firewall appliance fingerprint (vendor/model/version) or managed firewall service we should plan for?

From our side we want to align on the IaC contract: 
- Terraform: we expect the network module to expose: vpc_cidr, private_subnets[], public_subnets[], nat_gateway_count, allowed_asns, and an outputs block with transit_gateway_id / vlan_ids.
- Helm: values.yml should include calico.tunnel.enabled (bool), mtu, and a top-level annotation for network-policy (enforced|permissive).
- Ansible: runbooks should include a preflight gate that validates switch port config and a secrets flow (how to provide vault/KMS secrets during run).

If you can confirm the three bullet network constraints above and the MTU, I will loop in Karthik (Terraform owner) and Hannah (CI/GitLab owner) to vet the module contract. Vanessa is on the thread for procurement/security traces.

Proposed short window for a follow-up call: Mon 5/18 10:00–10:45 PT. If that works for you we will post an invite.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Senior TPM, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

Attachments: ci-patterns.pdf (summary), l2l3-network-drawings.v2.png (diagram)

From: Sanjay Patel <infra@acme.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: PaloSec Consulting <palo_sec@consulting.com>
Date: Fri, 15 May 2026 13:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: PrivInfra CI patterns and L2/L3 on-prem network vetting

Hi Logan,

Appreciate the artifacts — quick answers inline: 
- Egress: routable public via our edge router (we will advertise a private ASN to your transit). We can also NAT at the edge for egress if you prefer.
- MTU: our fabric supports up to 9216 but a couple of downstream aggregation links are currently at 1500. We can request a change but it will require a change window.
- VLANs: we can carve dedicated VLANs for the deployment (preferred).

About the firewall: we use Palo Alto PA-5250 cluster (PAN-OS 10.1). Our compliance team will want to see runbooks and evidence for preflight checks (SOC2 auditors). PaloSec is cc'd — they're supporting us on controls.

Two asks from our side: 
1) Do you have an example preflight checklist (what your Ansible preflight validates)?
2) Can your terraform module avoid creating account-level globals (we prefer project-scoped resources)?

We are broadly available Mon 5/18 10:00 PT for the call.

—Sanjay
Sanjay Patel
Infrastructure Lead, ACME DataOps

Attachment references: None.

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 16 May 2026 10:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: PrivInfra CI patterns and L2/L3 on-prem network vetting

Karthik / Hannah — forwarding ACME's replies (below). Please vet and flag where the Terraform/Helm/Ansible contract needs to change given their constraints. TL;DR requirements from ACME: dedicated VLANs, fabric MTU mixed (some links 1500, fabric supports 9216), Palo Alto PA-5250 firewall with managed zones, and they prefer project-scoped resources.

Specific asks: 
- Karthik: review the proposed network module inputs/outputs I listed (vpc_cidr, private_subnets[], allowed_asns, outputs transit_gateway_id/vlan_ids). If ACME wants project-scoped (no account/global globals), what should the module pattern be? Also, can we add an optional mtu_override variable so we can gate calico/helm config based on downstream limits?
- Hannah: CI gating — ci-patterns.pdf proposes: 
  * terraform fmt/validate/plan with tfsec/tflint
  * module unit-tests (kitchen-terraform or terratest) for network module APIs
  * helm lint + schema validation for values.yaml (we suggested using kubeval/helmfile-lint)
  * ansible-playbook --check for preflight with an extra verification job that verifies switch port config (SNMP/Netconf dry-run).

ACME also explicitly asked for a sample preflight checklist and evidence for SOC2. I have a short checklist in the attached ci-patterns.pdf but we should expand it if necessary (Hannah, can you add the GitLab job names / artifacts we will produce for auditor evidence?).

Quoted for context: 
> From: Sanjay Patel <infra@acme.ai>
> ... (see full reply above)

Thanks — would like to get a redlines pass by EOD Monday so we can present a single-contract draft in the 5/18 call.

—Logan
Logan Wright
Senior TPM, Private Deployments
Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 16:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: PrivInfra CI patterns and L2/L3 on-prem network vetting

Logan — quick redlines and action items after a first pass: 

1) Terraform module contract changes (network):
   - Rename module to privinfra-network (avoid account-global names). Module will accept an optional owner_scope = "project"|"account" (default project).
   - Add variable mtu_override = number (nullable). If provided, module emits a tf output network_mtu and a flag helm_mtu_override = true in outputs so downstream helm templates can pick it up.
   - Keep outputs minimal: vlan_ids, transit_gateway_id (nullable), network_mtu.

2) Helm/Values validation:
   - Add a pipeline job helm-values-validate which runs kubeval + helmfile-lint against values schema. Add a policy that blocks merge if helm-values-validate fails.
   - Enforce calico.mtu usage from network_mtu output via a small Kustomize/templating check as part of helm-ci.

3) Ansible/Preflight & secrets: 
   - Use Ansible-vault backed by customer KMS (ACME will provide a key). Document the secrets flow in the runbook (how to inject during run and how to rotate).
   - Preflight: ansible-playbook preflight.yml --check should validate: switch port config, VLAN tagging, trunk config, MTU echo test, and firewall zone mappings. We will add a small python verifier that records results to an artifacts.json for auditor evidence.

4) CI gating and approvals: 
   - GitLab: stop-the-line manual approval for any merge that targets production environment (protect branch). Add a required pipeline job that uploads artifacts (preflight artifacts.json, terraform-plan.json, helm-values-report.json) to an internal drive link and references it in MR description.

5) Next steps (owners & timeline):
   - Karthik: prepare a patch to privinfra-network module w/ mtu_override and outputs (MR target: privinfra/terraform-modules#220) — ETA Tue 5/19 EOD.
   - Hannah: add helm-values-validate job to the shared CI templates and a sample GitLab MR template that enumerates required evidence — ETA Tue 5/19 EOD.
   - Logan/Vanessa: compile the runbook and evidence checklist for ACME SOC2 review (I will attach the annotated MOP).

I attached acme-mop-annotated.docx (brief notes) and will push the module MR once the initial tests are green. If ACME prefers a NAT egress pattern, we can parameterize that in the module but will default to routed AS.

Happy to review on Mon during the call.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Principal Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: acme-mop-annotated.docx

Logan — quick redlines and action items after a first pass: 

1) Terraform module contract changes (network):
   - Rename module to privinfra-network (avoid account-global names). Module will accept an optional owner_scope = "project"|"account" (default project).
   - Add variable mtu_override = number (nullable). If provided, module emits a tf output network_mtu and a flag helm_mtu_override = true in outputs so downstream helm templates can pick it up.
   - Keep outputs minimal: vlan_ids, transit_gateway_id (nullable), network_mtu.

2) Helm/Values validation:
   - Add a pipeline job helm-values-validate which runs kubeval + helmfile-lint against values schema. Add a policy that blocks merge if helm-values-validate fails.
   - Enforce calico.mtu usage from network_mtu output via a small Kustomize/templating check as part of helm-ci.

3) Ansible/Preflight & secrets: 
   - Use Ansible-vault backed by customer KMS (ACME will provide a key). Document the secrets flow in the runbook (how to inject during run and how to rotate).
   - Preflight: ansible-playbook preflight.yml --check should validate: switch port config, VLAN tagging, trunk config, MTU echo test, and firewall zone mappings. We will add a small python verifier that records results to an artifacts.json for auditor evidence.

4) CI gating and approvals: 
   - GitLab: stop-the-line manual approval for any merge that targets production environment (protect branch). Add a required pipeline job that uploads artifacts (preflight artifacts.json, terraform-plan.json, helm-values-report.json) to an internal drive link and references it in MR description.

Happy to review on Mon during the call.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Principal Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: acme-mop-annotated.docx

messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@papertrail.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Supplier banking proof & PO routing playbook — PaperTrail Logistics

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for choosing Redwood for the upcoming integration. To get PaperTrail set up for first-payments and proper PO reconciliation, we need the minimal banking and remittance evidence below. I attached a short onboarding checklist and our PO routing template to make this easier.

Requested items (please upload or reply with attachments):
- Bank verification letter or bank-stamped account confirmation (PDF)
- Proof of account (voided check or bank statement redacted)
- W-9 (US entities) or local tax form for AP records
- Preferred remit-to address and invoice contact (name + email)
- Sample invoice (PDF or CSV) showing line-item and invoice number formatting
- Confirmation of ACH (preferred) or wire capability; if ACH, please confirm receiving bank ACH routing number and account type

Operational notes: we use a PO prefix to tag invoices for automatic routing. Please let us know if you want a custom prefix; otherwise we will assign PTL- as the supplier prefix. Once we confirm the micro-deposit and map the invoice fields to our PO template, Finance will enable your account for immediate PO-based payment runs.

Attachments: onboarding-checklist.pdf, po-routing-template.csv

Next steps: if you can reply with the bank letter and sample invoice today we can kick off micro-deposits this afternoon.

Thanks,

Jordan Blake
Senior Procurement Operations
Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0178
From: Elena Soto <elena.soto@papertrail.co>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Accounting Team <accounts@papertrail.co>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier banking proof & PO routing playbook — PaperTrail Logistics

Jordan —

Appreciate the checklist and clear instructions. I attached the bank letter and a redacted account proof. PaperTrail prefers ACH; our bank confirmed ACH routing is enabled. We will also attach a sample invoice (PDF) in a follow-up if the attached sample doesn’t align with your template.

Micro-deposit plan: we can accept a test micro-deposit today. Please send a note with the reference string(es) you will use (so our accounting team can reconcile).

Attachments: bank_letter.pdf, proof_of_account.pdf

Thanks,

Elena Soto
Head of Finance Operations
PaperTrail Logistics
elena.soto@papertrail.co
+1 (212) 444-9620

> Jordan Blake
> Senior Procurement Operations

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@papertrail.co>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 May 2026 14:26:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Supplier banking proof & PO routing playbook — PaperTrail Logistics

Hi Elena —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the bank docs. We’ll proceed with micro-deposits this afternoon from Redwood’s ACH clearing account.

Logan — can you please watch for the two small micro-deposit entries and confirm the amounts with PaperTrail? Once verified we’ll finalize the PTL- PO prefix and flip PaperTrail to active for PO-based payments. Also, Elena, would you send a sample invoice (PDF) with one paid-in-full line and the invoice number in the top-right? That helps our AP parser when mapping fields in po-routing-template.csv.

I attached a sanitized sample invoice for reference.

Attachments: sample-invoice.pdf

Best,

Marissa Cole
Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference

Subject: RE: Supplier banking proof & PO routing playbook — PaperTrail Logistics

Elena / team —

Confirmed: two micro-deposit entries hit PaperTrail’s account at 11:12 AM and 11:13 AM PDT. Amounts were $0.18 and $0.42. Elena, can you confirm those amounts on your side? That will finalize ACH verification and we’ll set the PO prefix to PTL- (unless you request a different prefix).

Once you confirm the two cents, I’ll update the supplier record and run a quick test invoice through the routing template to validate invoice header mapping. Expected next steps after your confirmation:
- Finance toggles PaperTrail to Active (AP vendor profile)
- Procurement sends initial PO (PO-PTL-000123) to PaperTrail for first payable
- PaperTrail invoices using the template and includes the PO number in the invoice number field or invoice reference line

Related links: onboarding folder on Drive and PaperTrail company record in HubSpot are attached for internal tracking.

Thanks,

Logan Wright

Subject: Supplier banking proof & PO routing playbook — PaperTrail Logistics

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for choosing Redwood for the upcoming integration. To get PaperTrail set up for first-payments and proper PO reconciliation, we need the minimal banking and remittance evidence below. I attached a short onboarding checklist and our PO routing template to make this easier.

Requested items (please upload or reply with attachments):
- Bank verification letter or bank-stamped account confirmation (PDF)
- Proof of account (voided check or bank statement redacted)
- W-9 (US entities) or local tax form for AP records
- Preferred remit-to address and invoice contact (name + email)
- Sample invoice (PDF or CSV) showing line-item and invoice number formatting
- Confirmation of ACH (preferred) or wire capability; if ACH, please confirm receiving bank ACH routing number and account type

Operational notes: we use a PO prefix to tag invoices for automatic routing. Please let us know if you want a custom prefix; otherwise we will assign PTL- as the supplier prefix. Once we confirm the micro-deposit and map the invoice fields to our PO template, Finance will enable your account for immediate PO-based payment runs.

Attachments: onboarding-checklist.pdf, po-routing-template.csv

Next steps: if you can reply with the bank letter and sample invoice today we can kick off micro-deposits this afternoon.

Thanks,

Jordan Blake
Senior Procurement Operations
Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0178

source.subject: BlueBay Payments — DPA template, subprocessor list & egress controls
messages:
From: Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@bluebaypayments.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com>
Cc: BlueBay Payments Legal <legal@bluebaypayments.com>
Date: Sat, 09 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: DPA + subprocessor list for onboarding

Hi Logan,

We're starting the procurement for our payments integration and legal asked me to collect the DPA and an inventory of subprocessors and data flows for our privacy assessment. Can you send over Redwood's standard DPA template and a current subprocessor list? A few specific asks from BlueBay Legal:

- Confirm if Redwood relies on SCCs for EU transfers or offers an EU data residency option
- Any available egress/safelist IPs and ports for network allowlisting
- KMS/HSM support and whether customer-managed keys are available
- Preferred e-sign platform (DocuSign/AdobeSign) and how long signature turnaround typically takes

I've attached our onboarding checklist (onboarding-checklist.pdf) for context. We need to get the DPA signed before the pilot in mid-June.

Thanks,
Sarah Jenkins
Senior Program Manager, Integrations
BlueBay Payments

Attachments: onboarding-checklist.pdf
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com>
To: Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@bluebaypayments.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodlabs.com>
Date: Sat, 09 May 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: DPA + subprocessor list for onboarding

Hi Sarah — thanks for the note and the checklist. Looping in Naomi (Privacy) and Marcus (Solutions) to help with the technical details.

Please find attached: Redwood_DPA_Template_v1.docx and a high-level dataflow matrix (dataflow-matrix.pdf). Quick answers up-front:

- Transfers: We support SCCs and also offer an EU-hosted private deployment for customers that require data residency.
- Egress: We can provide a stable set of egress IPs and a ports list; will include as a CSV for your networking team.
- Keys: Customer-managed keys via KMS can be supported on Dedicated/Private plans; hosted API customers can request envelope-level encryption — happy to discuss scope.
- E-sign: We primarily use DocuSign, but AdobeSign is supported. DocuSign tends to be faster for typical DPA workflows.

Two quick clarifying Qs that will affect the DPA scope:
1) Will BlueBay require us to list our subprocessor partners in the executed annex, or is a maintained published list acceptable?
2) Are there specific export controls or egress-only policies (e.g., blocking logs from leaving EU regions) that we should reflect in the annex?

Proposed next steps:
- Naomi to propose limited redlines on subprocessors and egress controls
- Marcus to confirm egress IPs and provide subprocessor inventory extract
- I'll share a proposed DocuSign envelope if that works for you

Regards,
Logan Wright
Head of Customer GTM, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: Redwood_DPA_Template_v1.docx, data_retention_summary.pdf
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com>
To: Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@bluebaypayments.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodlabs.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 10:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: DPA + subprocessor list for onboarding

Hi all — quick privacy review notes (I kept the original thread below). Thanks Logan for the docs.

High-level redline items we'd propose to surface in the annex for BlueBay:
- Subprocessor notifications: we can commit to a 30-day notice period for new subprocessors plus a maintained published list and on-request CSV extract.
- Egress/safelisting: we can provide stable egress IPs and support an allowlist; for highly restrictive networks we propose an IP-based safelisting addendum (we won't commit to bespoke ports changes without product impact review).
- Logs & exports: we can offer a configuration to disable non-essential debug logs for EU-hosted deployments and restrict admin-level log exports to approved network CIDRs.
- SCCs vs residency: default approach is SCCs; for residency we recommend Redwood Private (we can include a section on acceptance criteria and costs).

Marcus — can you confirm the expected turnaround to produce: (a) egress IPs CSV, (b) subprocessor-list.csv, and (c) a short note on KMS options we can insert into the DPA?

Thanks,
Naomi Feldman
Privacy Lead, Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com

On Sat, May 9, 2026 at 11:03 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com> wrote:
> Hi Sarah — thanks for the note and the checklist. Looping in Naomi (Privacy) and Marcus (Solutions) to help with the technical details.
> 
> Please find attached: Redwood_DPA_Template_v1.docx and a high-level dataflow matrix (dataflow-matrix.pdf). Quick answers up-front:
> 
> - Transfers: We support SCCs and also offer an EU-hosted private deployment for customers that require data residency.
> - Egress: We can provide a stable set of egress IPs and a ports list; will include as a CSV for your networking team.
> - Keys: Customer-managed keys via KMS can be supported on Dedicated/Private plans; hosted API customers can request envelope-level encryption — happy to discuss scope.
> - E-sign: We primarily use DocuSign, but AdobeSign is supported. DocuSign tends to be faster for typical DPA workflows.

> Regards,
> Logan
From: BlueBay Payments Legal <legal@bluebaypayments.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com>, Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@bluebaypayments.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: DPA + subprocessor list for onboarding

Logan / Naomi — thanks for the quick turnaround. Attached is our redlined DPA (BlueBay_DPA_v1_redline.docx) with three priority asks captured: explicit egress IP commit, 30-day subprocessor notification (we accept a maintained list + CSV), and a clause limiting log exports from EU-resident workloads.

We prefer DocuSign for execution. I created a draft envelope for Redwood: https://docusign.com/12345/BlueBay-DPA. Please confirm the signer name and email for DocuSign (we anticipate an admin-level signature from your legal/ops). Our target: sign by 2026-05-20 to meet the pilot timeline.

On items you flagged: we accept SCCs but would like the EU-hosted option to be captured as an addendum for future activation.

Next steps for Redwood:
- Provide final redline response or counter by EOD 2026-05-14
- Confirm DocuSign signer and we will route for signature
- Provide subprocessor-list.csv and egress IPs for our network team

Best,
BlueBay Payments Legal
legal@bluebaypayments.com

Attachments: BlueBay_DPA_v1_redline.docx

logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: Redwood_DPA_Template_v1.docx, data_retention_summary.pdf


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com>
To: Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@bluebaypayments.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodlabs.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 10:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: DPA + subprocessor list for onboarding

Hi all — quick privacy review notes (I kept the original thread below). Thanks Logan for the docs.

High-level redline items we'd propose to surface in the annex for BlueBay:
- Subprocessor notifications: we can commit to a 30-day notice period for new subprocessors plus a maintained published list and on-request CSV extract.
- Egress/safelisting: we can provide stable egress IPs and support an allowlist; for highly restrictive networks we propose an IP-based safelisting addendum (we won't commit to bespoke ports changes without product impact review).
- Logs & exports: we can offer a configuration to disable non-essential debug logs for EU-hosted deployments and restrict admin-level log exports to approved network CIDRs.
- SCCs vs residency: default approach is SCCs; for residency we recommend Redwood Private (we can include a section on acceptance criteria and costs).

Marcus — can you confirm the expected turnaround to produce: (a) egress IPs CSV, (b) subprocessor-list.csv, and (c) a short note on KMS options we can insert into the DPA?

Thanks,
Naomi Feldman
Privacy Lead, Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com

On Sat, May 9, 2026 at 11:03 AM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com> wrote:
> Hi Sarah — thanks for the note and the checklist. Looping in Naomi (Privacy) and Marcus (Solutions) to help with the technical details.
> 
> Please find attached: Redwood_DPA_Template_v1.docx and a high-level dataflow matrix (dataflow-matrix.pdf). Quick answers up-front:
> 
> - Transfers: We support SCCs and also offer an EU-hosted private deployment for customers that require data residency.
> - Egress: We can provide a stable set of egress IPs and a ports list; will include as a CSV for your networking team.
> - Keys: Customer-managed keys via KMS can be supported on Dedicated/Private plans; hosted API customers can request envelope-level encryption — happy to discuss scope.
> - E-sign: We primarily use DocuSign, but AdobeSign is supported. DocuSign tends to be faster for typical DPA workflows.

> Regards,
> Logan


From: BlueBay Payments Legal <legal@bluebaypayments.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodlabs.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodlabs.com>, Sarah Jenkins <sarah.jenkins@bluebaypayments.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: DPA + subprocessor list for onboarding

Logan / Naomi — thanks for the quick turnaround. Attached is our redlined DPA (BlueBay_DPA_v1_redline.docx) with three priority asks captured: explicit egress IP commit, 30-day subprocessor notification (we accept a maintained list + CSV), and a clause limiting log exports from EU-resident workloads.

We prefer DocuSign for execution. I created a draft envelope for Redwood: https://docusign.com/12345/BlueBay-DPA. Please confirm the signer name and email for DocuSign (we anticipate an admin-level signature from your legal/ops). Our target: sign by 2026-05-20 to meet the pilot timeline.

On items you flagged: we accept SCCs but would like the EU-hosted option to be captured as an addendum for future activation.

Next steps for Redwood:
- Provide final redline response or counter by EOD 2026-05-14
- Confirm DocuSign signer and we will route for signature
- Provide subprocessor-list.csv and egress IPs for our network team

Best,
BlueBay Payments Legal
legal@bluebaypayments.com

Attachments: BlueBay_DPA_v1_redline.docx

source.subject: NorthStar co-sponsor roadtrip slate & MDF terms
messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <partnership@northstarcloud.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 9 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: NorthStar + Redwood: regional roadtrip idea & initial slate

Hi Logan / Marissa —

Thanks again for the quick intro last week. NorthStar is planning a customer-facing roadtrip for Q3 (San Diego -> Phoenix -> Austin) and we would love to explore co-sponsoring a shared booth + 45-minute technical meetup in each city. High-level asks from our side: 1) co-branded slide deck and speaking slot for a Redwood engineer, 2) a modest MDF contribution to cover venue and local promos, 3) joint promotion (email + social) two weeks prior.

Attached is a draft event slate (NorthStar_Slate_v3.pdf) with audience estimates and preliminary dates (Aug 10–17 window). Can Redwood commit to the speaking slot and a $12k MDF split at 50/50? If so, we can lock venues and share the event ops contact.

Warmly,
Elena Ruiz
Partnerships Lead, NorthStar Cloud
partnership@northstarcloud.com

--
Attachment: NorthStar_Slate_v3.pdf (PDF, 320KB)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <partnership@northstarcloud.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 9 May 2026 11:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: NorthStar + Redwood: regional roadtrip idea & initial slate

Elena —

Appreciate the slate. We like the market mix and the short-form meetup format. Quick answers: yes on the engineer speaking slot (can do a 25–30 minute tech & 10 minute joint Q&A), and we can support MDF — need to confirm the exact split and invoicing. Marissa, Ben: can you confirm budget routing (50/50 NorthStar/Redwood) and whether 12k total is approved from our field marketing pool?

Two operational asks from our side:
- venue estimates (can EventOps provide a line-item cost for venue + A/V)
- attendee promo code tracking (we prefer a dedicated register link per city)

If we get a thumbs up on MDF and venue estimates, I'll draft a mutual action plan and propose a short SOW for each stop. Proposed next step: 30-minute ops sync Mon or Tues. What times work?

Best,
Logan Wright
Field Marketing Manager, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com

> On Sat, May 9, 2026 at 9:12 AM Elena Ruiz <partnership@northstarcloud.com> wrote:
> > Attached is a draft event slate (NorthStar_Slate_v3.pdf) with audience estimates and preliminary dates (Aug 10–17 window).
> >
> > --
> > Attachment: NorthStar_Slate_v3.pdf

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <partnership@northstarcloud.com>
Date: Sun, 10 May 2026 08:50:00 -0700
Subject: Re: NorthStar + Redwood: regional roadtrip idea & initial slate

Adding ops context: I ran the slate by EventOps (copied here) and asked for venue_estimate.xlsx — see attached stub we requested. Early estimates look like: venue + basic A/V ~$4k per city, staffing & local promo ~$1k per city, so the $12k MDF seems reasonable. A couple of notes:
- We should include a clause that MDF is refundable if fewer than X paying seats sold (or convert to credit against future MDF).
- Travel reimbursement for a Redwood engineer: recommend a capped per-stop travel allowance (economy airfare + up to $150/day per diem) to keep costs predictable.

Ops offered two sample dates per market in Aug; we should finalize after the sync but leave flexibility for the weeks of Aug 9–23.

Marissa
Sr. Field Marketing, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Attachment: venue_estimate.xlsx (XLSX, 24KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <partnership@northstarcloud.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 11 May 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Draft mutual action plan & next steps (attached)

Team —

I put together a short mutual action plan and a simple SOW per city (NS-Roadtrip-MAP-v1.docx). Key points:
- MDF: $12,000 total, invoiced 50% upfront from each side (Net 30), remainder 10 days before each event after venue confirmation.
- Deliverables: one Redwood engineer speaker per city (30 min), 1 co-branded slide deck, shared promo assets, unique RSVP links for tracking.
- Cancellation/credit: if event cancelled >21 days out, full refund of remaining MDF; if cancelled <21 days, convert to credit for future co-marketing within 12 months.
- Logistics: EventOps to provide line-item venue quotes; Redwood to provide a speaker pack (bios + slides) 10 days before each event.

Please review the attached MAP and SOW. Suggesting a quick ops call tomorrow at 10am PT to lock dates and confirm invoicing contacts.

Ben Carter
Head of Field Programs, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: NS-Roadtrip-MAP-v1.docx (DOCX, 42KB)

> On Sun, May 10, 2026 at 8:50 AM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Early estimates look like: venue + basic A/V ~$4k per city, staffing & local promo ~$1k per city, so the $12k MDF seems reasonable.
> >
> > Attachment: venue_estimate.xlsx

From: Elena Ruiz <partnership@northstarcloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: EventOps Ops <ops@eventops.com>
Date: Mon, 11 May 2026 16:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft mutual action plan & next steps (attached)

Thanks Ben — this looks great and aligns with what we discussed internally. Two quick confirmations:
1) NorthStar will sign the MAP and send a 50% deposit via PO to Redwood (billing contact is finance@northstarcloud.com).
2) EventOps — please share the line-item venue estimate by EOD Wed so we can pick final dates.

We also attached a slightly edited slate with confirmed audience segments for each city (we reduced Austin capacity by 15%). If everyone is good, we can move forward with the 10am PT ops sync tomorrow and I will have our finance lead on the call.

Elena Ruiz
Partnerships Lead, NorthStar Cloud
partnership@northstarcloud.com

Attachment: NorthStar_Slate_v3.pdf (updated)
